385: Chop Session - The Return of Big

items.title: Global hotspots monitoring roundup
items.pov_summary: The hosts are staying vigilant on a busy slate of international situations — Venezuela kicked off the monitoring new year, they've been tracking Iran for months, and Greenland is percolating again. They feel a bit worn out but committed to staying on top of it all.
items.quote: We're going to stay vigilant, though. We've been tracking Iran for months now. Yeah. Uh looks like Greenland is percolating again.
items.updates_summary: Major developments across all three hotspots since the Jan 8 episode. Venezuela: U.S. forces captured Maduro on Jan 3 in "Operation Absolute Resolve," with VP Delcy Rodríguez now running an interim government under U.S. pressure and Washington taking control of Venezuelan oil sales. Greenland: Trump escalated annexation pressure through January (tariff threats, refusing to rule out military force), then backed down at Davos after talks with NATO's Rutte, though he revived attacks in April amid NATO rifts over the new Iran war. Iran: A U.S.-Israeli war with Iran broke out in roughly late February/early March and, as of early April, was under a fragile two-week ceasefire.

updates.headline: U.S. Captures Maduro in 'Operation Absolute Resolve'
updates.summary: U.S. military struck Venezuela and took President Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores into custody on January 3, 2026, with strikes reportedly killing dozens of Venezuelan military officers, at least two civilians, and 32 Cuban officers. Maduro was transported to New York and arraigned on narco-terrorism charges.

updates.headline: U.S. to Control Venezuelan Oil Sales 'Indefinitely'
updates.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the U.S. would market Venezuela's sanctioned oil and control future sales, with proceeds held in U.S. Treasury accounts to be shared with Caracas. The Trump administration also announced a deal to 'turn over' 30-50 million barrels of sanctioned Venezuelan crude.

updates.headline: Delcy Rodríguez Sworn In as Interim Venezuelan President
updates.summary: Vice President Delcy Rodríguez was sworn in as Venezuela's interim president as authorities declared a national state of emergency. The International Crisis Group notes hardliners like Diosdado Cabello retain key ministries, complicating prospects for a democratic transition.

updates.headline: Trump Backs Down on Greenland at Davos After Rutte Talks
updates.summary: After threatening a 25% tariff on Denmark and several European nations and refusing to rule out military force, Trump reversed course at the World Economic Forum following discussions with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, pledging not to use tariffs or force to annex Greenland. Diplomatic talks between the U.S., Denmark, and Greenland launched in late January.

updates.headline: Trump Revives Greenland Attacks Amid NATO Rift Over Iran War
updates.summary: Trump called Greenland a 'big, poorly run, piece of ice' on Truth Social after NATO allies declined to support the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. The Pentagon is reportedly in talks with Denmark for access to three additional bases in Greenland — the first U.S. military expansion there in decades.

updates.headline: Greenland PM Says Citizens 'Don't Feel Safe' Under Trump Pressure
updates.summary: Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen told NBC that many Greenlanders are scared and angered by Trump's ongoing rhetoric, and that he does not believe Trump has taken ownership or control of Greenland off the table despite having walked back the invasion threat.

updates.headline: U.S.-Israel Iran War Enters Fragile Two-Week Ceasefire
updates.summary: After more than a month of fighting, Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, though Iran's parliamentary speaker accused Washington of violating terms within 24 hours. Several NATO members had denied U.S. aircraft the use of their airspace and declined naval contributions, fueling Trump's NATO grievances.

items.title: Gas tank arrow and flying V formations
items.pov_summary: DJ nominated the little arrow on the gas gauge that shows which side your tank is on — big's a fan, though he suspects plenty of drivers have no idea it exists. Also sick: geese/duck flying V formations, and Airbus and Delta researching flying-V formations across the Atlantic to save fuel.
items.quote: Flying Vs are sick. And I know Airbus and Delta are trying to research like a flying formation across the Atlantic to save fuel.
items.updates_summary: Not much new specifically on fello'fly/GEESE formation-flying trials since the January 8, 2026 episode — the main milestone (completion of GEESE rendezvous flight trials over the North Atlantic with Delta and Virgin Atlantic) was announced in December 2025, just before the episode aired. Airbus's next round of flight tests is planned for later in 2026, with commercial deployment expected in the first half of the 2030s. A tangentially related NASA fuel-saving aerodynamics test (CATNLF) hit a milestone in January 2026.

updates.headline: NASA tests laminar-flow wing tech that could cut commercial aircraft fuel burn
updates.summary: NASA completed a high-speed taxi test of its Cross Flow Attenuated Natural Laminar Flow (CATNLF) scale model mounted to an F-15B at Armstrong Flight Research Center, reaching about 144 mph. The design aims to reduce drag on swept wings and could save fuel on future commercial aircraft — another biomimicry-adjacent efficiency play in the same spirit as fello'fly.

items.title: Rollerblading goal execution update
items.pov_summary: Randy actually followed through — ordered XL rollerblades (only option for size 14+ feet), got them Jan 2, and knocked out two laps at Wash Park on day one with helmet and wrist guards. Felt it come back quickly, likened it to speed skating, and plans to go again before the cold snap. Counting it a smashing success.
items.status: goal in active execution; equipment acquired, first outing complete
items.quote: I think it was a smashing success. Honestly, I might go out today or tomorrow afternoon.
items.updates_summary: This is a personal goal/life update from Randy on the podcast, not a topic that generates external news coverage. No meaningful developments to report between the episode airing and today.

items.title: TC's tennis comeback and Neil match
items.pov_summary: TC is buying a new tennis racket tonight and plans to play Neil in the Dominican Republic next week on a film shoot. He hasn't played in a decade and expects to get smoked, but he's trying to get back on the wagon. Year of gut, maybe synthetic gut strings.
items.status: match scheduled next week in DR; racket TBD
items.quote: I mean, he'll smoke me, but uh I'm trying to get back on the wagon.
items.updates_summary: No meaningful news developments found on this topic. TC's tennis comeback and his planned match with Neil in the Dominican Republic is a personal podcast storyline that wouldn't generate outside press coverage — any follow-up would appear in a subsequent TrapDraw episode rather than in news outlets.

items.title: Buffalo Jake liver transplant recovery
items.pov_summary: Jake TGO (Buffalo Jake) is up and alert out of liver transplant surgery and already listening to the goals podcast. The hosts are thrilled — Matt Golden is producing a documentary on Jake that they'll publicize later this year.
items.quote: He's up and alert uh out of his liver transplant surgery. He's listening to the goals podcast right now.
items.updates_summary: No substantive public news developments on Buffalo Jake's (Jake TGO) liver transplant recovery or the Matt Golden-produced documentary were found between the episode air date (Jan 8, 2026) and April 18, 2026. Updates on this community story appear to be circulating within the Trap Draw/golf community circles rather than in indexed news sources.

items.title: Newark Terminal A over LaGuardia
items.pov_summary: Big is ready to declare Newark Terminal A better than LaGuardia's new terminal. LaGuardia has been glazed nonstop and may now be overrated — security is awful, rental car is a pain. Inside Terminal A, he says it's best in class right now.
items.status: hot take filed; big calling LaGuardia overhyped
items.quote: I feel like Lagoria is starting to get so overhyped. It might be might be underrated now.
items.updates_summary: Significant developments on both sides of the Newark-vs-LaGuardia comparison. Newark began major AirTrain construction that causes weekday service outages (potentially undermining Terminal A's convenience advantage), while LaGuardia saw both a high-profile upgrade (a new José Andrés/Capital One Landing dining destination at Terminal B) and a major setback — the first fatal accident at LGA in more than 30 years when an Air Canada Express plane collided with a fire truck on March 22, 2026. The Port Authority also proposed a 10-year $45B capital plan that would expand Newark's Terminal A with new gates and rebuild LaGuardia's Terminal A.

updates.headline: AirTrain Newark replacement enters major construction phase, causing weekday outages
updates.summary: Starting Jan. 15, 2026, <cite index="10-4,10-5">the Port Authority suspended AirTrain service between the Airport Train Station and P4 on weekdays from 5 a.m. to 3 p.m., replacing it with ADA-compliant shuttle buses running every four to five minutes</cite>. <cite index="10-7">Travelers should allow up to 15 additional minutes for trips during weekday outages</cite>, with additional outages planned in 2027 and 2028 — a meaningful hit to EWR's ground-access convenience that Big praised.

updates.headline: LaGuardia Terminal B opens Capital One Landing with José Andrés
updates.summary: <cite index="19-4,19-5">LaGuardia Gateway Partners opened the Capital One Landing at Terminal B, a 12,500-square-foot dining destination on one of the skybridges, created in collaboration with Chef José Andrés</cite>. <cite index="19-7,19-8,19-9">It's open to all travelers with a same-day boarding pass up to three hours before departure; Capital One Venture X cardholders get complimentary access, Venture and Spark Miles cardholders pay $45, and all others pay $90</cite> — a new amenity the hosts would probably want to weigh in the comparison.

updates.headline: Deadly Air Canada collision with fire truck shuts LaGuardia
updates.summary: <cite index="17-6">An Air Canada Express plane collided with a fire truck shortly after landing at LaGuardia — the first deadly accident there in more than 30 years</cite>. <cite index="17-2">The collision killed both of the plane's pilots</cite>, and the NTSB launched a major investigation; the airport reopened the following day.

updates.headline: Port Authority's 10-year capital plan calls for Terminal A expansion at EWR and Terminal A rebuild at LGA
updates.summary: <cite index="8-2,8-3,8-4">The Port Authority proposed a 10-year, $45 billion capital plan (2026-2035) that includes replacing LaGuardia's 85-year-old Terminal A and building a new Terminal B at Newark, plus expanding Newark's Terminal A with new gates and funding the new AirTrain Newark</cite>. Note: this was unveiled before the episode but public comments ran through December — the broader debate over which airport is 'best in class' is now formally tied to the next decade of investment.

items.title: Randy wins Balco fantasy title for Vic Conte
items.pov_summary: After ~20 years in a brutal two-QB auction keeper league, Randy finally won it all — team name Balco, and he thinks Vic Conte (who passed in November) was pulling strings from above. Also won the rare Oil Bowl in week 18. Kamish returned to league management. Meanwhile THG finished last.
items.quote: I can't help but think maybe Vic Ky was pulling some strings from up above. Pour pouring some clear and the cream out.
items.updates_summary: No meaningful public news developments on this topic since the episode aired. This is a personal fantasy football league story about host Randy's long-awaited championship in his private keeper league, which wouldn't generate follow-up news coverage.

items.title: Cabbage caper lemon recipe becomes identity
items.pov_summary: TC keeps making a caper-lemon-cabbage roasted wedge recipe that DJ says has 'become a massive part of his entire identity.' Super simple — wedge the cabbage, olive oil, capers, lemon juice and zest, garlic, roast at 400-425 for 25-30 minutes. Capers are vastly underutilized.
items.quote: DJ said it's like it's become a massive part of his entire identity. That's what he said the other day.
items.updates_summary: This was a personal recipe bit rather than a news story, so there's no direct follow-up. However, one cultural tailwind is worth noting: <cite index="3-9">Vogue declared 2026 the "Year of the Cabbage,"</cite> meaning TC's identity-defining cabbage wedge is suddenly very on-trend.

updates.headline: Vogue declares 2026 the 'Year of the Cabbage'
updates.summary: Vogue has named 2026 the Year of the Cabbage, sparking a wave of roasted cabbage recipe features — vindicating TC's caper-lemon-cabbage obsession as culturally on-trend.

items.title: AFC playoff picture wide open
items.pov_summary: The hosts see the AFC as wide open with no team they really trust. Patriots are the scariest in all three phases but Broncos are beatable. TC actually booked flights to Denver for a potential AFC Championship game. Jags could absolutely win it and there's a universe where they go to the Super Bowl.
items.status: Wild Card weekend upcoming; hosts rooting Jags
items.quote: There's a universe the Jags are going to the Super Bowl. Like, I'm not counting anything out.
items.updates_summary: The hosts' read that the AFC was wide open proved prescient. The Patriots rode a dominant defense through the playoffs to win the AFC, beating the Chargers, Texans, and top-seeded Broncos (10-7 in snowy Denver with Bo Nix out) before losing Super Bowl LX 29-13 to the Seahawks. TC's Denver flights didn't pay off — Denver did host the AFC Championship but lost — and the Jaguars did not factor into the deep playoff run.

updates.headline: Patriots beat Broncos 10-7 in snowy AFC Championship Game
updates.summary: New England won a defensive slugfest in Denver to reach Super Bowl LX, with Jarrett Stidham starting in place of injured Bo Nix. <cite index="9-24">Sean Payton's decision to go for it on 4th down instead of kicking a field goal was criticized as a key turning point.</cite>

updates.headline: Bo Nix breaks ankle in Broncos' Wild Card win over Bills
updates.summary: <cite index="4-19,4-20">Nix broke a bone in his ankle and missed the rest of the postseason, with Jarrett Stidham named the starter for the AFC Championship.</cite>

updates.headline: Patriots force five turnovers to dispatch Texans in Divisional Round
updates.summary: <cite index="4-23">New England's five turnovers were the most for the team in a playoff game since the 2003 AFC Championship Game</cite>, setting up the trip to Denver.

updates.headline: Seahawks beat Patriots 29-13 in Super Bowl LX
updates.summary: Seattle won its second Lombardi Trophy, with <cite index="7-20,7-21">Maye's fumble leading to the Seahawks' only offensive TD and Devon Witherspoon forcing a strip-sack that Uchenna Nwosu returned 45 yards for the clinching score.</cite>

items.title: Peter Carroll Raiders firing cheapens legacy
items.pov_summary: Hosts hate that this is probably it for Peter Carroll — it cheapens his whole legacy. He deserved better than how it ended in Vegas, and Tom Telesco's statement throwing him under the bus was brutal. They think the Raiders roster is better than what they've gotten out of it, and Kyler Murray has to own some of that in Arizona.
items.status: Carroll out; Raiders coaching opening now active
items.quote: I hate that this is probably going to be it for Peter Carroll... it just cheapens his whole legacy a bit for me. I hate that he went out this way.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the Raiders hired Klint Kubiak (Seahawks OC fresh off a Super Bowl LX win) as head coach on Feb. 9, 2026, traded Geno Smith to the Jets, and signed Kirk Cousins. In March, Carroll broke his silence and pointed the finger back at the Raiders' front office — particularly for failing to fix the offensive line — partially validating the hosts' view that he was scapegoated.

updates.headline: Raiders hire Klint Kubiak as head coach after Seahawks' Super Bowl LX win
updates.summary: Las Vegas named Seahawks OC Klint Kubiak its new head coach one day after he helped Seattle win Super Bowl LX over the Patriots. He becomes the Raiders' third head coach in three seasons, with Mike McCoy as assistant HC and Rob Leonard promoted to DC.

updates.headline: Pete Carroll breaks silence, rips Raiders' front office over O-line
updates.summary: In his first extensive comments since being fired, Carroll took aim at the Raiders' front office for failing to fix the offensive line, shouldered blame for Geno Smith's struggles, and supported Smith's trade to the Jets. The comments partly vindicate the hosts' take that Telesco/Spytek unfairly pinned the season on Carroll.

updates.headline: Raiders trade Geno Smith to Jets, sign Kirk Cousins
updates.summary: Las Vegas moved on from Carroll's hand-picked QB Geno Smith in a late-round pick swap with the Jets and signed Kirk Cousins as the veteran bridge ahead of an expected No. 1 overall pick of Fernando Mendoza.

items.title: Bengals running it back despite defense
items.pov_summary: Bengals are keeping Zack Taylor and Duke Tobin, which frustrates Big — if anyone should've gone it was Tobin for his free agency and draft record. But Burrow endorsed the coaching staff. Mission this offseason is crystal clear: fix the defense, probably draft a defensive player at 10, spend in free agency. If they miss playoffs again next year, heads will roll.
items.status: coaching staff retained; offseason all-in on defense
items.quote: I think this might be like I think we may have hit rock bottom with the Bengals. I would be shocked if we're here next year talking about the Bengals missing the playoffs.
items.updates_summary: The Bengals followed through on the offseason mandate to fix the defense, making a top-ten free agency splash by signing EDGE Boye Mafe, DT Jonathan Allen, and S Bryan Cook (~$126M combined), plus later adding S Kyle Dugger and CB Ja'Sir Taylor. Heading into the April 23-25 draft, most mocks project the Bengals to take a defender at No. 10 — LSU CB Mansoor Delane is the consensus favorite, with Ohio State LB Sonny Styles, S Caleb Downs, and EDGE Rueben Bain Jr. as other possibilities.

updates.headline: Bengals land three defensive starters in first wave of free agency
updates.summary: Cincinnati addressed its defense by signing EDGE Boye Mafe (3 years), S Bryan Cook (3 years, $40.25M), and DT Jonathan Allen (2 years) in the opening days of free agency to replace losses including Trey Hendrickson and Joseph Ossai.

updates.headline: Bengals add S Kyle Dugger and CB Ja'Sir Taylor
updates.summary: Cincinnati continued to patch the secondary in April, signing former Patriots safety Kyle Dugger and former Chargers CB Ja'Sir Taylor to one-year deals.

updates.headline: Mock drafts converge on LSU CB Mansoor Delane at No. 10
updates.summary: Heading into the April 23-25 draft, Mel Kiper Jr., Dane Brugler, and most major analysts have the Bengals taking Delane at No. 10, with DJ Turner II and Dax Hill entering contract years. Other names in play: Sonny Styles, Caleb Downs, and Rueben Bain Jr.

updates.headline: Joe Flacco re-signs as Burrow's backup
updates.summary: Veteran QB Joe Flacco returned on a one-year deal for his 19th NFL season to back up Joe Burrow.

updates.headline: Orlando Brown Jr. signs two-year extension
updates.summary: The Bengals extended LT Orlando Brown Jr. through 2028 on a two-year, $32M deal, avoiding a potential cap cut.

items.title: Indiana Hoosiers and Curt Cignetti rising
items.pov_summary: TC's an Indiana alum and never been more proud — they straight-up kicked Alabama's ass in the trenches, nothing fluky about it. Love Cignetti's efficiency philosophy (90-minute practices, direct and candid). Curious there's no NFL buzz for him, though he might be too old. Also shocked Charlie Weis Jr. followed Kiffin to LSU instead of calling plays at Ole Miss.
items.status: Cignetti riding high; NFL interest unclear
items.quote: Alabama, you're like, you're not as good as in like there was nothing fluky about that. Indiana is by far the better football team.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, Indiana completed a 16-0 perfect season by winning the program's first national championship over Miami on Jan 19, 2026. Cignetti parlayed that into a $105.6M/8-year contract extension (averaging $13.2M/year), making him the second-highest-paid coach in college football behind only the Georgia/LSU tier. Indiana also reloaded via the portal (17 transfers, highlighted by QB Josh Hoover from TCU and WR Nick Marsh) after key losses, and opened spring practice on March 26 with the spring game set for April 23.

updates.headline: Cignetti gets massive pay raise after national title
updates.summary: After Indiana's CFP semifinal appearance triggered a market review clause, Cignetti agreed to a new deal worth $13.2M annually through 2033, cementing him among the sport's highest-paid coaches.

updates.headline: Full $105.6M contract details released
updates.summary: The MOU confirmed Cignetti's 8-year, $105.6M deal is the richest at any public university, edging Kirby Smart and Lane Kiffin. It includes a $1M annual retention bonus, $25K car allowance, and free family golf at Pfau.

updates.headline: Big Ten releases Indiana's 2026 schedule
updates.summary: The defending champs open Sept. 5 vs. North Texas, host Ohio State on Oct. 17, travel to Michigan Oct. 24, and host USC Nov. 14 before the Purdue finale.

updates.headline: Spring practice opens with massive roster turnover
updates.summary: Indiana began spring ball with 17 new transfers, highlighted by QB Josh Hoover (TCU) and WR Nick Marsh, as most of the JMU core (Fisher, Kamara, Ponds, Hemby) has departed. Spring game set for April 23.

updates.headline: Cignetti pulls Hoover from first-team reps mid-spring
updates.summary: In a headline-grabbing move, Cignetti sat starter Josh Hoover to give redshirt freshman Tyler Cherry first-team reps — framed as building backup depth rather than a threat to Hoover's job.

updates.headline: Cignetti shoots down $40M roster narrative
updates.summary: Amid offseason NIL spending chatter, Cignetti publicly denied Indiana built its title team with a $40M roster, reinforcing his program-building pitch rather than checkbook recruiting.

items.title: Miami RedHawks basketball 15-0
items.pov_summary: TC's RedHawks are 15-0, cracked the receiving-votes area around #28, and have won 23 straight at Millett Hall. Travis Steele (fired from Xavier, lol) has changed the culture. Haven't been ranked since Zeriaak was in school in the late '90s and haven't made the tournament since 2007. It's appealing five-out shooting basketball.
items.status: season rolling, chasing first ranking since late '90s
items.quote: I never thought I'd see a Miami basketball team this good again.
items.updates_summary: Miami's storybook season delivered in huge fashion after the 15-0 start: they ran the table in the regular season at 31-0, climbed to No. 19 in the AP Poll, earned an at-large NCAA bid, won their First Four game over SMU, and lost in the Round of 64 to Tennessee to finish 32-2. Travis Steele won multiple national Coach of the Year honors (Field of 68, John McLendon) and was a Naismith semifinalist, and he's now finalizing a new contract to stay at Miami after fielding significant outside interest.

updates.headline: Miami RedHawks last undefeated team in Division I at 27-0
updates.summary: Miami reached 27-0 and was ranked No. 22 in the AP, with Steele becoming one of the most sought-after coaches in the country as the RedHawks chased their first MAC regular-season title since 2004-05 and first NCAA bid since 2006-07.

updates.headline: Steele named Field of 68 Coach of the Year as Miami finishes regular season 31-0
updates.summary: Miami completed a 31-0 (18-0 MAC) regular season, claiming its first MAC regular-season title since 2004-05 and setting MAC records for best start, most consecutive conference wins, and longest win streak.

updates.headline: Miami suffers first loss of the season to UMass in MAC tournament
updates.summary: The RedHawks' undefeated run ended with an 87-83 loss to UMass in the MAC tournament, putting their NCAA at-large bid into question heading into Selection Sunday.

updates.headline: Miami's season ends with NCAA Tournament loss to Tennessee
updates.summary: After beating SMU 89-79 in the First Four at UD Arena for the program's first NCAA Tournament win since 1999, No. 11 Miami fell 78-56 to No. 6 Tennessee in the Round of 64 to finish 32-2.

updates.headline: Steele named Naismith Coach of the Year semifinalist
updates.summary: Steele was one of 10 semifinalists for the Naismith Coach of the Year award after Miami set program records for wins (32) and climbed as high as No. 19 — its highest ranking since 1978.

updates.headline: Steele wins John McLendon National Coach of the Year
updates.summary: Steele added the John McLendon National Coach of the Year award to his haul. Peter Suder was named MAC Player of the Year and was a finalist for the Lou Henson Award.

updates.headline: Steele close to signing new contract, says 'I'm not going anywhere'
updates.summary: After fielding interest from other programs mid-season, Steele told Miami's AD he was staying and is finalizing a new contract (on top of his 2025 extension through 2031-32). Miami lost three players to the portal (Byers, Elmer, Perry) and added Milwaukee transfer guard Stevie Elam.

items.title: Harbaugh out as Ravens head coach
items.pov_summary: Harbaugh is out in Baltimore and the hosts feel vindicated — they were on this a year-plus ago. Sunday night Steelers-Ravens game felt like a loser-leave-town. It's a thin coaching cycle overall with Atlanta, Cleveland, Vegas, Tennessee, Giants, and Arizona all open, plus Flus freshly chopped in Dallas.
items.quote: That Sunday night game felt like a loser leave town.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired on Jan. 8, Harbaugh quickly landed on his feet: the Giants signed him to a five-year deal to replace Brian Daboll. The Ravens replaced Harbaugh with Chargers DC Jesse Minter, and the rest of the carousel filled out — Stefanski to Atlanta, Monken to Cleveland, Kubiak to the Raiders, LaFleur to Arizona, Saleh to Tennessee, and McCarthy to Pittsburgh after Mike Tomlin also stepped down. Mike McDaniel was also later fired by Miami, adding another opening beyond the hosts' original list.

updates.headline: Giants hire John Harbaugh as head coach on five-year deal
updates.summary: The Giants went 'all in' on Harbaugh after his Ravens firing and signed him to a five-year deal to replace Brian Daboll, who had been fired midseason.

updates.headline: Ravens hire Jesse Minter to replace Harbaugh
updates.summary: Baltimore replaced Harbaugh with 42-year-old Chargers DC Jesse Minter, who previously spent 2017-2020 as a Ravens defensive assistant under Harbaugh before following Jim Harbaugh to Michigan and the Chargers.

updates.headline: Bisciotti explains firing: 'I made the decision by myself'
updates.summary: Owner Steve Bisciotti said the decision was his alone, that he called Harbaugh on Jan. 6 while Harbaugh was driving home, and the two have since remained close — trading advice about the Ravens job and Harbaugh's next opportunities.

updates.headline: Browns hire Ravens OC Todd Monken as head coach
updates.summary: Cleveland picked Monken over Rams pass game coordinator Nate Scheelhaase and Browns DC Jim Schwartz, meaning Baltimore lost both its head coach and offensive coordinator in the same cycle.

updates.headline: Full 2026 carousel takes shape: Stefanski to Atlanta, Kubiak to Vegas, LaFleur to Arizona, Saleh to Tennessee
updates.summary: Kevin Stefanski (Falcons), Klint Kubiak (Raiders, after the Super Bowl), Mike LaFleur (Cardinals), and Robert Saleh (Titans) all filled openings the hosts had flagged. Mike McCarthy also took over in Pittsburgh after Mike Tomlin's unexpected departure.

updates.headline: Dolphins fire Mike McDaniel, adding another opening to the cycle
updates.summary: Miami moved on from McDaniel after four seasons and an 0-2 playoff record, extending the coaching carousel beyond the initial set discussed on the pod.

items.title: Trae Young on the trade market
items.pov_summary: Trae Young's trade value is reportedly near zero — anonymous exec quotes say nobody wants to play with him, teammates and coaches hate him, and he's not a good guy. Randy's hated him since Oklahoma and has been terrified Sacramento trades for him, though they deny interest. Hawks played great when he was out.
items.status: trade market soft; Hawks apparently better without him
items.quote: This guy's trade value is no like nobody wants to play with him. Everybody on the team hates him.
items.updates_summary: Right after the episode aired, the Hawks traded Trae Young to the Washington Wizards for CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert — confirming the hosts' "near-zero trade value" take, as Atlanta netted only two role players for a four-time All-Star. Young was sidelined with injuries for nearly two months before making his Wizards debut on March 5, 2026, and the Wizards have also acquired Anthony Davis, teeing up a new-look core in D.C.

updates.headline: Hawks Trade Trae Young to Wizards for CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert
updates.summary: Atlanta moved Young in a three-player deal, getting out from under his $49M player option while netting McCollum's expiring $30.6M deal and Kispert. The trade reunited Young with former Hawks GM Travis Schlenk, now in Washington's front office.

updates.headline: Young trade seen as proof of depressed star market ahead of deadline
updates.summary: Analysis framed the return — just two role players for a four-time All-Star and reigning assists leader — as evidence that even star-level talents can't fetch market value when contract and fit concerns stack up.

updates.headline: Trae Young makes long-delayed Wizards debut in loss to Jazz
updates.summary: After missing nearly two months with quad and knee injuries, Young scored 12 points with 6 assists in 19 minutes on a restriction in Washington's 122-112 loss. It was the Wizards' seventh straight defeat, with Washington still chasing a top-8 protected pick.

updates.headline: Young embraces Wizards' defensive identity, acknowledges past 'weak link' reputation
updates.summary: Young publicly committed to improving his defense, saying he'd make sure being targeted as the weak link wouldn't be 'as loud as it's been' — part of a broader attempt to turn a new leaf in Washington after the rocky Atlanta exit.

items.title: FBI investigating New England seafood heists
items.pov_summary: A $400K cache of oysters, crab, and lobster meat was stolen across three sophisticated heists in Casco Bay and Taunton, Mass. — 14 cages of full-grown oysters, a load of crab from a Lineage Logistics warehouse (same Lineage sponsoring pro golfers), and Costco-bound lobster meat grabbed by a fraudulent trucking company. FBI is on the case. Randy jokes TC might need an alibi.
items.quote: TC, I didn't know any better... You might have to get your alibis in order. You know, we know you love seafood.
items.updates_summary: No arrests or major breakthroughs in the FBI's New England seafood heist investigation have been reported since the episode. Post-episode coverage has largely been follow-up analysis on the broader cargo-theft trend the heists exemplified, including a late-January local TV segment and a mid-January supply-chain podcast/blog dissecting how the "fictitious pickup" scheme worked.

updates.headline: Art of Supply breaks down the logistics of the $400K lobster heist
updates.summary: A supply-chain analysis piece detailed how the thieves used a 'fictitious pickup' — spoofing an email address with an added dash, falsely branding a truck, and producing a fake CDL — and then turned off GPS once the load was in transit. No new law-enforcement developments were reported.

updates.headline: Cargo theft like Taunton lobster heist becoming a growing problem
updates.summary: A local I-Team follow-up used the Taunton lobster heist as a case study for the rise in tech-enabled cargo theft, quoting a Bentley University expert on how organized crime is now teaming up with hackers. The story did not report any arrests or recovered product.

items.title: Miracle flight wheelchair abuse investigation
items.pov_summary: Wall Street Journal feature on the 'miracle flight' phenomenon — people wheeled onto NY-to-Florida flights who then walk off under their own power. TC sees it constantly. Big wants the trap draw to really sink its teeth into this righteous story of early-boarding wheelchair abuse in 2026.
items.status: ongoing story; trap draw pledging deeper coverage
items.quote: This feels like the type of righteous story that like we could really sink our teeth in on the trap draw this year just digging deep on the problem of people abusing the early boarding via wheelchair.
items.updates_summary: Since the early January 2026 episode, the 'miracle flight' story has continued generating coverage — notably a late-January Entrepreneur piece citing the WSJ reporting, plus Fox News follow-ups — but no major policy change targeting preboarding abuse has occurred. In adjacent news, DOT paused enforcement of several disability-regulation requirements while considering modifications, and Southwest's move to assigned seating (widely cited as a countermeasure to preboarding gaming) remains in focus ahead of the June 17, 2026 ACAA training-rule compliance deadline.

updates.headline: Able-Bodied Travelers Are Faking Disabilities to Skip Airport Lines
updates.summary: Follow-up to the WSJ feature recounting Carlos Gomez's delayed flight with 25 wheelchair passengers who mostly walked off unassisted; notes Southwest's move to assigned seating is partly a response to preboarding chaos.

updates.headline: DOT pauses enforcement of four disability-rule requirements pending new rulemaking
updates.summary: DOT's Traveling with a Disability page notes it temporarily paused enforcement of four ACAA requirements — covering wheelchair-return liability, refresher-training frequency, pre-departure notifications, and related provisions — while it considers modifications, a development relevant to the broader preboarding/wheelchair debate.

items.title: British family wheels dead grandma onto flight
items.pov_summary: Daily Mail exclusive: a British family allegedly wheeled their dead 89-year-old grandmother onto an easyJet flight from Spain, telling crew she was just 'tired,' Weekend at Bernie's-style. Crew was alerted just before takeoff, flight turned around. Hosts flagging it for follow-up.
items.quote: Like a weekend of Bernie style... Would you like — Did she die in vac? God, that raises so many questions.
items.updates_summary: No substantive follow-up developments have been reported since the episode aired on January 8, 2026. All available coverage dates from December 2025, and as of the most recent reporting, no arrests had been made, the family had not been identified, and Spanish authorities had not announced formal charges or investigation conclusions. The Málaga Civil Guard's stated cause of death remained cardiac arrest, and easyJet continued to maintain the woman had a valid fit-to-fly certificate and was alive at boarding.

items.title: United first class meal goes viral
items.pov_summary: A viral United first class meal post called out a 'bowl of sadness' with 3D-printed mystery meat, cafeteria cheese cubes, and an uncut whole tomato. Hosts concede it's a tough look — catering has improved overall but clearly still has work to do. Big also separately gripes about Jacksonville's new Premier Access self-checkin kiosk being low-rent.
items.quote: Between the 3D printed mystery meat, the cafeteria cheese cubes, and the whole tomato that I needed chainsaw to cut, this is genuinely unbelievable.
items.updates_summary: The viral "bowl of sadness" moment peaked in early January 2026 just before the episode aired, and there's been no meaningful follow-up specifically on that incident since (no refund news, no formal catering overhaul tied to it). However, United did roll out premium-product news in March 2026 — pricing details for the new "Polaris Studio" emphasizing elevated food and beverage — which is tangentially relevant to the hosts' point that catering still has work to do at the top end.

updates.headline: Pricing Revealed: New United "Polaris Studio" Will Offer Champagne, Caviar, More Space
updates.summary: United unveiled pricing and details for its new Polaris Studio suite, leaning into an elevated premium food and beverage experience (champagne, caviar) — a possible response to ongoing criticism that its premium catering doesn't match competitors.

items.title: Eric Adams departs to Dubai and crypto
items.pov_summary: Eric Adams left office Jan 1, immediately flew to Dubai (disappointing he didn't hit Istanbul first), and announced he'll use cryptocurrency to go after violence, educate children, and fight anti-semitism. Hosts will miss him — don't think this is the last we've heard.
items.status: out of office but hosts expect a sequel
items.quote: He's going to be using cryptocurrency to go after violence, educate our children, and deal with anti-semitism.
items.updates_summary: The hosts were right that we hadn't heard the last of Adams. Days after the episode, Adams held a Times Square press conference launching "NYC Token," a meme coin that quickly hit a $580-600M market cap before crashing roughly 75-80%, with blockchain analysts accusing the project of a "rug pull." Adams denied profiting, but reporting revealed his former chief adviser Frank Carone and a real-estate investor linked to Israeli hotels were behind the launch, while a separate entrepreneur claimed Adams hijacked his trademarked "NYC Token" name.

updates.headline: Adams launches 'NYC Token' in Times Square after Dubai/Congo trip
updates.summary: Less than two weeks after leaving office, Adams announced a crypto coin with undisclosed backers, saying profits would fight antisemitism and 'anti-Americanism.' He confirmed he'd just returned from Dubai and the DRC, and said he'd be 'setting up some companies' and working with international business partners.

updates.headline: NYC Token crashes ~75% after launch; rug pull accusations fly
updates.summary: After surging to roughly a $580-600M market cap, NYC Token collapsed within hours once a wallet tied to its creation pulled ~$2.5M in liquidity (adding back only $1.5M). Bubblemaps CEO Nicolas Vaiman called it 'such an obvious rug'; Adams' team denied wrongdoing.

updates.headline: Tech entrepreneur says Adams hijacked his trademarked 'NYC Token' name
updates.summary: Eddie Cullen, founder of crypto company Crescite, says he pitched the NYC Token concept to Adams' administration months earlier and holds a July trademark on the name. He plans to send a cease-and-desist.

updates.headline: AP: Frank Carone and Israeli real-estate investor Yosef Sefi Zvieli behind the token
updates.summary: Reporting revealed Adams' former chief adviser Frank Carone was closely involved in the launch, along with Carone's former client Yosef Sefi Zvieli, a real estate investor tied to Israeli hotels and a controversial Brooklyn homeless shelter. Brock Pierce said he was only looped in after launch and would have assembled 'more qualified' people.

updates.headline: Adams' team formally denies profiting from NYC Token
updates.summary: Through spokesperson Todd Shapiro, Adams insisted he did not move investor funds or profit from the launch, calling reports otherwise 'false and unsupported by evidence,' even as on-chain analysts maintained developers netted roughly $1M.

items.title: Assad living large in Russia
items.pov_summary: NYT piece on Assad living it up in Russia, plus an accompanying piece on him screwing over his own generals and personal assistant financially after dragging them to Moscow. Hosts joke about sending Urban to Russia and appointing Assad to the New College board as a life-swap.
items.quote: There's the Assad one and there's the accompanying piece talking about all of his generals and how he kind of pulled the rug from his like personal assistant.
items.updates_summary: Since the January 8 episode, the biggest development has been Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa's second Moscow visit on January 28, 2026, where Assad's extradition was again raised (and refused by the Kremlin), alongside Russia's withdrawal from the Qamishli airport. In February, Syria's Justice Minister publicly pushed extradition as part of transitional justice. Assad himself remains under strict Russian restrictions — no media, no politics — and reports continue describing his secluded luxury life in Rublyovka.

updates.headline: Al-Sharaa meets Putin in Moscow; Assad extradition again unresolved
updates.summary: Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa made his second visit to Moscow, focused on Russia's military bases, while the Kremlin again declined to say whether it would extradite Assad. Al-Sharaa reiterated there would be justice for victims of Assad's repression.

updates.headline: Russia withdraws from Qamishli airport in northeastern Syria
updates.summary: Russian forces pulled back from Qamishli airport as Sharaa visited Moscow, leaving Russia with only its two Mediterranean bases; Moscow continues refusing to hand over Assad despite Syrian demands.

updates.headline: Syrian Justice Minister pushes for Assad extradition
updates.summary: Syrian Justice Minister Mazhar al-Wais said the government was pressing for the extradition of Assad and his allies as part of its transitional justice effort.

updates.headline: Academic analysis: Assad's exile fits the 'golden parachute' pattern
updates.summary: Princeton researchers used the NYT Assad exile piece as a case study arguing exile in Russia has neutralized him politically — he's playing video games, not plotting a return — unlike opposition-in-exile cases such as Venezuela's.

items.title: Meta accepting rampant China ad fraud
items.pov_summary: Reuters investigation found Meta as a general policy accepts high levels of fraudulent Chinese ads across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp — scams from bogus Taiwanese supplements to investment scams on US/Canadian savers. Meta makes roughly $7B/year from ads it considers 'high risk.' Material revenue, not a rounding error. Big: not good.
items.status: ongoing, no sign of Meta acting
items.quote: It earns about 7 billion a year from uh the portion of scam ads it considers quote high risk.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, two major developments have advanced the Meta China ad fraud story: (1) a class action lawsuit was filed in California federal court in February 2026 targeting Meta's alleged role in enabling stock pump-and-dump scams via Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp ads — with plaintiffs attempting to bypass Section 230 protections; and (2) Meta publicly announced its own lawsuits in late February 2026 against scam advertisers in Brazil, China, and Vietnam, a move widely seen as a response to the Reuters exposés. U.S. senators Gallego and Moreno also introduced legislation to force platforms to take reasonable steps against fraudulent ads.

updates.headline: Class action filed accusing Meta of knowingly facilitating pump-and-dump stock scams
updates.summary: A proposed class action filed February 5 in California federal court alleges Meta turned a blind eye to stock manipulation scammers who used Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp ads. The complaint notably argues Meta charged higher ad rates to suspected scammers rather than banning them — a theory designed to bypass Section 230 immunity.

updates.headline: Meta files lawsuits against Brazil, China, and Vietnam-based scam advertisers
updates.summary: Meta announced lawsuits against deceptive advertisers including China-based Shenzhen Yunzheng Technology (accused of running celeb-bait investment scams targeting US and Japan) and issued cease-and-desist letters to eight marketing consultants who advertised the ability to evade Meta's enforcement. The move comes directly after the Reuters investigation and a review of Meta's Badged Partners program.

updates.headline: U.S. senators introduce bill forcing platforms to take 'reasonable steps' against fraudulent ads
updates.summary: Senators Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) introduced legislation explicitly citing the November Reuters report, which would require online platforms to take reasonable steps to stop fraudulent and deceptive ads.

updates.headline: Meta and Google face growing wave of suits designed to circumvent Section 230
updates.summary: A CNBC analysis frames the Meta scam-ads class action as part of a broader legal trend — including recent verdicts against Meta on child safety — where plaintiffs are crafting cases to pierce Section 230's liability shield, potentially exposing Meta to significant damages over its ad business conduct.

items.title: Bank of America reply-all Outlook meltdown
items.pov_summary: Someone sent an Outlook calendar invite to essentially all of Bank of America, triggering endless reply-all chains, Outlook crashes, and a thousand-email inboxes. Security told employees further reply-alls would be a security issue. Big verified with a BofA buddy that this happens every couple years. TC is tickled.
items.quote: Breaking news. Someone sent an Outlook calendar invite to everyone at BFA. Everyone keeps replying all to the email chain. Outlook keeps crashing. They're trying to take us down from within.
items.updates_summary: No substantive follow-up coverage found between the January 8, 2026 episode and April 18, 2026 on the Bank of America reply-all Outlook incident. Searches surfaced only unrelated BofA news (market outlook, earnings, return-to-office policy) and no reporting on consequences, internal memos, or a recurrence of the email storm.

items.title: Telluride ski patrol strike drags on
items.pov_summary: Ski patrollers are in week two of a strike at Telluride. Mountain opened less than 2% of terrain with temporary (scab) patrollers. Broader dispute between town and the 'maverick' Telluride owner over gondola maintenance and costs. Combined with poor early-season snow, it's a rough start out west — Randy's glad he didn't buy a pass.
items.status: strike ongoing, no resolution in sight
items.quote: No no strike stoppage in sight right now.
items.updates_summary: The strike ended Jan. 8-9, 2026 (the day the episode aired) with patrollers accepting a contract after 13 days — the longest ski patrol strike in U.S. history. But the bigger story exploded afterward: a secret $127.5M offer from two local officials to buy 51% of the resort from owner Chuck Horning led to the resignations of Mountain Village Mayor Marti Prohaska and Telluride Mayor Pro Tem Meehan Fee, the town manager being placed on leave, dual investigations, and national media coverage (WSJ). In March, FOIA documents revealed Telski had federal approval for a contingency plan to keep operating during the strike.

updates.headline: Telluride ski patrol strike ends after 13 days with roughly 20% pay bump
updates.summary: The 72-member TPSPA ratified a new contract with close to a 20% pay increase over the life of the deal — about 50% less than their initial ask. Lift 4 reopened Jan. 10, though less than a third of the mountain was open at that point.

updates.headline: Secret $127.5M offer to buy 51% of Telluride Ski & Golf revealed
updates.summary: Mountain Village Mayor Marti Prohaska and Telluride Mayor Pro Tem Meehan Fee had flown to Horning's Newport Beach office in late December with an investor-backed bid for majority control. The contract leaked on Facebook, Horning filed open records requests, and the resort publicly stated it is not for sale.

updates.headline: Mountain Village Mayor Prohaska resigns amid sale scandal
updates.summary: Prohaska — who is also a ski patroller — stepped down after the Mountain Village council ordered a private investigation into her negotiations with Horning. Fee was later asked to resign by Telluride's council as well.

updates.headline: Town manager placed on leave; dual investigations launched
updates.summary: Mountain Village Town Manager Paul Wisor was placed on paid administrative leave after an accidentally-recorded executive session showed he'd known about the California trip and had connected the officials with investors. Both Telluride and Mountain Village councils hired independent investigators.

updates.headline: FOIA docs: Telluride had federal approval to keep operating during strike
updates.summary: Public-records requests revealed the U.S. Forest Service had approved a contingency plan in late December allowing limited lift operations on federally permitted terrain even without normal patrol staffing — raising fresh questions about Telski's handling of the shutdown.

updates.headline: WSJ dubs saga the 'ski-gate scandal'
updates.summary: The Wall Street Journal published a deep dive on the ownership fight, characterizing Telluride as being in 'a pitched battle with the eccentric owner of its luxury resort' and recounting that Horning has even fired his own son Chad from the company.

items.title: Mike Tomlin steps down after Steelers collapse
items.pov_summary: The hosts give Tomlin credit for stepping away cleanly after a long run rather than trying to go down with the ship. Art Smith's scheme sounds good on a spreadsheet but doesn't translate, and the Steelers are back to square one at QB with Rodgers likely retreating into a cave.
items.status: Tomlin out, Art Smith reportedly interviewing for Lions OC; Steelers QB situation wide open
items.quote: I'll go ahead and make this easy for everybody. Been here a long time. Time for me to move on... he deserves credit for that instead of trying to go down with the ship.
items.updates_summary: The Steelers moved quickly to replace Tomlin, hiring Pittsburgh native and former Packers/Cowboys head coach Mike McCarthy on a five-year deal just 10 days after Tomlin's resignation. McCarthy has assembled a largely new staff (Patrick Graham as DC, Brian Angelichio as OC), and the front office has been aggressive in free agency — trading for WR Michael Pittman Jr. and adding RB Rico Dowdle — while Aaron Rodgers' future remains unresolved, though the McCarthy reunion has fueled speculation he could return for one more year.

updates.headline: Steelers hire Mike McCarthy as new head coach on five-year deal
updates.summary: Pittsburgh hired the former Packers and Cowboys head coach — and Pittsburgh native — as Tomlin's replacement, making him just the fourth Steelers head coach since 1969.

updates.headline: McCarthy introduced as 17th head coach in Steelers history
updates.summary: The team officially introduced McCarthy, noting a 174-112-2 career regular-season record and ties to GM Omar Khan dating back to their time together with the Saints in 2000.

updates.headline: Steelers finalize 2026 coaching staff under McCarthy
updates.summary: Pittsburgh named Patrick Graham defensive coordinator, Brian Angelichio offensive coordinator, Joe Whitt Jr. assistant head coach/secondary, and Danny Crossman special teams coordinator — a near-total overhaul from the Tomlin era.

updates.headline: Steelers trade for WR Michael Pittman Jr., sign RB Rico Dowdle
updates.summary: Pittsburgh acquired Pittman from the Colts to pair with DK Metcalf and added Dowdle (who had success under McCarthy in Dallas), signaling an aggressive offensive reshaping in McCarthy's first offseason.

updates.headline: Rodgers reunion speculation builds after McCarthy hire
updates.summary: Rodgers' 2026 plans remain unresolved, but the hiring of his former Green Bay head coach plus assistants with ties to his past has fueled speculation he could return for another year rather than retire.

updates.headline: Tomlin not planning to coach in 2026
updates.summary: Though multiple teams reached out, Tomlin told them he won't coach next season, with a Sean Payton-style media year seen as the most likely path before a potential return.

items.title: Jags playoff exit and apology tour for Trevor
items.pov_summary: TC won't apologize for the Jags — everyone else needs to apologize to them for a 13-4 season with a first-year coach. Trevor was under duress because Coen egotistically left starters in a meaningless Titans game and lost Van Lanningham. Still, competent coaching resurrected Trevor and TC now owes him an apology for calling him dumb.
items.status: Season over, bullish on 2026 with Travis Hunter returning; Brian Thomas trade speculation
items.quote: It was a masterclass. It was some ego at the end of what ended up being a meaningless game at the end of the year. Um, and I just did not. It cost him.
items.updates_summary: The Jags' offseason has validated the hosts' defense of the team: Lawrence finished 5th in MVP voting and is a finalist for MVP and Comeback Player of the Year, Coen is a finalist for Coach of the Year, and all three coordinators (Udinski, Campanile, Farwell) returned despite multiple head-coaching interviews. Etienne departed in free agency, the Jags signed RB Chris Rodriguez Jr., extended Travon Walker, and head into the draft without a first-rounder (No. 56 is their first pick) after the Travis Hunter trade.

updates.headline: Lawrence, Coen named NFL Honors finalists
updates.summary: Trevor Lawrence was named a finalist for AP MVP and AP Comeback Player of the Year, and Liam Coen a finalist for AP Coach of the Year, with Lawrence ultimately finishing fifth in MVP voting after a franchise-record 38 total touchdowns.

updates.headline: All three coordinators retained for 2026
updates.summary: Offensive coordinator Grant Udinski, defensive coordinator Anthony Campanile and special teams coordinator Heath Farwell all returned for 2026 despite Udinski interviewing for the Bills/Browns HC jobs and Campanile interviewing for the Ravens/Dolphins/Cardinals jobs — a rare continuity edge in the NFL.

updates.headline: Lawrence has first fully healthy offseason since 2023
updates.summary: Lawrence said he feels good entering the 2026 offseason with no rehab limitations — a sharp contrast to last year's recovery from shoulder surgery — and Coen praised his in-game audibles as having reached 'triple-layer cake' level late in 2025.

updates.headline: Coen at league meetings: team can't chase 13 wins
updates.summary: At the NFL annual meetings, Coen emphasized urgency and warned that improvement in 2026 may not look like 13 wins, also addressing the departure of leading rusher Travis Etienne Jr. and the addition of RB Chris Rodriguez Jr.

updates.headline: Jags enter draft without a first-rounder; Travon Walker extended
updates.summary: At the pre-draft luncheon, GM James Gladstone confirmed Jacksonville's first pick is No. 56 overall (having traded their '26 first to Cleveland in the Travis Hunter deal) and announced a contract extension for DE Travon Walker. Travis Hunter will be a limited participant through the offseason program as he returns from a Week 9 knee injury.

items.title: Guy watching porn at max volume in O'Hare stall
items.pov_summary: DJ reports a man in the stall next to him at O'Hare Terminal 1 Concourse C was blasting porn with no headphones. The hosts are disturbed by the broader societal collapse of people not using headphones in public, and wonder if Texas-style age verification laws have driven people to desperate measures.
items.quote: I don't want to speculate on what he was doing specifically other than what I could hear.
items.updates_summary: While no direct follow-up emerged on the specific O'Hare stall incident, the broader "no-headphones in public" problem the hosts ranted about had a notable flare-up: in late February/early March 2026, a viral incident saw an American Airlines passenger booted from a Miami-to-Tampa flight for repeatedly playing videos out loud, and United Airlines updated its conduct rules to explicitly allow refusal of service to passengers who won't use headphones. Separately, a man was arrested at Austin-Bergstrom in February after allegedly filming in a staff restroom stall — where he admitted to police he'd been watching porn on his phone.

updates.headline: American Airlines passenger kicked off flight over refusing to use headphones
updates.summary: A passenger on a Miami–Tampa American Airlines flight had a viral meltdown after being removed for repeatedly playing videos out loud. The story notes United Airlines recently updated its passenger conduct rules to include a headphone requirement, stating the airline can refuse transport to passengers who won't use headphones with audio/video content.

updates.headline: Man arrested at Austin airport, told police he was watching porn in staff restroom stall
updates.summary: LaShawn Garrett, 35, was arrested at Austin-Bergstrom after a worker spotted a phone camera under the stall partition. Garrett told officers he had been sitting on the toilet watching porn on his phone — a Texas angle the hosts specifically speculated about regarding age-verification laws.

items.title: Airline status, lounges and United vs American
items.pov_summary: Soly rides hard for American (Executive Platinum) and is baffled he hasn't been gifted ConciergeKey yet. TC and Cody argue over United vs American, with universal agreement that the Dulles and Dallas United lounges are dungeons. Neil is committed to United and refuses to chase segments.
items.status: ongoing tribal warfare; Soly still waiting on ConciergeKey invite
items.quote: It blows my mind how much you ride for American that you haven't received the invitation to Concierge Key.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, United revealed sweeping lounge expansion plans that directly address the hosts' Dulles complaint: a new 39,000-sq-ft United Club is set to open at IAD in tandem with a new terminal by fall 2026, a massive 54,000-sq-ft club is coming to Houston, and CEO Scott Kirby teased another "big" Dulles club tied to a proposed Saarinen terminal connector. On the American side, the main post-episode development is the rollout of the March 1, 2026 AAdvantage terms and continued ConciergeKey challenge/invitation cycles, though no meaningful news about Soly actually getting CK.

updates.headline: United eyes huge Houston lounge, teases 'even bigger' Dulles club
updates.summary: United plans to open at least three new United Clubs in 2026, including a 39,000-sq-ft facility at Dulles alongside a new terminal opening by fall, a 54,000-sq-ft behemoth at Houston IAH (set to be the largest airline-branded lounge in America), and an SFO club. Kirby also teased an additional 'big' Dulles club tied to a proposed connector building linking the Saarinen main terminal to the A/B concourse — directly relevant to the hosts' Dulles 'dungeon' complaint.

updates.headline: New AAdvantage terms and conditions take effect
updates.summary: American Airlines' updated AAdvantage terms took effect March 1, 2026, formally defining ConciergeKey as part of AAdvantage Status and clarifying that CK membership years run from acceptance/May 1 through April 30 — the window in which Executive Platinums like Soly would typically be tapped.

items.title: Fermentation, flyovers, spiral staircases, rain chains
items.pov_summary: Nominations include fermentation (nature's water-to-wine), F-35 flyovers at Jags games (pilots getting training hours, not wasteful), sick-but-non-functional spiral staircases that add texture to a structure, ceviche acid cooking, Wi-Fi auto-connecting at places you haven't been in years, and rain chains off gutters.
items.quote: Flowers are really cool... these guys are getting their training hours and like they're getting their they're and they're flying.
items.updates_summary: This was a lifestyle/appreciation show-bits segment (fermentation, flyovers, spiral staircases, rain chains, etc.), so there's no tracking-worthy "news" per se. The one tangential development: Super Bowl LX on Feb. 8, 2026 featured a notable military flyover relevant to the hosts' F-35 appreciation — originally planned to include F-22s alongside F-35s, but the F-22s were pulled for operational assignments, with F-35Cs still participating in the first-ever joint Air Force–Navy Super Bowl flyover.

updates.headline: F-22s pulled from Super Bowl LX flyover; F-35Cs remain in first joint Air Force–Navy formation
updates.summary: The Super Bowl LX flyover over Levi's Stadium went ahead as the first joint Air Force–Navy Super Bowl flyover, with two B-1Bs, two F-15Cs, two F/A-18E Super Hornets, and two F-35C Lightning IIs. Originally-planned F-22 Raptors were pulled for operational assignments. A planner reiterated the hosts' exact point: these flyovers serve as time-over-target training for crews, not waste.

items.title: Eagles offense sputtering, Jalen Hurts is who we thought
items.pov_summary: Neil says Hurts is who we thought he is — the AJ Brown drop cost them, but Hurts refuses to throw downfield and isn't running anymore. Super Bowl and MVP buy him time. Eagles fans are in shambles realizing they have a lemon at QB.
items.status: season over, Patullo fired, QB question lingers
items.quote: My fellow Eagles fans are in shambles right now. I think they're realizing that they they kind of have a lemon at quarterback.
items.updates_summary: Significant developments have validated the podcast's concerns. The Eagles fired OC Kevin Patullo and hired inexperienced Sean Mannion, while ESPN published a bombshell report detailing internal "friction" and Hurts' "rigid preferences" — including his refusal to play under center. The Eagles reportedly have no plans to extend Hurts, who has only one year of guaranteed money left, and A.J. Brown remains in persistent trade rumors.

updates.headline: Hurts doesn't complete a pass beyond 14 air yards in Eagles' wild-card loss to 49ers
updates.summary: Hurts went 20-of-35 for 168 yards with a 4.8 YPA in the 23-19 loss. A 49ers WR (Jauan Jennings) had a longer air-yards completion than Hurts all game, underscoring his refusal/inability to push the ball downfield.

updates.headline: Hurts declines to endorse OC Kevin Patullo after playoff exit
updates.summary: A day after the loss, Hurts refused to back Patullo, signaling he was ready for yet another new play-caller. Patullo's house had been egged during the season as fans directed frustration at him.

updates.headline: Eagles fire Patullo, hire inexperienced Sean Mannion as new OC
updates.summary: After a highly publicized search that didn't land bigger names like Mike McDaniel or Brian Daboll, the Eagles hired 33-year-old Sean Mannion, who has never called plays. He'll implement a McVay/Shanahan-style system with more under-center and play-action — something Hurts has historically resisted.

updates.headline: ESPN bombshell: Hurts' 'rigid preferences' frustrated coaches and teammates
updates.summary: Jeremy Fowler and Tim McManus reported extensive internal friction, with Hurts 'continually fighting' going under center because he doesn't want to turn his back on the defense. A.J. Brown's relationship with Hurts was described as tested, and sources said Hurts needs coaches who will 'check him.'

updates.headline: Eagles reportedly have no plans to extend Hurts this offseason
updates.summary: Per Jeff McLane, Philadelphia is intentionally not extending Hurts — viewed internally as a message. 2026 is the last year of his guaranteed money ($51.5M), making it a true prove-it year.

updates.headline: A.J. Brown trade rumors persist; Eagles add Hollywood Brown and Elijah Moore
updates.summary: The Eagles declined to definitively shut down A.J. Brown trade rumors at the league meetings, with many expecting a post-June 1 trade. Philly added Hollywood Brown, Elijah Moore, and Dontayvion Wicks to the WR room as potential insurance.

items.title: 49ers substation electromagnetic pulse conspiracy
items.pov_summary: Agents are reportedly calling the NFL claiming a substation near Levi's Stadium is emitting electromagnetic pulses that weaken tendons and ligaments, explaining the Niners' injury woes. TC notes it's peak Trap Draw content — conspiracy, Niners, and Kyle all in one. Others say the real reason is they actually hit in practice.
items.quote: It's that's just two trap like everything about the trap all lined in one thing. A conspiracy and Niners and Kyle.
items.updates_summary: The conspiracy theory escalated from meme status to official investigation. The 49ers hired an independent scientist who concluded the substation's EMF levels were hundreds of times below unsafe thresholds, effectively debunking the theory. Along the way, NFL CMO Dr. Allen Sills publicly dismissed it, and owner Jed York mocked it at Super Bowl Radio Row.

updates.headline: Niners to look into 'everything,' including substation theory
updates.summary: GM John Lynch confirmed the team would formally investigate the viral substation theory as part of a broader injury review, saying health and safety required looking at 'everything' even if the theory had been debunked by scientists.

updates.headline: NFL medical official addresses viral conspiracy theory linking 49ers injuries to electrical substation
updates.summary: NFL Chief Medical Officer Dr. Allen Sills addressed the theory at a league health and safety briefing, saying he was not aware of anything in sports medicine literature supporting the EMF-injury link.

updates.headline: Jed York addresses substation theory at Super Bowl Radio Row
updates.summary: 49ers owner Jed York dismissed the theory during an appearance with Kay Adams, joking that the substation had been near the facility since 1987 and it 'didn't seem to affect Jerry Rice.' NFLPA president Jalen Reeves-Maybin said the union was monitoring but emphasized the need for credible data.

updates.headline: Injuries, energy fields and the strange theory that has made its way to the Super Bowl
updates.summary: Ahead of Super Bowl LX at Levi's Stadium, the theory became a national story, with former Niner Josh Dobbs (an aerospace engineer) saying he couldn't rule it out and ex-Niner Eric Saubert calling it a recurring locker-room joke.

updates.headline: The 49ers Hired a Scientist to Investigate Electrical Substation. Here's What They Found
updates.summary: Lynch revealed the results of the independent scientific investigation, calling it a 'big nothing burger' and saying EMF levels at the facility were measured at 400 times below unsafe zones — effectively ending the organization's inquiry into the theory.

items.title: MLB headed toward 2027 Armageddon lockout
items.pov_summary: Soly is sounding the alarm: Kyle Tucker just signed $240M with the Dodgers, LA has eight $100M+ contracts and keeps 100% of TV revenue from their bankruptcy deal. Small-market teams like the Reds literally cannot compete. Owners want a cap, players won't budge — a 2027 lockout looks inevitable.
items.status: impending lockout, no elegant solution proposed
items.quote: Baseball's headed towards Armageddon. It's really bad... they are tripling down on all of this and we are headed towards Armageddon in 2027.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the drumbeat toward a 2027 lockout has grown louder. MLBPA acting executive director Bruce Meyer publicly called a lockout "all but guaranteed," Tony Clark resigned amid the Players Way scandal with Meyer taking over on an interim basis, and ESPN's Jeff Passan reported nearly every owner wants a cap and is "ready to burn the f—ing house down." The union has also more than doubled its strike-fund war chest compared to the last CBA cycle, and Meyer warned a missed 2027 season could jeopardize MLB player participation in the 2028 LA Olympics.

updates.headline: Chris Bassitt blasts salary cap idea as MLBPA subcommittee digs in
updates.summary: Orioles pitcher and MLBPA executive subcommittee member Chris Bassitt publicly argued a salary cap 'doesn't fix anything,' signaling players are unequivocally opposed heading into negotiations. Jeff Passan separately reported nearly every owner wants a cap and is 'ready to burn the f—ing house down,' even if it jeopardizes 2027.

updates.headline: MLBPA war chest more than doubles ahead of CBA fight
updates.summary: Reports from Jesse Castillo and Evan Drellich indicate the union's reserve fund has more than doubled since the last round of negotiations (from ~$171-192M to a much larger sum), with players allowing the union to withhold licensing checks since 2024 to prepare for a work stoppage. Tony Clark has resigned amid the Players Way federal investigation, with Bruce Meyer taking over on an interim basis.

updates.headline: The Ringer: TV rights consolidation may be the defining negotiation of the decade
updates.summary: A labor-battle FAQ notes MLB has assumed production/distribution for 14 teams in 2026 after the Main Street Sports Group collapse, and Manfred hopes to bundle all 30 teams' local rights for a 2028 streaming package — a fight that could matter more than the salary cap itself.

updates.headline: Meyer: Lockout 'all but guaranteed,' could threaten 2028 Olympics
updates.summary: At the World Baseball Classic, acting MLBPA executive director Bruce Meyer said a lockout is 'all but guaranteed' when the CBA expires Dec. 1, 2026, and warned that if 2027 games are missed, MLB players may not participate in the 2028 LA Olympics at Dodger Stadium.

items.title: Kiefer Sutherland and Daniel Stern arrests
items.pov_summary: Kiefer Sutherland allegedly told an Uber driver 'pull over or I'll kill you' — tough one because Jack Bauer's voice in the back seat will not play well in court. Home Alone's Daniel Stern got popped for soliciting a prostitute in Camarillo, prompting great wet bandits replies online. Tough one for Marv.
items.quote: That'd be really tough for the voice of Jack Bower in the back seat. That's going to work against him in court.
items.updates_summary: Both cases have seen major developments since the episode. Daniel Stern's solicitation case was dismissed on February 6 after he completed an education class, though he had already exited an ABC comedy pilot over the charge. Kiefer Sutherland's case remains under DA review as of March, with new evidence (including potential dash cam footage) being examined; no charges have been formally filed yet.

updates.headline: New allegations: Kiefer Sutherland allegedly punched and tried to choke Uber driver
updates.summary: Law enforcement sources told ABC News that <cite index="2-2">Sutherland allegedly punched an Uber driver multiple times and tried to choke him</cite>, and that <cite index="2-9">it appeared to those on scene that Sutherland was under the influence during the altercation</cite>. Sources also said the driver's car had a dash cam that may have captured the incident.

updates.headline: Daniel Stern's prostitution solicitation case dismissed
updates.summary: <cite index="14-1,14-2">A Ventura County judge dismissed the misdemeanor solicitation case against Daniel Stern following a hearing on February 6</cite>. The DA's office said <cite index="14-7,14-8,14-9">the defendant completed his education class and earned a dismissal, which is standard disposition for first-time prostitution offenses</cite>.

updates.headline: Stern exited ABC comedy pilot after charge was filed
updates.summary: A collateral consequence: <cite index="14-5,14-6">In January, following the filing of the formal charge, Stern exited the ABC comedy pilot 'Do You Want Kids?', where he had been cast as a series regular</cite>.

updates.headline: DA reviewing new evidence in Sutherland case; no charges yet
updates.summary: <cite index="3-16,3-17,3-18">Sutherland's case is under review by the L.A. County District Attorney's Office. He was arrested on suspicion of felony criminal threats after allegedly assaulting the driver, but no charges have been filed yet, and he was released on a $50,000 bond the same day of his arrest</cite>. <cite index="3-19,3-20">TMZ reports the case is being closely examined as more information comes to light, with the DA's office reviewing newly submitted evidence</cite>.

updates.headline: Sutherland spotted taking another Uber at LAX
updates.summary: In a notable sighting, <cite index="1-3">Sutherland was spotted for the first time since he allegedly threatened to kill his Uber driver — and apparently grabbed another ride with the service</cite> at LAX.

items.title: Vervet monkeys and goats loose in St. Louis
items.pov_summary: Up to four vervet monkeys are on the loose in North St. Louis with no one knowing how they got there, and they may have joined forces with a pack of loose goats running amok. The hosts are out on exotic pets generally — it always goes poorly.
items.status: still at large, possibly running in packs
items.quote: I think the goats and the monkeys potentially have joined together and just kind of run in a muck.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the St. Louis "monkey mystery" has quietly fizzled out. Police pushed back on the case's only "verified" sighting, saying the 911 call came from a mail carrier relaying a secondhand report from a coworker, raising doubts about whether the vervet monkeys ever actually existed. No captures, owners, or new confirmed sightings have been reported; the loose goat's fate also remains unknown.

updates.headline: Are the monkeys even real? Police dispute report of officer seeing loose monkey in north St. Louis
updates.summary: St. Louis Metropolitan Police cast doubt on the lone 'verified' sighting that had anchored the investigation, saying the 911 call came from a mail carrier passing along a coworker's secondhand claim, and that police have been unable to reach the alleged witness.

items.title: Argentina meteor tsunami hits Mar del Plata
items.pov_summary: A meteo-tsunami with waves up to 16 feet hit Mar del Plata, killing one and injuring 35 on a beach that holds tens of thousands daily. No seismic or meteorological models predicted it — a sudden atmospheric pressure change created a wave wall. Also, Argentina repaid the US Treasury loan with interest.
items.quote: There's no meteorological or scientific or seismic studies that predicted it... it's like a perfect storm that creates a wave wall.
items.updates_summary: No substantive new developments were found between the episode date (Jan 16, 2026) and April 18, 2026. Coverage from mid-January confirmed the event was a meteotsunami caused by atmospheric pressure changes, but no follow-up investigations, official scientific reports, or new warning-system announcements appear to have been published in the weeks since.

items.title: Ex-mayor Eric Adams launches rug-pull NYC coin
items.pov_summary: Fresh off trips to Dubai and the DRC, private citizen Eric Adams launched the NYC token claiming it would fight antisemitism — it hit $600M valuation then lost 75% within hours after an insider account dumped $2.5M worth. Sie's take: don't buy any of these coins ever, just buy Bitcoin, and you deserve to lose your money if you fall for it.
items.status: apparent rug pull, Adams now zero-fucks private citizen
items.quote: Like literally all of them go like this. Like all of them. You're just counting on people being stupid and people keep doing it.
items.updates_summary: Limited substantive follow-up since the episode. A tech entrepreneur surfaced claiming he trademarked "NYCToken" before Adams' launch and pitched the idea to the Adams administration, adding a potential IP dispute to the saga. No regulatory action, lawsuit, or criminal investigation into the token itself has been publicly reported in the three months since the launch.

updates.headline: Adams' NYC Token Accused of Hijacking Trademark From Tech Entrepreneur
updates.summary: Tech entrepreneur Eddie Cullen told THE CITY he had pitched the NYCToken concept to the Adams administration last summer and obtained a trademark on the name in July, claiming Adams' team ran with his idea. Adams' spokesperson Todd Shapiro declined to comment on Cullen's assertions.

updates.headline: Mamdani Administration Seeks to End City-Funded Legal Defense for Adams in Separate Sexual Assault Suit
updates.summary: Not directly token-related, but part of the continuing Adams post-mayoral fallout: NYC's corporation counsel moved to withdraw from defending Adams in a 1993 sexual assault civil case, arguing he wasn't acting within the scope of city employment. Shows the broader post-office legal environment Adams is navigating alongside the token controversy.

items.title: Chinese universities surge past Harvard in rankings
items.pov_summary: The new number one school in the world is in China, with seven Chinese schools in the top 10 and Harvard dropped to three. Neil isn't worried about his Columbia CV taking a hit — the hosts agree the whole ranking system is pay-for-play BS like any top 100 list.
items.status: ongoing, hosts skeptical of rankings methodology
items.quote: The ranking system is Yeah. A lot of pay for play going on there. Just like top 10 top 100 list.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, coverage has broadened from the initial Leiden Ranking news into wider analyses of American higher-ed decline. Chinese state media responded with a measured editorial, and follow-up reporting has framed the ranking shift as part of a broader "reverse brain drain" tied to Trump administration research cuts, though some analysts argue the rankings don't capture the full picture.

updates.headline: Global Times editorial urges composure over Leiden Ranking results
updates.summary: China's state-run Global Times responded to the ranking with a notably measured editorial, <cite index="12-6,12-7">calling the Leiden Ranking "a mirror, reflecting both our achievements and our shortcomings" and acknowledging that Harvard and Oxford remain "dream schools" for many Chinese students</cite>.

updates.headline: Analysts push back on 'American decline' framing
updates.summary: A follow-up analysis argued <cite index="3-14,3-15,3-16">Zhejiang University surged to No. 1 not because Harvard faltered but because Chinese institutions dramatically expanded research capacity, noting Harvard actually produces more research today than it did two decades ago, along with six other major US universities — the issue is not American regression but others advancing faster</cite>.

updates.headline: Report details scope of Chinese dominance and US funding cuts
updates.summary: Deeper reporting revealed <cite index="13-7">19 of the top 25 universities in the world are now Chinese</cite>, and tied the shift to <cite index="13-23,13-24">a funding gap where the National Science Foundation's Technology, Innovation, and Partnership directorate received only $410 million of its authorized $4 billion annual budget, starving critical research initiatives</cite>.

updates.headline: The Week surveys six major ranking systems showing US slippage
