updates.summary: A follow-up analysis looked across multiple ranking systems, finding that <cite index="15-12,15-13,15-14">while an American university was ranked first in the initial iteration of each system, in four of the systems the number of top-10 spots occupied by US universities declined, with Chinese schools replacing many American institutions</cite>.

items.title: DJ's podcast gear lost in FedEx limbo
items.pov_summary: DJ forgot all his podcast equipment before flying to Orlando to caddie, tried to have his wife ship it, and FedEx lost the package. He's furious at FedEx, can't get anyone on the phone with actual power, and is recording on a $30 karaoke mic he bought at Guitar Center. He misses AJ at the Atlantic Beach UPS store.
items.status: ongoing — package whereabouts unknown, DJ still trying to track it down
items.quote: I would love to meet anybody that has any power at FedEx right now who can tell me where the [ __ ] this package is.
items.updates_summary: This was a personal anecdote about DJ's lost FedEx package containing his podcast gear — not a public news story. No meaningful developments are trackable via news sources; any resolution would surface on subsequent Trap Draw episodes rather than in reporting.

items.title: Brutal cold snap killing Florida plants
items.pov_summary: TC's plants are all dying from an unprecedented cold stretch in Jacksonville — six of the ten coldest nights since he moved there nine years ago have been in the last three weeks. He feels like he's gone soft but also thinks this isn't what he signed up for living in Florida.
items.status: ongoing, hosts waiting for it to end
items.quote: That's not supposed to happen here and that's not why I live here. I need to talk to the manager. This is unacceptable.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the scale of damage from Florida's brutal cold snap has come into focus at the state and federal level. FDACS released preliminary estimates pegging statewide agricultural losses at over $3.1 billion, the USDA issued a disaster declaration, and growers began applying for block grant relief. An April USDA update suggested Florida's citrus crop held up better than feared, though tree and future-production losses remain significant.

updates.headline: Florida freeze damage estimate tops $3 billion
updates.summary: FDACS released preliminary loss estimates from winter storms Ezra and Gianna topping $3 billion, with citrus ($674M), strawberries ($307M), sweet corn ($255M), and greenhouse/nursery ($240M) hit hardest.

updates.headline: Citrus industry lost 15% of its trees to the freeze
updates.summary: A deeper FDACS breakdown estimated 80% of Florida's citrus acreage was significantly affected, with an average annual productivity loss of 27% projected to persist for years.

updates.headline: USDA issues disaster declaration for Florida freeze damage
updates.summary: USDA formally declared a disaster covering dozens of Florida counties, unlocking federal relief as FDACS's preliminary estimate held at more than $3.1 billion in statewide agricultural losses.

updates.headline: Florida citrus growers can apply for $600M+ in USDA disaster grants
updates.summary: Florida became the first state to implement the Block Grant Disaster Relief Program, with growers who lost at least 10% of their crop eligible for up to about $200 per acre in future-loss payments through August 25, 2026.

updates.headline: USDA revises Florida citrus forecast upward despite freeze
updates.summary: The latest USDA NASS forecast bumped Florida's 2025/26 citrus estimate up 2% to 12.2 million boxes — only 1% below last year — suggesting growers weathered the February freeze better than initially feared, though Valencia fruit size was below average and droppage above.

items.title: Winter Olympics in Italian Alps preview
items.pov_summary: TC is more into the Winter Olympics than Summer and thinks people are sleeping on them because the last two (China and Sochi) sucked. He's into skeleton, luge, biathlon, curling, speed skating. Italian Alps should be a glitzy, baller setting. DJ is indifferent but could be won over.
items.quote: This is going to be a baller, you know, baller Italian Alps, you know, pretty glitzy, glamorous... You're whipping me around a little bit.
items.updates_summary: The Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics ran from February 6-22 and delivered the kind of spectacle TC was hyped about. Norway dominated with a record 18 golds and 41 total medals, while the U.S. had its best Winter Games ever (12 golds, 33 total), capped by the men's hockey team beating Canada 2-1 in OT for its first gold since 1980. Standout storylines included Johannes Høsflot Klæbo winning six cross-country golds (a single-Games record), Mikaela Shiffrin winning slalom gold, Jordan Stolz sweeping the men's speed skating distances, and host Italy finishing fourth.

updates.headline: Norway sets Winter Olympics record with 18 golds; USA finishes second with historic 12
updates.summary: Norway broke its own Winter Games medal record with 41 total medals, while the United States posted its best-ever Winter gold total with 12 and 33 medals overall. Jordan Stolz and Alysa Liu tied as top U.S. gold medalists.

updates.headline: USA beats Canada 2-1 in OT for first men's hockey gold since 1980
updates.summary: Jack Hughes scored the overtime winner as Team USA claimed its first men's ice hockey Olympic gold in 46 years, closing the Games on the final day.

updates.headline: Klæbo becomes first athlete to win six golds at a single Winter Olympics
updates.summary: Norwegian cross-country star Johannes Høsflot Klæbo capped his Games with a sixth gold in the men's 50km, setting single-Games and career (11 total) Winter Olympic gold-medal records.

updates.headline: USA women's hockey avenges 2022 loss, beats Canada 2-1 in OT for gold
updates.summary: Team USA claimed its third women's ice hockey Olympic title, and Alysa Liu became the first American woman in 24 years to win figure skating gold.

updates.headline: Canada wins men's curling gold over Great Britain
updates.summary: Skip Brad Jacobs led Canada to a 9-6 win over GB for Canada's fourth Olympic men's curling title — one for the curling fans TC mentioned.

updates.headline: Milano Cortina Paralympics conclude; China tops table, US sled hockey wins fifth straight gold
updates.summary: China led the Paralympic medal table with 44 medals including 15 golds, while the US men's sled hockey team won a record fifth consecutive gold with a 6-2 win over Canada.

items.title: Hard Knocks in-season NFC East review
items.pov_summary: DJ loved the in-season Hard Knocks. His takeaway: Jalen Hurts doesn't have a single interpersonal relationship with anyone on the Eagles, Sirianni's motivational tactics are laughable, and the Commanders are the most boring franchise in America now that Snyder's gone.
items.quote: Jaylen Herz, I don't think he has a single interpersonal relationship or emotional connection with a single guy on his team.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, DJ's critical read of the Eagles has been vindicated by major offseason reporting. An ESPN long-form piece detailed friction between Jalen Hurts, coaches and teammates (including A.J. Brown), confirming the "calcified" offense narrative; the Eagles fired OC Kevin Patullo and hired 33-year-old Sean Mannion, while reports say Philly has no plans to extend Hurts. Lurie publicly backed Hurts and Sirianni but framed 2026 as a critical year.

updates.headline: ESPN: Inside the Eagles' 2025 friction as Jalen Hurts stands at a crossroads
updates.summary: Long-form ESPN report describes Hurts having 'rigid preferences,' pushing back on coaches, a 'tense' prior relationship with Kellen Moore, and strained ties with A.J. Brown — corroborating DJ's take that Hurts lacks strong interpersonal bonds in the building.

updates.headline: Eagles hire Sean Mannion as offensive coordinator after firing Patullo
updates.summary: Philadelphia moved on from OC Kevin Patullo and hired 33-year-old Sean Mannion to implement a new, Shanahan/McVay-style scheme — Hurts' sixth play-caller of his career.

updates.headline: Lurie calls Hurts' 2025 'good,' signals critical 2026 ahead
updates.summary: At the league meetings, owner Jeffrey Lurie offered a terse one-word review of Hurts' season and the Eagles reportedly have no plans to extend him this offseason despite the looming guarantee cliff after 2026.

updates.headline: Howie Roseman responds to ESPN's Hurts report
updates.summary: Roseman publicly addressed the wave of anonymous criticism of Hurts, saying the Eagles handle issues 'directly' with players, while Jason Kelce defended Hurts on Pat McAfee's show.

items.title: Miami RedHawks basketball undefeated in MAC
items.pov_summary: TC's Miami RedHawks are still undefeated, ranked 23rd, and nobody will schedule them non-conference. He's taking it one game at a time but the remaining schedule is soft enough they might run the table. Meanwhile THG (Xavier) is in shambles under Patino Jr.
items.quote: Nobody will play them. Like nobody will schedule them non-conference.
items.updates_summary: Miami finished the regular season a perfect 31-0 and won the MAC regular-season title, with Travis Steele named MAC Coach of the Year and Peter Suder named MAC Player of the Year. However, they were shockingly upset by 8-seed UMass 87-83 in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals, then earned an at-large 11 seed in the NCAA Tournament, beat SMU 89-79 in the First Four for their first NCAA tourney win since 1999, before losing to 6-seed Tennessee 78-56 to end the season 32-2.

updates.headline: Miami goes 30-0, clinches 22nd MAC regular-season title with win over Toledo
updates.summary: The RedHawks beat Toledo 74-72 in front of a sellout Millett Hall crowd to clinch the outright MAC regular-season title — their first since 2005 — and become just the fourth team this century to start 30-0.

updates.headline: Steele named MAC Coach of the Year, Suder MAC Player of the Year
updates.summary: Travis Steele was a unanimous selection as MAC Coach of the Year and Peter Suder was named MAC Player of the Year, with three additional RedHawks earning All-MAC honors. Steele is Miami's first MAC COY since Charlie Coles in 2005.

updates.headline: UMass stuns undefeated Miami 87-83 in MAC quarterfinals
updates.summary: 8-seed UMass upset the 31-0 RedHawks in the MAC quarters behind a 41-24 rebounding edge and 24-point advantage in the paint, ending Miami's bid at an undefeated regular season carrying into a guaranteed NCAA bid.

updates.headline: Miami earns at-large NCAA Tournament bid as 11 seed, headed to First Four vs. SMU
updates.summary: The RedHawks earned their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2007 and became the first MAC at-large since the 1999 Wally Szczerbiak team, drawing SMU in Dayton with a potential first-round matchup against 6-seed Tennessee.

updates.headline: Miami beats SMU 89-79 in First Four for first NCAA Tournament win in 27 years
updates.summary: Eian Elmer scored 22 points on 6-of-9 from three as Miami rolled SMU in front of a partisan Dayton crowd to move to 32-1 and notch the program's first tournament win since 1999.

updates.headline: Tennessee ends Miami's season 78-56 in Round of 64
updates.summary: Ja'Kobi Gillespie scored 29 points with six threes as 6-seed Tennessee cruised past the RedHawks. Peter Suder had 27 points in the loss; Miami finished 32-2.

items.title: Brewers trade Freddy Peralta to Mets
items.pov_summary: DJ is conflicted on the Brewers trading Freddy Peralta. He was on team keep-Peralta given the $8M price tag and best record in baseball, but gets it — Brewers have to live that way. Jet Williams sounds like Dustin Pedroia 2.0 built in a lab. Losing Tobias Myers quietly stings.
items.quote: If we're not kind of pushing chips in now, like when does that when does that happen?
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the early returns on the trade show the Mets getting more immediate MLB production while the Brewers wait on their prospect haul. Peralta has been excellent in New York, Tobias Myers has thrived in a bigger role, and Brandon Sproat impressed in spring training, while Jett Williams is still working through a slow start in Triple-A.

updates.headline: Mets option prospect Jonah Tong to Triple-A, validating Brewers' choice of Sproat
updates.summary: The Mets optioned prospect Jonah Tong to Triple-A after a shaky spring, while Brandon Sproat drew rave reviews in Brewers camp with a six-pitch mix, an early signal Milwaukee picked the right arm in the Peralta package.

updates.headline: Peralta trade revisited after Mets' 9-8 start: Peralta, Myers both performing in New York
updates.summary: Through four starts, Peralta has a 3.86 ERA with a 25-to-8 K/BB ratio, and Tobias Myers has a 3.46 ERA in six appearances in an expanded role. Meanwhile, Jett Williams is slashing .217/.347/.250 with zero homers in Triple-A and has not yet been promoted; Williams has also slid from the No. 30 to No. 46 overall prospect.

items.title: Jim McBride, Alan Jackson co-writer, RIP
items.pov_summary: TC pays respect to country songwriter Jim McBride, who co-wrote Chattahoochee and 14 Alan Jackson songs across five early-90s albums. Callback to the Nebraska trip conversation about the deceptive complexity of great country songwriting.
items.quote: There's a guy that could do it well. Uh you know, RIP.
items.updates_summary: Little has happened since the episode aired beyond the planned public memorial. A public celebration of McBride's life was held Feb. 19, 2026, at Mars Music Hall in Huntsville's Von Braun Center, open to the public.

updates.headline: Public memorial service for Jim McBride scheduled for Feb. 19 in Huntsville
updates.summary: A public memorial honoring McBride was held on Feb. 19, 2026, from 5-8 p.m. at Mars Music Hall in the Von Braun Center in Huntsville, AL — the most notable follow-up event after the episode's Feb. 5 air date.

items.title: Jimmy Buffett studio becoming a bar
items.pov_summary: Parrot heads are furious that Jimmy Buffett's old Key West recording studio is being converted into a bar. DJ stands with the outraged fans — there's no shortage of Buffett-themed bars, and this is a chance to preserve something authentic.
items.status: ongoing, hosts siding with parrot heads
items.quote: I just don't think we've got a shortage of Jimmy Buffet themed bars going. Maybe we just preserve, you know, something a little authentic.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the Key West Bight Management District Board approved a compromise lease: rather than picking between "bar" or "studio," the two competing bidders merged into a joint venture that will keep Shrimpboat Sound as a working recording studio while also adding a margarita bar, museum and retail space — with 100% of studio profits going to Key West charities. The plan has preliminary backing from Mac McAnally, Kenny Chesney, Zac Brown Band, Coral Reefer Band members, and members of the Buffett family.

updates.headline: A second verse for Jimmy Buffett's studio: Key West approves hybrid studio-bar lease
updates.summary: The Key West Bight Management District Board agreed on a new lease combining proposals from Spottswood Management and Shrimpboat Sound Key West LLC. The 1,447-square-foot waterfront building will operate as a working recording studio with museum tours, a margarita bar, retail, and live entertainment, with 100% of recording studio profits pledged to Key West charities.

updates.headline: Shrimpboat Sound Studio 'A Key West treasure continues'
updates.summary: Follow-up piece detailing the Bernsen-Spottswood merged concept, which preserves the studio's recording function (including its famed vintage 1969 Neve console heritage) alongside museum tours, a frozen drink bar, retail, and live entertainment in the 1,500-square-foot building.

items.title: Jersey Mike's pursuing $12B IPO
items.pov_summary: Jersey Mike's is working with Morgan Stanley and JP Morgan on an IPO at a $12B valuation after Blackstone bought them for $8B last year. Big payday coming. TC jokes about whether it affects his cache of Shore Points — he still hasn't exhausted them from last year.
items.quote: Blackstone bought them for like eight billion last year and they're seeking to value the IPO at 12 billion.
items.updates_summary: Little has moved publicly since the episode aired. No S-1 filing, pricing, or firm launch date has been reported between Feb 5 and April 18, 2026. A Feb 8 recap piece reiterated the $12B target valuation and tied the IPO to the company's 2026 international expansion plans (including 400 UK/Ireland stores) and continued U.S. growth.

updates.headline: Changes Coming To Jersey Mike's In 2026
updates.summary: Post-episode recap confirming Jersey Mike's is targeting a $12B valuation and a $1B raise, and framing the IPO as fuel for 2026 menu innovation, UK/Ireland expansion (400 stores), and continued U.S. growth toward a stated runway of 8,000 domestic locations.

items.title: Sinners sets record with Oscar nominations
items.pov_summary: DJ was surprised Sinners cleaned up on nominations to the tune of setting the all-time record. He knew it'd be a juggernaut but not that big. Relieved Wicked for Good and Avatar missed Best Picture so he didn't have to watch them.
items.status: heading into Oscars season, hosts pumped
items.quote: I think we dodged a couple big big bullets with uh Wicked and uh or Wicked for Good, I should say, and Avatar.
items.updates_summary: At the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026, Sinners won 4 of its record 16 nominations, losing Best Picture to One Battle After Another (which won 6). Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor, Ryan Coogler won Best Original Screenplay, Ludwig Göransson won Best Score, and Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history as the first woman to win Best Cinematography — but with 12 losses, Sinners now holds the record for most Oscar losses for a single film.

updates.headline: Sinners wins 4 Oscars, including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan
updates.summary: Sinners took home Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), Best Original Screenplay (Ryan Coogler), Best Cinematography and Best Original Score, while One Battle After Another won Best Picture and 5 other awards.

updates.headline: Autumn Durald Arkapaw becomes first woman to win Best Cinematography
updates.summary: Sinners DP Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history as the first woman — and first Black and Asian woman — to win the Best Cinematography Oscar, considered an upset over One Battle's Michael Bauman.

updates.headline: Sinners sets unexpected record: most Oscar losses in history
updates.summary: With only 4 wins out of 16 nominations, Sinners ended up setting an unexpected record with 12 losses — the most of any film in Oscars history, breaking the previous mark of 11 shared by five films including The Color Purple and The Power of the Dog.

updates.headline: THR postmortem: How One Battle beat Sinners
updates.summary: Scott Feinberg analyzed that Sinners' loss in Best Supporting Actress (Wunmi Mosaku), the inaugural Best Casting category, and Best Supporting Actor (Delroy Lindo) signaled early that One Battle After Another would hold on for Best Picture despite Sinners' SAG ensemble win.

items.title: Uber Eats is ruining restaurants
items.pov_summary: A piece TC sent DJ claims three of every four restaurant orders aren't eaten in the restaurant. The hosts find this wild and bleak. Another piece says Uber Eats drivers spend their earnings on Uber Eats — a closed loop system. They'd rather sit at the bar, have one beer, and grab takeout like normal humans.
items.status: ongoing cultural decline, hosts want to read more
items.quote: Three out of every four orders uh at restaurants these days are not eaten in a restaurant. That's so [ __ ] wild.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the conversation around delivery apps harming restaurants has continued with substantive new reporting. Wharton published research arguing that DoorDash and Uber Eats are indeed intensifying competition and forcing closures, Restaurant Business published a new Market Leader report showing 53% of operators are trying to reduce their reliance on third-party delivery, and Uber Eats announced it is raising marketplace fees for restaurants — adding more weight to the "closed loop" and margin-squeeze critique TC and DJ discussed.

updates.headline: Wharton: Delivery apps are hurting restaurants and driving closures
updates.summary: New research from Wharton concluded that <cite index="11-5,11-6">apps like DoorDash and Uber Eats intensify competition while imposing fees that shrink profit margins, making survival harder for many establishments, with restaurants especially at risk of closure when delivery platforms enter highly competitive areas</cite>.

updates.headline: Uber Eats raises marketplace fees on restaurants
updates.summary: Uber Eats announced it would increase marketplace fees across most tiers, with <cite index="10-1">some restaurants seeing rates go up by 5% for delivery, while all merchants see commissions for pickup orders increase by 1%</cite>. The company framed it as necessary to cover rising operating costs.

updates.headline: Market Leader report: Over half of operators trying to cut reliance on Uber Eats/DoorDash
updates.summary: A new Restaurant Business/Nation's Restaurant News survey found <cite index="19-9,19-10">more than half (53%) of operators said they are trying to reduce their reliance on third-party delivery, based on a survey of nearly 450 operators from across segments</cite>, even as <cite index="19-12,19-13">customers spent more than $72 billion on DoorDash through the first three quarters of 2025 and Uber Eats processed more than $65 billion in orders during that time</cite>.

updates.headline: Industry pushback: Delivery may not actually be killing dine-in
updates.summary: Responding directly to The Atlantic piece cited on the show, Restaurant Business argued the picture is more nuanced, noting major chain CEOs claim <cite index="12-1,12-2,12-3">"very little overlap between our dine-in guests and our delivery guests" (Olive Garden), Starbucks' delivery business "looks to be highly incremental," and First Watch's CEO saying delivery occasions are "incremental, not a replacement for in-restaurant dining visits"</cite> — though it conceded delivery still carries up to 30% commissions that hurt operators.

items.title: Melania biopic bombs on opening weekend
items.pov_summary: The Melania movie is getting savaged by critics invoking Birth of a Nation, Goebbels and Leni Riefenstahl — not names you want associated with your life story. The theater was selling commemorative Melania buckets that weren't moving, and a Florida Republican was set up in the lobby taking no questions.
items.quote: I don't think those are the I don't think that's the crew you're you're trying to invoke from critics.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the Melania doc continued its commercial collapse — plunging 67% in its second weekend, losing hundreds of theaters, and effectively vanishing from box-office charts by early March with a worldwide total of roughly $16.7M against a $75M combined acquisition-and-marketing spend. Amazon MGM moved the film to Prime Video on March 9, 2026, while additional controversies piled up, including director Brett Ratner's renewed mentions in the Epstein files and accusations from the film's own crew and unlicensed music use.

updates.headline: 'Melania' Drops 67% in Second Weekend as 'Send Help' Tops Super Bowl Box Office
updates.summary: The documentary plunged to No. 9 with $2.37 million from 2,003 theaters, a 67% decline. Amazon MGM issued an unusual statement framing the theatrical run as a 'first step' toward its Prime Video debut.

updates.headline: 'Melania' Loses Money in Theaters, But Box Office Was Never the Point
updates.summary: Variety reports Amazon MGM is unlikely to recoup its spend, fueling speculation that the $40M acquisition — roughly $25M above Disney's next-best offer — was an attempt to curry favor with the Trump administration rather than a genuine commercial bet.

updates.headline: Melania Film Struggles at Box Office, Loses Theaters
updates.summary: The film lost 799 theaters in a single week, leaving only 1,204 still playing it, with a Tuesday take of just $132,181 and a domestic total of $15.7M.

updates.headline: 'Melania' Vanishes From Box Office Charts After Just 4 Weeks
updates.summary: The doc effectively disappeared from the domestic charts despite still showing in cinemas, and was pulled from South Africa entirely while flopping in the UK. The piece also notes director Brett Ratner resurfaced in the latest Epstein files, the crew publicly slammed the film, and filmmakers were accused of using musical numbers without permission.

updates.headline: Melania Gets Early Prime Video Streaming Release Date of March 9
updates.summary: After just over a month in theaters, Amazon moved the bomb to streaming. Rotten Tomatoes also had to publicly defend the film's 98% audience score against manipulation accusations given the chasm with its 11% critics score.

items.title: Silicon Valley AI scanning millions of books
items.pov_summary: A Washington Post piece detailed how Silicon Valley built AI by buying, scanning and discarding millions of books. Hosts find it bleak. At least there's one good AI use case — AI sorting through trash.
items.quote: Yeah, that's pretty pretty bleak stuff.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the fallout from the Washington Post report on Anthropic's "Project Panama" has continued to play out in court. The $1.5 billion Bartz v. Anthropic class-action settlement entered its final phase—the claims deadline passed on March 30, 2026, with a final approval hearing set for May 14, 2026—while music publishers (UMG, Concord, BMG) filed a new ~$3 billion lawsuit alleging "mass torrenting" of lyrics and compositions. Nearly 10,000 authors also staged a protest titled "Don't Steal This Book" at the London Book Fair in March 2026.

updates.headline: Anthropic faces three simultaneous legal fronts as settlement deadline nears
updates.summary: As of late March 2026, Anthropic was navigating the final claim phase of the $1.5B Bartz settlement (deadline March 30), a newly filed ~$3B music publisher lawsuit, and a suit against the U.S. Department of Defense over a 'supply-chain risk' designation blocking federal use of Claude.

updates.headline: Bartz v. Anthropic settlement claim deadline passes; final approval hearing set for May 14
updates.summary: The claims deadline was March 30, 2026, and the final fairness hearing before Judge Alsup is scheduled for May 14, 2026, with payment disbursement expected to begin in June 2026 or later.

updates.headline: Music publishers expand suit against Anthropic to ~$3 billion over alleged torrenting
updates.summary: A coalition led by Universal Music Group and Concord filed claims alleging Anthropic illegally downloaded over 20,000 copyrighted songs—including sheet music and lyrics—to train Claude, with founders Dario Amodei and Benjamin Mann individually named.

updates.headline: 'Don't Steal This Book': 10,000 authors publish empty novel protesting AI training
updates.summary: Distributed at the London Book Fair in March 2026, nearly 10,000 authors published a blank book to protest AI companies scraping copyrighted works without permission—a direct cultural response to the revelations about Project Panama.

updates.headline: ISBNDB analysis: Anthropic's physical book scanning operation ruled legal
updates.summary: A retrospective analysis argues Judge Alsup's ruling combined the First-Sale Doctrine with transformative fair use (drawing on Authors Guild v. Google) to create a 'compliant path' for destructive scanning of lawfully purchased books—while noting the PR damage Anthropic suffered from the unsealed discovery documents.

items.title: SEC probes Sable Offshore, Mickelson tie
items.pov_summary: Phil Mickelson-touted Sable Offshore is being probed by the SEC for insider trading per Bloomberg/Hunterbrook. TC notes how hard it is to even GET investigated for insider trading in the current environment — you have to really be putting the pedal to the metal.
items.quote: Do you know how hard it is right now to get investigated for insider trading on anything in this environment?
items.updates_summary: Shortly before the episode aired, Sable disclosed in a Feb. 3 regulatory filing that both the SEC and SDNY had served subpoenas (dated Dec. 2, 2025) related to the Hunterbrook allegations involving Mickelson — confirming the federal probe the hosts discussed. Since the episode, there appears to be a March 2026 civil/shareholder suit (filed in federal court) but no public resolution of the SEC probe or new charges against Mickelson.

updates.headline: Feds Subpoena Sable Offshore Over Insider Information
updates.summary: Sable's Feb. 3 filing confirmed SEC and SDNY subpoenas tied to the Hunterbrook report, and warned that if an enforcement action is pursued, the SEC could impose civil penalties and sanctions. The filing also disclosed CalGEM nearly doubled a required decommissioning bond to $57 million, adding to Sable's mounting legal challenges.

updates.headline: Sable Probed by SEC on Insider Information
updates.summary: Bloomberg reported the subpoenas were actually received on Dec. 2, 2025 — meaning federal investigators moved on the Hunterbrook allegations within roughly a month — and that Sable had formed a special committee in the immediate aftermath of the report.

updates.headline: Shareholder suit filed against Sable Offshore
updates.summary: A complaint against Sable surfaced on Courthouse News in March 2026, suggesting investor litigation is piling on top of the federal probe, though details of the allegations were not available in search previews.

items.title: Darius McCollum, subway hijacker folk hero
items.pov_summary: TC is obsessed with Darius McCollum, a New Yorker with Asperger's who has been arrested 32 times for impersonating MTA employees and running subway and bus routes perfectly on time. Tragic irony — the MTA won't hire him because of his record, a record he only has because he loves trains. Needs to be a movie.
items.quote: The only man who cares enough to make the transit system run on time is the only one that they won't let drive.
items.updates_summary: Little substantive news since the episode. A February 18, 2026 feature revisited McCollum's story, noting he awaits trial in a 2012-related case with a potential 15-year sentence, but no major new arrests, court rulings, or film announcements have surfaced in the window between the episode and today.

updates.headline: Darius McCollum awaits trial with risk of 15 years behind bars
updates.summary: A retrospective feature recounts McCollum's decades of impersonating MTA workers and notes that, following a 2012 case, he is awaiting judgment with a potential 15-year sentence. It also details how during prior incarcerations he helped the government identify security vulnerabilities in the NYC transit system.

items.title: Epstein files dump captures hosts' attention
items.pov_summary: DJ hates that part of his brain but can't stop scrolling the Epstein email dumps — who's implicated, Mamdani's dad, Elon being too cringe to visit the island, actors groveling for Tom Cruise intros. TC notes Mr. Unlimited (Mickelson) denied involvement, while Soly notably had 'no comment.'
items.status: ongoing drip of revelations, hosts rubbernecking
items.quote: God, I hate this part of my brain, TC. It's pretty reptilian stuff, but... it's kind of hard not to stop and see like who was implicated.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the Epstein files saga has produced major political fallout: Larry Summers resigned all Harvard roles, Kathryn Ruemmler stepped down from Goldman Sachs, Prince Andrew (Mountbatten-Windsor) was arrested in the UK, and Trump fired AG Pam Bondi in April over her handling of the files. NPR also reported that DOJ appears to have withheld documents relating to allegations against Trump, while Senate Finance ramped up a probe of Leon Black. No US arrests have resulted from the file release to date.

updates.headline: Prince Andrew arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office
updates.summary: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested in the UK over allegedly sharing confidential trade-envoy documents with Epstein, stemming from the January 30 file release. He denies wrongdoing and has not been formally charged.

updates.headline: Larry Summers resigns all Harvard roles over Epstein ties
updates.summary: Following emails showing Summers sought Epstein's dating advice about a mentee, Harvard announced he would resign from all academic and faculty roles by year-end. His wife's PBS show 'Poetry in America' was also cancelled after it emerged Epstein helped fund it.

updates.headline: NPR: DOJ withheld or removed Epstein files pages involving Trump
updates.summary: Reporter Stephen Fowler found a DOJ PowerPoint listed Trump atop 'prominent names' with two accusations, including a salacious claim of sexual abuse of a minor — but supporting FBI interview material was missing from the public release.

updates.headline: Senate Finance's Wyden presses Leon Black over new Epstein revelations
updates.summary: Wyden's probe uncovered that $10M Black paid Epstein was routed through a sham charity, and emails suggest Black used Epstein as a middleman to pay millions to women — raising federal tax-law questions.

updates.headline: Trump fires AG Pam Bondi over Epstein files handling
updates.summary: Trump removed Bondi following bipartisan criticism of her handling of the file release; Todd Blanche became acting AG and reaffirmed DOJ had met its legal obligations.

updates.headline: NPR: No US arrests despite massive file release
updates.summary: More than two months after the January 30 release, DOJ has brought no new charges. Legal experts cite a lack of prosecutable evidence beyond Epstein and Maxwell; fallout has been limited to resignations and UK arrests (Andrew, Mandelson).

updates.headline: Kathryn Ruemmler resigns as Goldman Sachs chief legal officer
updates.summary: Less than two weeks after January files showed Ruemmler referring to Epstein as 'Uncle Jeffrey' and receiving expensive gifts, she resigned from Goldman Sachs, effective June.

updates.headline: Melania Trump denies being an Epstein victim
updates.summary: Melania Trump publicly denied being a victim of Epstein after references to her surfaced in the files.

items.title: WWI shell in patient's ass evacuates French hospital
items.pov_summary: A French hospital had to be evacuated after a live 8-inch WWI artillery shell was found lodged in a patient. The bomb squad had to extract it. Of all the things you could put in there.
items.quote: Of all of all the things you can put in there.
items.updates_summary: Post-episode updates: The Toulouse public prosecutor confirmed that no charges will be filed against the 24-year-old patient, since the WWI shell turned out to be demilitarized/inert and did not constitute a criminal offense under France's weapons laws. The patient was reported stable and recovering under medical observation.

updates.headline: No charges for Frenchman in WWI-shell hospital incident; munition ruled inert
updates.summary: The Toulouse public prosecutor confirmed no charges would be filed after explosives experts determined the WWI shell had been demilitarized and posed no detonation risk. The 24-year-old patient's condition stabilized and he remained under observation.

items.title: Elephant attacks sweep rural India
items.pov_summary: A spate of elephant attacks in India, possibly driven by severe deforestation leaving elephants nowhere to go. Bleak. Hosts also tracking bull shark attacks in Sydney after heavy rain muddied the water.
items.quote: Maybe the the product of some some like severe deforestation. Basically, it sounds like kind of this elephant maybe has nowhere to go.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb 5 episode, elephant attacks in rural India have continued, with a fatal farmer trampling in Karnataka's Belur in April and new research/policy commentary published on the worsening human-elephant conflict. A March Mongabay piece reported a counterintuitive finding that organized crop-guarding squads in Assam were associated with higher elephant mortality, and a Frontiers study documented nearly 7,868 human fatalities from elephant encounters over 16 years.

updates.headline: Farmer killed in wild elephant attack in Karnataka's Belur as villagers protest
updates.summary: A 65-year-old farmer named Rajashetty was trampled by a wild elephant hiding in a bush while working on his farmland in Belur, prompting village protests over ongoing human-elephant conflict in Karnataka.

updates.headline: Study finds counterintuitive rise in elephant deaths after organised monitoring
updates.summary: A new study of two decades of crop-guarding in Assam's Sonitpur district found villages with active anti-depredation squads recorded two to three times more elephant deaths, though researchers note this may reflect that squads were deployed in the worst-affected areas to begin with.

updates.headline: New national study: 7,868 human deaths from elephants in India over 16 years
updates.summary: A peer-reviewed analysis of 2009-2024 data found nearly 500 human deaths annually, with Odisha, Jharkhand, West Bengal and Assam accounting for about 70% of incidents, and 1,653 elephants killed by electrocution, trains, poaching and poisoning over the same period.

updates.headline: Policy briefing flags rising human-elephant conflict in Jharkhand and West Bengal
updates.summary: A current-affairs briefing reiterated that India holds ~60% of global Asian elephants and that conflict causes about 500 human deaths annually, pointing to habitat fragmentation, infrastructure blocking migratory corridors, and climate variability as primary drivers.

items.title: Chinese fishing fleet forms wall near Taiwan
items.pov_summary: TC continues monitoring the Chinese fishing fleet — overfishing in Peruvian and Ecuadorian waters with transponders off, and apparently testing out forming an impassable wall around Taiwan. Plus Chinese generals keep getting disappeared, creating a big power vacuum in the military.
items.quote: They they just formed a big wall like in around Taiwan basically like you couldn't get through it.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the Chinese military purge has escalated dramatically — at the March 2026 "Two Sessions," the Central Military Commission's top table was reduced to just Xi Jinping and one vice chairman, and reporting revealed Xi has now purged 29 of 35 generals he personally promoted. Zhang Youxia, Xi's sworn brother and former second-in-command, is now accused of leaking nuclear secrets to the U.S. Coverage of the fishing-fleet "wall" tactic has continued, with analysts framing the December/January formations as a rehearsal for an A2/AD-style blockade of Taiwan.

updates.headline: China's 2026 defense budget rises 7% as CMC shrinks to just two members
updates.summary: At the March 5 National People's Congress, China announced a 7% defense budget hike to ~$277B. Notably, the CMC's top table was manned by only Xi Jinping and vice chairman Zhang Shengmin, with analysis suggesting 41 of 47 generals from 2022 have been potentially purged.

updates.headline: PLA generals conspicuously absent from 2026 Two Sessions
updates.summary: Only four active-duty full generals appeared at Xi's March 7 meeting with military delegates. Full generals in the delegation dropped from 26 in 2023 to just six in 2026, and three more senior officers had their CPPCC memberships revoked in March.

updates.headline: Xi accuses sworn brother Zhang Youxia of leaking nuclear secrets to US
updates.summary: Per CNN reporting cited in the piece, Zhang Youxia — Xi's longtime ally and former first vice chairman of the CMC — is now accused of passing core nuclear weapons technical data to the United States. Xi has purged 29 of 35 generals he personally promoted.

updates.headline: CSIS database: over 100 PLA senior officers purged since 2022
updates.summary: A new CSIS dataset documents 101 confirmed or potential purges, including 46 general officers who went missing in 2025 alone based on absences at key events. 14 generals and 5 lieutenant generals were conspicuously absent from the October 2025 fourth plenum.

updates.headline: China Leadership Monitor: Zhang and Liu purges may mark the peak
updates.summary: Analysis frames the January removals of Zhang Youxia and Liu Zhenli as potentially the peak of a purge wave begun in mid-2023, which has extended well beyond ground forces into the navy and Rocket Force.

updates.headline: Analyst: Chinese fishing-fleet 'walls' are A2/AD rehearsal for Taiwan blockade
updates.summary: Follow-up analysis argues the December/January wall formations were a winter-season rehearsal of Anti-Access/Area-Denial tactics, integrating the maritime militia with the PLA Navy and Coast Guard to obstruct commercial shipping and contest access around Taiwan.

items.title: Iranian state TV hacked with protest footage
items.pov_summary: Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting got hacked and protest footage aired on state TV. DJ loves a good V for Vendetta-style hack — when hackers do something with a wink of humor while advancing a cause, that's throwback stuff.
items.quote: I like when the hackers are doing some stuff that has a little bit of a a wink of humor and advancing their cause.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the aftermath of the state TV hack has been eclipsed by escalation: a U.S.-Iran conflict reportedly began on Feb. 28, 2026, and the DOJ later seized four domains tied to Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security used for "faketivist" psychological operations and retaliatory hacks. Iran also announced sweeping arrests of alleged Israeli spies across multiple provinces in March 2026, part of an internal crackdown following the protests and hacking incidents. No public attribution for the January state TV hijack itself appears to have been confirmed.

updates.headline: Iran says it arrested dozens accused of spying for Israel in new internal crackdown
updates.summary: Iranian authorities announced arrests across several provinces of suspects accused of sharing locations of military sites and infrastructure with Israel, part of a broader crackdown following the protest wave and cyberattacks on state institutions.

updates.headline: Justice Department seizes domains tied to Iranian MOIS hacking and psychological operations
updates.summary: DOJ announced the seizure of four domains (including Handala-Hack.to) used by Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security for 'faketivist' operations, noting that after the U.S.-Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, 2026, the domains published personal data of targeted individuals and claimed credit for a destructive malware attack on a U.S. medical technologies firm.

items.title: Bobi Wine abduction rumors in Uganda
items.pov_summary: Rumors swirling that Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine was abducted — or his wife, or his mom. TC can't pin down reliable info but tracking closely.
items.quote: I'm tracking the heck out of this one. I can't get any good info though.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, Bobi Wine resurfaced from hiding and fled Uganda. He announced his departure on March 14, 2026, and appeared publicly in Washington, DC on March 18, 2026, meeting with US lawmakers on Capitol Hill to push for sanctions against Museveni and his son Muhoozi Kainerugaba. Reports indicate he escaped via Rwanda with assistance from sympathetic security officials, and he has signaled intentions to eventually return home.

updates.headline: Bobi Wine announces temporary departure from Uganda
updates.summary: After two months in hiding, Wine announced via video on X that he had left Uganda to meet global allies abroad and push for international sanctions, while vowing to return.

updates.headline: Bobi Wine resurfaces in Washington, DC
updates.summary: Wine appeared publicly on Capitol Hill after nearly two months in hiding, highlighting election-related human rights violations and calling for sanctions against Museveni and Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba.

updates.headline: Details emerge about Bobi Wine's escape route from Uganda
updates.summary: Sources indicate Wine crossed by road via the Gatuna border into Rwanda, then flew through the Middle East and the UK before reaching New Jersey. Regime insiders reportedly told Museveni of his whereabouts but the president chose not to act, suggesting internal factional tensions.

updates.headline: Bobi Wine speaks from US exile, signals return to Uganda
updates.summary: In an exclusive interview, Wine said he fled to save his life but intends to return to Uganda. He alleged Muhoozi issued threats to 'behead' him and said members of the military and police who felt oppressed helped him escape.

items.title: Malawi presidential dog custody battle
items.pov_summary: Outgoing Malawi president trained dogs to sniff out intruders AND witchcraft. New president says they're civic property; old president says they're his. Police botched the warrant by calling them German shepherds when none actually are. One guy claims to be a prophet. Lot of questions, few answers.
items.quote: He said basically don't expect any help from above and try to get these dogs because I think the big man's on his side.
items.updates_summary: The dog custody case itself hasn't produced a definitive public ruling since the episode, but Chakwera's situation has escalated dramatically on other fronts. In April 2026 he took up a role as Commonwealth Special Envoy to Tanzania while simultaneously facing an "imminent arrest" warning back home over the Greenbelt and Fertilizergate corruption scandals, with several of his former ministers and aides already in custody.

updates.headline: Chakwera arrest reportedly imminent over Greenbelt and Fertilizergate scandals
updates.summary: Investigators are reportedly probing Chakwera over an alleged K39 billion looted from the Greenbelt Authority, with several of his former ministers and aides already arrested. The ACB claims some stolen funds financed the 2025 election campaign.

updates.headline: Chakwera deployed as Commonwealth Special Envoy to Tanzania
updates.summary: Chakwera arrived in Dar es Salaam on April 8 as Commonwealth Special Envoy and was permitted to meet detained Tanzanian opposition leader Tundu Lissu at Ukonga Maximum Security Prison on April 15, a notable post-presidency role amid his domestic troubles.

updates.headline: Chakwera warns 'the fight you engage in fights back' in Euronews interview
updates.summary: In his first major international interview since leaving office, Chakwera defended his anti-corruption record and attributed delays in appointing an ACB director to judicial processes, without directly addressing the dog saga.

updates.headline: MCP insurgent faction demands Chakwera step down as party president
updates.summary: A pressure group within Chakwera's own Malawi Congress Party publicly demanded he relinquish the party presidency, accusing him of corruption and mismanagement — signaling his post-election political capital is collapsing domestically.

items.title: Burkina Faso junta bans political parties
items.pov_summary: Burkina Faso's military junta has banned all political parties after canceling elections post-2022 coup. Hosts (and Randy) continue to be in awe of these guys' aesthetic. The apparent governing philosophy: the thing causing fighting is having two different sides — what if we were all on the same side?
items.quote: The thing that's causing all the fighting is having two different sides. What if we were just all on the same side?
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, Burkina Faso's parliament formally codified the party ban into law, and Captain Ibrahim Traoré escalated his rhetoric — telling citizens in an April 3 state TV interview to "forget about democracy," saying "democracy kills." A new Human Rights Watch report also found government forces and allies have killed more than twice as many civilians as Islamist militants since 2023.

updates.headline: Burkina Faso's transitional parliament approves bill banning all political parties
updates.summary: Lawmakers unanimously adopted a bill repealing the charter of political parties and laws on party financing and opposition status, formalizing the January decree into statute. The UN rights chief had called days earlier for the junta to reverse course.

updates.headline: Traoré tells citizens to 'forget democracy,' says 'democracy kills'
updates.summary: In a lengthy state TV interview, Traoré explicitly rejected elections and democratic rule, citing Libya as a cautionary tale. He confirmed the government is focused on other priorities, signaling indefinite military rule.

updates.headline: HRW: Burkina Faso military killed more than twice as many civilians as jihadists since 2023
updates.summary: A new Human Rights Watch report released the same day as Traoré's interview documented state forces and allies killing civilians at more than double the rate of Islamist militants. The government did not respond to requests for comment.

updates.headline: Traoré announces arrests of alleged foreign-backed 'traitors'
updates.summary: Traoré said several people had been detained for allegedly working through diplomatic missions to destabilize the country, continuing the crackdown on perceived opposition.

items.title: Novelty politicians running for Congress
items.pov_summary: Bob Huggins is running for Congress in West Virginia. DJ compares these celebrity political runs to Tiger being best-positioned to fix the PGA Tour — technically possible but unlikely to actually work out. Tommy Tuberville and Marc Teixeira in Texas cited as examples.
items.quote: I think the odds are that it's a little bit like Tiger being the best one to fix the uh the PJ tours... you're the right person for both of these totally unrelated jobs. Uh best of luck.
items.updates_summary: The biggest development since the episode is that Mark Teixeira decisively won the Republican primary for Texas' 21st Congressional District on March 3, clearing the 50% threshold and avoiding a runoff. He landed a Trump endorsement shortly after the episode aired and will face Democrat Kristin Hook in November. No notable updates surfaced on Bob Huggins' rumored West Virginia congressional run.

updates.headline: Mark Teixeira lands Trump endorsement in bid for Congress
updates.summary: Just days after the episode, Teixeira picked up Donald Trump's endorsement in the TX-21 GOP primary. <cite index="20-20,20-21,20-22">He had loaned $2.5 million of his own money to the campaign and was also endorsed by Gov. Greg Abbott and nine of the 25 Republicans from Texas' congressional delegation.</cite>

updates.headline: Teixeira wins TX-21 GOP primary outright, avoids runoff
updates.summary: <cite index="15-8,15-9,15-10,15-11">Teixeira beat out 11 contenders in the Republican primary, winning with 60.9% of the vote. Because Texas requires a candidate to receive more than 50% to avoid a runoff, the crowded field made a first-round victory a significant hurdle, but Teixeira cleared it and clinched the nomination outright.</cite>

updates.headline: Teixeira to face Democrat Kristin Hook in November
updates.summary: <cite index="17-2,17-3,17-4">The general election will pit Teixeira against Democrat Kristin Hook. Teixeira is running on a platform that includes a hardline approach to immigration, support for gun rights, and banning transgender athletes from competing in school sports.</cite> <cite index="17-6">Hook previously worked as a biological scientist at the National Institutes of Health.</cite>

items.title: Weird NHL arenas oral history
items.pov_summary: The Athletic's Rob Rossi piece on quirky old NHL arenas was a great read for the hosts — Boston's smaller ice shaping roster decisions, no glass behind the Montreal benches, Chicago Stadium's fog and massive organ, Hartford Whalers in a shopping mall. DJ recommends a follow-up Architectural Digest video on baseball stadium history.
items.quote: I think hockey is like at the top of the list for just having weird stuff historically.
items.updates_summary: No meaningful news developments on The Athletic's quirky NHL arenas oral history piece surfaced between the episode air date (Feb 5, 2026) and today. The story remains an evergreen nostalgia piece, and searches didn't turn up follow-up reporting, related new articles from Rob Rossi, or news tied to the specific arenas discussed (Boston Garden, Chicago Stadium, Hartford Civic Center).

items.title: Hotels ditching proper bathroom doors
items.pov_summary: Wall Street Journal trend piece: hotels are getting rid of proper bathroom and shower doors under the guise of 'modern minimalism.' Hosts say it's just cheap and miserable.
items.quote: All of it kind of in the in the under the guise of like modern minimalism. And it's just it's just miserable.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb 5 episode, the story has continued to get traction in travel media — notably eTurboNews and Travel Noire expanded on the WSJ piece, highlighting the guest backlash and specifically naming chains (1 Hotel, AC Hotel, Aloft, Hyatt Centric) with doorless or semi-private bathrooms. The "Bring Back Doors" grassroots database run by Sadie Lowell has grown as a go-to resource being cited by multiple outlets, but there's no sign hotels are reversing course.

updates.headline: Hotels ditch bathroom doors: The weird trend annoying guests
updates.summary: Follow-up to the WSJ piece quoting NYU hospitality expert Bjorn Henson saying some hotels have dismantled the bathroom entirely and integrated it into the room, and highlighting the Bring Back Doors website as a civic resource.

updates.headline: Hotels Without Bathroom Doors: Design Trend Guests Hate
updates.summary: Names specific offending properties — including Hyatt Centric South Beach Miami, 1 Hotel Brooklyn Bridge, 1 Hotel Central Park, AC Hotel Denver Downtown, and Aloft Playa del Carmen — and calls for hotels to disclose bathroom layouts at booking.

updates.headline: No Door, No Privacy: Travelers Are Fed Up With Hotels' Open Bathroom Trend
updates.summary: Frames the issue as a 'bathroom privacy crisis,' reports travelers are now calling hotels in advance to confirm doors exist, and spotlights the Bring Back Doors cataloging project.

items.title: Airplane sandwich assembly mom goes viral
items.pov_summary: Viral video of a mom turning tray tables into a sandwich-making station for a family of six on a 5-hour NY-to-Colorado flight. DJ says extremely look-at-me — make the sandwiches ahead of time and wrap them. Hosts appreciate the hustle and quality of ingredients (good bread, lots of meat and cheese) but question whether it was staged.
items.quote: I don't know why we can't make these sandwiches ahead of time. Wrap them up... it did look a little Look at me.
items.updates_summary: Follow-up coverage after the episode identified the "sandwich mom" as 71-year-old Shelly Murphy of Morgan, Utah, and clarified the flight was Salt Lake City to Boston (not NY to Colorado as discussed on the pod). Coverage continued into mid-February with additional outlets weighing in on the hygiene/etiquette debate, but no indication the video was staged.

updates.headline: Mom, 71, Goes Viral After Making Elaborate Sandwiches for Her Family Mid-Flight
updates.summary: Identifies the mom as Shelly Murphy, 71, of Morgan, Utah, and notes the family framed it as a familiar act of care rather than a stunt — pushing back slightly on the 'staged' theory.

updates.headline: Mom Goes Viral for Making Full Sandwiches on a Plane & People Are Divided
updates.summary: Later write-up noting the TikTok creator @ileshadepasquale was one of the adult children being fed, and that commenters remained split between praising the effort and calling out hygiene concerns.

updates.headline: Mom goes viral for turning entire airplane row into a 'sandwich shop'
updates.summary: Mid-April follow-up piece revisiting the viral moment, with a working mom/writer arguing the setup was excessive given the kids were grown adults and raising tray-table cleanliness concerns citing flight attendant Sue Fogwell.

items.title: Things That Are Sick nominations update
items.pov_summary: Housekeeping on nominations: Northern Lights is a rubber-stamp inclusion. Pond nominated whales — DJ wants specificity (orcas yes, beluga yes, northern right whale maybe not). Sign language is a great nom. Hosts note too many nominations lean systems/science and want to zag for homework.
items.quote: I think it's kind of a again a bit like say all whales... I think there's some that are are pretty uninteresting. So that's maybe a hold for me at the moment.
items.updates_summary: This is an internal podcast running bit rather than an external news topic, so there's no outside news coverage. However, the hosts continued developing the list in a subsequent episode — TrapDraw 383 (Feb 19, 2026) featured further additions to Things That Are Sick during TC and DJ's chop session.

updates.headline: TrapDraw 383: Chop Session with D.J. Pie adds more Things That Are Sick nominations
updates.summary: In the follow-up episode, TC joined DJ from a family vacation and the pair added further nominations to the Things That Are Sick list alongside airline travel updates and the monitoring list.

items.title: American Airlines earnings look bleak
items.pov_summary: American Airlines earnings were bad. DJ thinks Big Game Bob Isom's days in the lead chair are numbered. Silver lining: they announced flights to Venezuela so Soly can use his Advantage points to visit Caracas.
items.quote: I don't know how much longer big game Bob Ism is going to be in the the lead chair there.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, pressure on CEO Robert Isom has intensified — the APFA flight attendants union held a historic first-ever no-confidence vote against him on Feb. 9, with the pilots union also critical. Meanwhile, American set a firm April 30, 2026 start date for its Miami–Caracas route (operated by Envoy Air on an Embraer E175), making it the first U.S. carrier back to Venezuela since 2019.

updates.headline: APFA flight attendants hold historic first no-confidence vote against CEO Robert Isom
updates.summary: The Association of Professional Flight Attendants, representing 28,000 American cabin crew, issued a unanimous no-confidence vote — the first in the union's 50-year history — citing Winter Storm Fern meltdown, 0.3% profit sharing, and Isom's executive compensation increases despite poor performance.

updates.headline: Pressure mounts on Isom as unions question leadership
updates.summary: Pilot and flight attendant unions publicly called Isom's leadership into question after the airline trailed Delta and United by wide margins, with Isom telling 6,000 leaders at a Globe Life Field conference that 2026 'has to be different.'

updates.headline: Fortune op-ed defends Isom as 'misunderstood'
updates.summary: Yale's Jeffrey Sonnenfeld published a contrarian defense arguing Isom has navigated unique headwinds — Boeing delivery disruptions and Winter Storm Fern (9,000+ cancellations, $150–200M revenue hit) — that no competitor faced.

updates.headline: American sets April 30 launch date for Miami–Caracas service
updates.summary: American confirmed daily MIA–CCS flights starting 'as soon as April 30,' operated by Envoy Air with a 76-seat Embraer E175 — the first scheduled nonstop U.S.–Venezuela service since 2019. Soly's Caracas trip is imminent.

updates.headline: DOT grants Envoy two-year exemption for Venezuela service
updates.summary: The U.S. Transportation Department in March granted Envoy Air a two-year exemption to operate scheduled passenger, cargo and mail service between Miami and both Caracas and Maracaibo, clearing the path for the April 30 launch.

items.title: John DeMarc SNY firing scandal
items.pov_summary: DJ Pie offers a mealy-mouthed mea culpa for previously glazing Mets broadcaster John DeMarc after a listener/former SNY employee alleges he was fired for an affair with a cameraman's daughter. DJ stands by loving the cinematic art from afar but concedes the fake-signature Twitter exit note was a cringy look. More as it develops.
items.status: allegations surfacing, hosts deferring to local SME perspective
items.quote: If what this listener is saying is true, then the way he went out with a... souped-up notes app... for immediate release with a fake signature on the bottom is tough, man.
items.updates_summary: No substantive news developments have emerged since the episode aired on Feb 20, 2026 regarding the alleged scandal behind John DeMarsico's SNY departure. One tangential piece from Giant Freakin Robot briefly referenced the "controversial rumors" while noting the author could not find reputable sources to confirm them, and reiterated that the official reason remains "creative differences." No new reporting, lawsuits, or confirmed statements from SNY or DeMarsico have surfaced.

updates.headline: Giant Freakin Robot references DeMarsico rumors while framing departure in broader MLB local-broadcast context
updates.summary: A column on MLB's push to nationalize local broadcasts mentions DeMarsico's exit, acknowledging that 'controversial rumors spread about the real reasons for his departure' but stating the author could not find reputable sources and that the official reason given by both DeMarsico's account and SNY remains 'creative differences.'

items.title: El Paso airport drone/balloon shutdown
items.pov_summary: The El Paso airport shutdown turned out to be something like a party balloon, not cartel drones, though cartel drones have reportedly been crossing the border. DHS, DoD and FAA aren't communicating well and have a laser system they can't guarantee won't hit commercial aircraft. Hosts think drones probably shouldn't even be legal.
items.quote: Drones should not be legal. I think about that every time we fly them around... I'm like, I shouldn't be allowed to fly this thing the way that I am.
items.updates_summary: No substantive news developments on the El Paso airport drone/balloon shutdown surfaced between the episode air date (Feb 20, 2026) and today. Available reporting is concentrated around the Feb 11 incident itself and the immediate aftermath that the hosts already covered — the CBP laser shooting down party balloons, the Pentagon-FAA coordination breakdown, and disputed cartel-drone claims. No follow-up congressional investigation outcomes, FAA/DoD policy changes, or new cartel drone incidents were found in this window.

items.title: Balloon Boy deep-dive pod idea
items.pov_summary: Riding the high of the CB McDonald deep dive, DJ wants to pitch a full Trap Draw exhaustive dive on the Balloon Boy scandal — what happened, where are they now. Thinks it'd be an immense project they could be proud of. Adding to long-range strategic planning.
items.quote: You know what a sick one would be would be balloon boy... the whole Balloon Boy scandal from start to finish. What happened? Where are they now?
items.updates_summary: No meaningful news developments on the Balloon Boy / Heene family story between the Feb 20, 2026 episode and April 18, 2026. The most recent substantive coverage is tied to the July 2025 Netflix "Trainwreck: Balloon Boy" documentary and follow-up interviews, all of which predate the episode. Since DJ's pitch is a long-range strategic item and not tied to a news peg, there's nothing new to react to.

items.title: Denver airport price-gouging exposé
items.pov_summary: 9News in Denver exposed vendors charging 3x street pricing for Cokes/Pepsis despite the 10–20% markup cap. Hosts frame the law as a 'just be cool' rule that's being flagrantly violated, and wonder if this is why Steve's Snappin' Dogs may be going out of business for refusing to participate.
items.quote: It's kind of a just be cool law, right? Like we will allow you to gouge but let's not be ridiculous.
items.updates_summary: The Denver airport price-gouging exposé has spurred legislative action: Colorado Democrats advanced HB26-1012, a "captive consumer" bill targeting markups at airports, stadiums, and hospitals. However, the Colorado Sun reported in early March that the bill died, though the broader junk fees debate continues.

updates.headline: Colorado Democratic bill takes on price gouging at airports, hospitals, and delivery platforms
updates.summary: House Bill 26-1012, sponsored by Rep. Yara Zokaie, would require cost comparisons and address captive-consumer pricing at places like DIA. It was scheduled for a House Judiciary Committee hearing following the 9News investigation.

updates.headline: Debate set for bill that would consider high stadium costs to be price-gouging
updates.summary: HB 1012 would bar vendors at 'captive-consumer' facilities from selling food/drinks above the county average price and create a private right of action. Business groups and Republicans opposed it, arguing it amounts to price caps that ignore vendors' operating costs.

updates.headline: What's Working: Junk fees debate continues, but HB 1012 is dead
updates.summary: The Colorado Sun reported that HB 1012 — which aimed to cap markups at airports, stadiums, and hospitals — had died, even as the broader junk-fee debate continues.

updates.headline: Customers at major airport overcharged in illegal move
updates.summary: National coverage amplified the 9News findings, noting that two separate investigations 21 years apart show continued non-compliance with DIA's 15% markup rule and highlighting legislative responses protecting 'captive' consumers.

items.title: Robert Isom no-confidence vote at American
items.pov_summary: American Airlines CEO Robert Isom is getting absolutely crushed — flight attendants union delivered a unanimous vote of no confidence and the airline is falling further behind. TC doesn't think Bob makes it past April. Related reader submission: an American regional jet literally had a seat with no seatback, no tray table.
items.status: hosts predict Isom ouster by April
items.quote: Your boy Robert Isom... continues to get absolutely crushed... I don't think Bob's going to make it past April.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb 20 episode, Bob Isom remains in the CEO seat at American despite TC's prediction. In mid-March, the APFA flight attendants union escalated with a "WAR" campaign, urging members to wear symbolic strike regalia on duty, while the board has continued to back Isom. American also added Mary Dillon to its board on March 24, and a Q1 2026 earnings webcast was announced for April 9, 2026.

updates.headline: American Airlines flight attendants launch 'WAR' campaign against CEO Isom
updates.summary: APFA escalated its post-no-confidence campaign, urging its 28,000 members to wear symbolic strike regalia on duty. Aviation A2Z reported that American's board has so far maintained its support for Isom, who continues to emphasize his long-term premium strategy.

updates.headline: American Airlines elects Mary Dillon to its board of directors
updates.summary: American added Mary Dillon to its board, a notable governance move amid union pressure for leadership change.

updates.headline: American Airlines announces Q1 2026 earnings webcast
updates.summary: The company set the date for its Q1 2026 results call, the first earnings update since the no-confidence vote and a key test of Isom's promised 2026 turnaround.

items.title: British Airways crew dosed by passenger gummies
items.pov_summary: A passenger on a Heathrow-LA flight gave BA crew 300mg weed-laced gummies; three staff were hospitalized with out-of-body experiences after eating them on the crew bus. Hosts emphasize this is not funny — it's a Barry McCarney-style OD and a genuine terror for the crew. Investigation ongoing to find the passenger.
items.quote: These things had 300 mg. That is not a funny prank for a passenger to just offer a flight attendant what seems like a harmless gummy bear and then watch them get rushed to the hospital.
items.updates_summary: No substantive developments have been publicly reported between the episode's air date (Feb 20, 2026) and today (Apr 18, 2026). Coverage remains limited to the initial mid-February reports; there have been no announced arrests, identifications of the passenger, or charging decisions, and British Airways' internal investigation has not publicly updated its findings.

items.title: Lifetime bans as a concept
items.pov_summary: Sparked by a Jet2 brawl flight and the Jack Dohety Phoenix Open ejection, DJ muses that lifetime bans might be sick — airlines can probably enforce them via TSA and known-traveler databases, but the PGA Tour has no real dossier on random vloggers. Intriguing but warrants further exploration before making the list.
items.status: under consideration for Things That Are Sick
items.quote: Lifetime bans might be on the list of things that are sick... if you can enforce it.
items.updates_summary: No substantive post-episode developments on either triggering incident. Coverage after Feb 20 has largely been rehashes of the original Jet2 brawl ban and Jack Doherty PGA Tour ban — no reversal, no legal escalation beyond what was already announced, and no new notable "lifetime ban" story to broaden the concept.

items.title: Bitcoin tumbling to 66k
items.pov_summary: Bitcoin is down around $66k and TC is perversely curious about it despite having no position. He'll maybe nibble at $25-30k. Meanwhile Notip, Scottie Pippen, Pooch, and Neil keep HODLing together. Hosts watching from the sidelines.
items.status: price falling, hosts on sidelines monitoring
items.quote: Soly keeps asking me if I'm gonna get in the game. Not till it gets down to about 25 or 30 that I might nibble a little bit.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, Bitcoin actually continued tumbling — crashing all the way to $60,000 on Feb. 5 (closer to TC's $25–30k "nibble" zone than the $66k discussed, though not there) — before staging a sharp rebound. By mid-April 2026 BTC had fully recovered the Feb. 5 crash, climbing to roughly $75,000–$76,000 amid easing Middle East tensions and strong spot ETF inflows. The HODLers appear vindicated for now; TC's dip-buying level never hit.

updates.headline: Bitcoin trades around $66,650 in early April
updates.summary: In early April BTC was hovering near the $66k level the hosts discussed, down roughly $16,500 from a year earlier, suggesting the selloff persisted for weeks after the episode.

updates.headline: Bitcoin climbs to highest level since Feb. 5 crash that sent price to $60,000
updates.summary: BTC rose to nearly $76,000, completing the rebound from a Feb. 5 crash that had briefly sent the price to $60,000. The rally was fueled by Middle East de-escalation, falling oil prices, and spot Bitcoin ETF inflows that have now surpassed $56 billion.

updates.headline: Bitcoin priced at $75,747 on April 17
updates.summary: BTC continued grinding higher into mid-April, trading at $75,746.90 — well above the $66k level at the time of the episode but still about $9,200 below year-ago prices.

items.title: Prince Andrew arrest, Wexner fallout
items.pov_summary: Prince Andrew finally arrested — TC says 'about time.' People are only now distancing themselves from Les Wexner, though Trap Draw was years ahead on that one. Ohio State still has football facilities and a hospital named after him; maybe time for some statues to come down.
items.status: ongoing, hosts calling for OSU to act
items.quote: Buck Tech continued their football facilities still named after Les Wexner, their hospital... maybe sort that stuff out, guys.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb 20 episode, the Wexner fallout at Ohio State has intensified — student walkouts, a Knowlton School banner protest, and nearly 300 formal name-removal requests — but OSU has denied the initial petition and is still processing others. OSU President Ted Carter resigned in March (over an unrelated 'inappropriate relationship'), and the university is now also investigating Epstein-linked payments to a former OSU gynecologist. On Prince Andrew, the UK government is reportedly weighing legislation to remove him from the line of succession once the police investigation concludes.

updates.headline: UK weighs removing ex-Prince Andrew from line of succession
updates.summary: Officials told the BBC that Keir Starmer's government will consider legislation to strip Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor — currently eighth in line — from royal succession once police finish the misconduct-in-public-office investigation.

updates.headline: Ohio State has received nearly 300 requests to remove Wexner name from buildings
updates.summary: As of Feb. 18, OSU had received ~295 formal naming-review requests related to the Wexners, targeting the medical center, football complex, and Wexner Center for the Arts. The Ohio Nurses Association and state lawmakers have joined the push.

updates.headline: Knowlton architecture students unfurl 'Oust Wexner' banner demanding name removal
updates.summary: Students dropped a four-story banner at Knowlton Hall on Feb. 18 — concurrent with Wexner's House Oversight deposition — noting he appears in the Epstein files over 1,000 times. OSU again pointed to its 2022 review procedure.

updates.headline: Hundreds of OSU students walk out demanding Wexner name removal
updates.summary: Protesters marched across the Oval to the Wexner Center for the Arts, saying official channels have failed. OSU President Ted Carter resigned days later over an unrelated 'inappropriate relationship.'

updates.headline: Ohio State investigating Epstein payments to former OSU gynecologist
updates.summary: New OSU probe expands the Wexner/Epstein fallout on campus, examining payments from Epstein to a university gynecologist.

updates.headline: Opinion: It's time for Ohio State to remove Les Wexner's name
updates.summary: OSU's student paper runs a prominent op-ed arguing the Wexner Football Complex name — sitting beneath Woody Hayes's — contradicts the values the program claims, signaling continued campus pressure two months after the arrest.

items.title: Kristi Noem chartered 737 with Lewandowski
items.pov_summary: WSJ piece on Kristi Noem reveals she's openly dating Corey Lewandowski, flying around on a chartered 737 with a luxury cabin in back, and fired a pilot on the spot for forgetting to move her favorite blanket between planes (then had to rehire him to fly it). One of the most outrageous administration stories yet — Bond-villain energy.
items.status: ongoing reporting, hosts watching the creatures wash out
items.quote: It's like one of the most outrageous things I've read within this administration thus far... that's rare air there.
items.updates_summary: Huge developments since the episode: Noem was fired from DHS on March 5, 2026 following contentious bipartisan congressional hearings that focused on the Lewandowski relationship, the luxury 737, a $220M ad campaign, and DHS tactics in Minnesota. Trump named Sen. Markwayne Mullin as her replacement, and congressional Democrats have since filed a criminal referral with DOJ accusing Noem of perjury. PunchUp also published photos from inside the plane's luxury "bedroom" cabin.

updates.headline: Trump fires Kristi Noem as DHS chief, names Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace her
updates.summary: Trump ousted Noem effective March 31 after two days of contentious House and Senate Judiciary hearings where she sparred with lawmakers of both parties over immigration tactics, agency spending, and leadership. Mullin will need Senate confirmation to take over permanently.

updates.headline: Noem out at DHS following tumultuous tenure
updates.summary: At the March 3 Senate Judiciary hearing, Republican Sen. Thom Tillis called Noem's tenure 'a disaster' and threatened to block all of Trump's nominations. Noem is being moved to a new role as special envoy for 'The Shield of the Americas.'

updates.headline: Noem fired after congressional hearings expose 'wholesale corruption' at DHS
updates.summary: Trump fired Noem citing frustrations over a $220 million taxpayer-funded ad campaign featuring Noem on horseback at Mount Rushmore, which had been awarded without standard competitive bidding.

updates.headline: Fired DHS chief Kristi Noem faces criminal referral from congressional Democrats
updates.summary: Rep. Jamie Raskin and Sen. Dick Durbin sent a letter to DOJ accusing Noem of perjury and making false statements to Congress about the $220M ad contract, Lewandowski's role in DHS contracts, and whether DHS follows court orders. Trump undercut Noem's testimony by telling Reuters he 'never knew anything about' the ad campaign.

updates.headline: See Inside Kristi Noem's Mile-High Jet Boudoir
updates.summary: Newsletter published exclusive photos purportedly from inside the $70M 737 MAX's luxury bedroom cabin, describing the interior as resembling 'a ski chalet designed by the production team behind the Austin Powers movies.' Reports that Trump is keeping the jet for the White House even after Noem's ouster, with ICE still footing the bill.

items.title: Dan Bongino FBI departure
items.pov_summary: Dan Bongino stepping down from the FBI — hosts note these creatures are getting unceremoniously chewed up and spit out at the bottom of the funnel. TC wants closure and is punting to Randy for a full breakdown post-paternity.
items.quote: Seems like some of these creatures are kind of washing out of the bottom of the funnel at this point. They get pretty unceremoniously chewed up and spit out at the end.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb 20, 2026 episode, there haven't been major new developments on Bongino's FBI departure itself — the big post-FBI moves (podcast relaunch Feb 2, Fox News contributor return Feb 10) both happened before the episode aired. Since then, coverage has mostly been retrospective commentary on his tumultuous tenure and his reintegration into conservative media, with no new court filings, investigations, or formal follow-up actions.

items.title: Hudson Tunnel and Gordie Howe Bridge
items.pov_summary: Trump froze then unfroze funds for the $20B Hudson Tunnel rail project (already over a billion in) and is now threatening to block the new Canadian-built Gordie Howe Bridge from Detroit to Windsor from opening. Hosts think these should be no-brainer bipartisan infrastructure wins, not political footballs. Background: the privately-owned Ambassador Bridge is controlled by the sketchy Maroun family who've gouged rates for years.
items.quote: This this this should probably be a a bipartisan thing... DT is saying he's gonna try to close it and prevent it from opening.
items.updates_summary: Since the Feb. 20 episode, both stories have advanced significantly. On the Hudson Tunnel, construction resumed Feb. 23/24 after the full $205M in withheld federal funds was released, and a federal appeals court on March 11 rejected the Trump administration's attempt to halt payments.
