On the Gordie Howe Bridge, the opening date is reportedly being "quietly" negotiated behind the scenes to let Trump's criticism subside, while House Democrats are escalating an investigation into ties between Trump and the Moroun family that owns the competing Ambassador Bridge.

updates.headline: Hudson Tunnel construction resumes after full release of $205M in withheld federal funds
updates.summary: The Gateway Development Commission confirmed Feb. 18 it had received the full $205M owed, and construction on the $16B project officially resumed the week of Feb. 23, restoring nearly 1,000 jobs that had been idled by the Feb. 6 shutdown.

updates.headline: Federal appeals court rejects Trump bid to halt Hudson Tunnel payments
updates.summary: A federal appeals court ruled that the Trump administration must continue making payments on the Gateway Tunnel project, a second major court loss for the administration's attempt to freeze funding.

updates.headline: Gordie Howe Bridge opening date being 'quietly' negotiated to avoid angering Trump
updates.summary: An American source familiar with talks says the opening date for the completed bridge is being deliberately kept vague to let Trump's criticism die down. Officials continue to say only that it will open in 'spring 2026' while the Breakaway toll program has already sold over 12,000 transponders.

updates.headline: House Democrats escalate investigation into Trump-Moroun ties over Gordie Howe threat
updates.summary: Democrats are probing the relationship between Trump and the Moroun family, owners of the Ambassador Bridge, after Commerce Secretary Lutnick reportedly met with Matthew Moroun just hours before Trump's Truth Social post threatening to block the new bridge. Rep. Robert Garcia of the House Oversight Committee has formally requested documents related to that meeting.

updates.headline: Partial dismissal of Gateway suit, but court knocks Trump admin for breach of contract
updates.source: New York Daily News / NJ Globe
updates.summary: A federal judge dismissed most of the Gateway Development Commission's suit as moot since funds were released, but ruled the Trump administration did breach its contract — opening the door for the agency to seek damages for the February construction pause.

items.title: North Korean IT-worker infiltration scam
items.pov_summary: WSJ report: North Korean IT workers with polished LinkedIn profiles and Midwest IP addresses are stealing foreigners' identities to land remote jobs at hundreds of Fortune 500 companies, funneling money to fund the nuclear program. Hosts say we need to squash it immediately — but they grudgingly admit it's kind of a sick idea that would make a great movie.
items.quote: Probably not supposed to be the takeaway, but kind of a sick idea... I would watch it if that was a movie.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, two major sentencings have landed in the North Korean IT-worker infiltration case. In mid-April, two New Jersey men who ran "laptop farms" for the scheme were sentenced to 7.5 and 9 years in federal prison, and in February a Ukrainian national who supplied stolen identities was sentenced to 5 years. The State Department also offered up to $5 million in rewards for information on additional operatives.

updates.headline: Two Americans sentenced to prison for North Korean tech worker scheme
updates.summary: A federal court in Boston sentenced Zhenxing 'Danny' Wang to over seven years and Kejia 'Tony' Wang to nine years in prison for running laptop farms from their New Jersey homes that let North Korean workers infiltrate Fortune 500 firms and, in one case, steal export-controlled data from a California defense contractor.

updates.headline: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
updates.summary: DOJ announcement confirms the scheme netted North Korea around $5 million, involved stealing identities of more than 80 Americans, and placed workers at more than 100 U.S. corporations including Fortune 500 companies.

updates.headline: US nationals behind DPRK IT worker 'laptop farm' sent to prison
updates.summary: Details the broader enforcement picture: the Wangs caused an estimated $3 million in damages to victim companies via shell firms like Tony WKJ LLC and Hopana Tech LLC, and notes that in February 2026 Ukrainian national Oleksandr Didenko was separately sentenced to 5 years for supplying stolen identities to DPRK IT workers.

updates.headline: State Department offers $5M bounties for additional DPRK IT-worker facilitators
updates.summary: Alongside the sentencings, the State Department offered rewards of up to $5 million for information on several other individuals allegedly involved in generating revenue for the North Korean regime, signaling the crackdown is ongoing.

items.title: Reese's Peanut Butter Cup recipe change
items.pov_summary: Brad Reese, grandson of the inventor, is publicly criticizing Hershey for quietly replacing the peanut butter recipe in flagship Reese's cups. The theory on X is the minis still taste correct but full-size cups taste like a knockoff. DJ, a self-professed massive Reese's guy, is off sugar but may need to break the fast to weigh in as an SME.
items.quote: Brad Reese, the grandson of the inventor of Reese's Peanut Butter Cups, is criticizing the Hershey Company for quietly replacing Candy's flagship peanut butter ingredients.
items.updates_summary: Hershey caved to the backlash. On April 2, the company announced it will return all Reese's products to their classic milk chocolate and peanut butter recipes in 2027, alongside a 25% boost in R&D spending and other portfolio changes (natural colors, a creamier Kit Kat).

updates.headline: Hershey to shift back to classic recipe for all Reese's products after criticism
updates.summary: Hershey said it will move the small portion of Reese's and Hershey's products currently made with reduced-chocolate coatings (like mini Easter eggs) back to classic milk and dark chocolate recipes in 2027, and will boost R&D spending by 25%.

updates.headline: Hershey is moving back to the original recipe for Reese's Peanut Butter Cups
updates.summary: Fortune covers the reversal and notes Hershey acknowledged some recipe changes but framed them as innovation-driven, while high cocoa prices had pushed manufacturers toward less chocolate.

updates.headline: Hershey will go back to original Reese's recipe after public backlash
updates.summary: Notes Hershey's stock dipped in March as the controversy grew (though shares are still up 10% on the year) and confirms the company did not directly mention Brad Reese's complaints in its announcement.

updates.headline: Hershey's Reverses Reese's Recipe Changes After Backlash
updates.summary: Reports that roughly 3% of Reese's products currently use 'peanut butter crème' instead of USDA-defined peanut butter, and ties the original changes to CEO Kirk Tanner, who took over in August 2025.

items.title: MapTap geography game
items.pov_summary: DJ nominates MapTap.gg — daily game where you tap where a city is on an unmarked map — as sick. Exposes what you know, educational, satisfying when you nail one. Geography in general is sick. Likely iPad advantage over phone.
items.status: nominated for Things That Are Sick
items.quote: Gives you a little satisfaction if you get one that's pretty close... all things that I think add up to something that's decidedly sick.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, MapTap has pushed several app updates. Most notably, a new Versus Mode was added letting players challenge friends to a 20-second, 5-location head-to-head game, along with new practice maps for Italy, Spain, and Turkey and the ability to play prior days back 90 days. The app also rose a spot in the Education chart as of April 18, 2026.

updates.headline: MapTap v3.0 adds Versus Mode for head-to-head geography battles
updates.summary: MapTap released a Versus Mode letting players challenge friends to a 20-second, 5-location game, framed as a way to prove skills if you suspect a friend cheats at the daily. The update also fixed sound interruption with Spotify and other bugs.

updates.headline: MapTap v3.03 expands practice maps and extends prior-day play window
updates.summary: Subsequent point releases added new practice maps for Italy, Spain, and Turkey, extended 'play prior days' back to 90 days, bumped daily challenge practice level from 20 to 25 seconds, and fixed Sign in with Apple. The app rose +1 in the Education chart as of April 18, 2026.

items.title: Things That Are Sick nominee triage
items.pov_summary: Hosts reject several listener nominations: 'soup' too vague, 'jargon' too system-y (though the list is mostly systems), 'Venus fly traps' too obvious like monster trucks, and 'perfectly aligning your tire at the car wash' too specific. Microclimates and controlled burns/avalanche triggering get floated favorably. Tight list philosophy reaffirmed.
items.quote: We've now missed on the complete opposite side of the soup conundrum... we're just way too in the weeds with stuff like that.
items.updates_summary: No external news developments to track—this is an internal podcast bit about curating the hosts' "Things That Are Sick" list. Subsequent TrapDraw episodes have continued to revisit the nominations thread as part of the show's regular segments, but there's no substantive outside story to update.

items.title: Tony Clark MLBPA ouster
items.pov_summary: Tony Clark stepped down as MLBPA head over an alleged inappropriate relationship with his sister-in-law (also a union employee), uncovered while he was being investigated for giving himself equity in a union licensing company. Terrible timing right before the labor apocalypse, but the negotiation is such a runaway train that plugging in a replacement may be easier than it sounds.
items.quote: Probably not what you want to lose right before this the labor fight of the century.
items.updates_summary: The MLBPA's internal cleanup expanded beyond Clark: on April 15, the union fired COO Xavier James and HR head Michael O'Neill with cause, following a report from the internal investigator. Chris Capuano was installed as new COO. Interim ED Bruce Meyer (elected Feb 18) has begun his spring training camp tour and publicly reiterated the union's firm anti-salary-cap stance heading into CBA talks.

updates.headline: MLBPA fires COO and HR head with cause amid ongoing federal probe
updates.summary: The MLBPA dismissed COO Xavier James and HR chief Michael O'Neill following a report from internal investigator Adam Braverman. Former MLB pitcher Chris Capuano was named new COO and Ian Penny was named interim chief HR officer. Investigators are reportedly looking at OneTeam Partners and the shuttered Players Way youth initiative, on which the union allegedly spent over $10M with limited returns.

updates.headline: Meyer tours spring training camps, doubles down on anti-cap stance
updates.summary: Interim ED Bruce Meyer made the rounds of MLB clubhouses (Tigers, Phillies, etc.) as part of the annual camp tour, framing the upcoming CBA fight and signaling no softening of the union's position against a salary cap despite the leadership shakeup.

items.title: Brewers trade Caleb Durbin, left side of infield hollowed
items.pov_summary: Milwaukee dealt rookie-of-the-year finalist Caleb Durbin and basically everyone capable of playing third base, bringing back nondescript infielders and prospects. DJ is nervy — he thought Durbin was a 25-year Brewer. Joey Ortiz can't hit. The Brewers always figure it out with their lab magic, but this feels like pushing it a little too far coming off the best record in baseball.
items.status: roster construction ongoing, hosts skeptical but trusting
items.quote: I wonder if we're odding on the 'oh this is just the Brewers, they're going to figure it out'... I'm a little bit like okay man.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, the Brewers filled the left-side infield gaps through a mix of veteran signing and internal shuffling: Luis Rengifo was added to share third base with Jett Williams, while Joey Ortiz opened 2026 as the starting shortstop with David Hamilton as the primary utility backup. Early returns on Ortiz's bat remain ugly (3-for-19 through five April games), validating the hosts' concern, though Pat Murphy has publicly backed him.

updates.headline: Brewers 2026 preview: Ortiz keeps grip on shortstop job, but it's loosened
updates.summary: Preview confirms Ortiz enters 2026 as the starter at shortstop with Hamilton as the primary backup across 2B/3B/SS, while naming Cooper Pratt as a potential in-season alternative if Ortiz continues to struggle.

updates.headline: Rengifo handling third base, Ortiz off to cold start
updates.summary: Milwaukee has been running Luis Rengifo at third base and David Hamilton at shortstop on Ortiz's off days. Through five April games Ortiz is 3-for-19 (.158) with five strikeouts.

updates.headline: Pat Murphy bullish on David Hamilton's potential after Durbin trade
updates.summary: Manager Pat Murphy publicly hyped Hamilton — the utility infielder acquired in the Durbin deal — saying he believes Hamilton can take his offensive game to another level, despite a .590 OPS in 2025.

items.title: Norwegian biathlete cheating confession
items.pov_summary: Norwegian biathlete won bronze and used his interview to publicly confess he'd cheated on his girlfriend 10 days earlier. Girlfriend responded calling out how dare he make the admission to the world. TC stands with her — that should've been handled behind closed doors, not a proposing-on-the-jumbotron move.
items.status: public fallout, hosts siding with girlfriend
items.quote: I stand with her. That feels like that's best handled behind closed doors. Uh not in front of the world.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired on Feb 20, there have been no substantive new developments on the Lægreid cheating-confession story. On the day the episode aired, Reuters/NBC reported he'd capped a turbulent Games with five total medals (three silver, two bronze), and he indicated he was ready to "live the real life again" upon returning home. No further public statements from the ex-girlfriend or follow-up reconciliation news have surfaced in the two months since.

updates.headline: Lægreid finishes Milan Cortina Games with five medals after viral confession
updates.summary: Lægreid took silver in Friday's mass start to bring his total to three silvers and two bronzes — an unexpected haul given he hadn't made a World Cup podium all season. He said he was ready to leave the 'bubble' of the Olympics and return to real life.

items.title: Miami Red Hawks 25-0
items.pov_summary: TC's Miami (OH) Red Hawks remain undefeated at 25-0, ranked 22nd AP / 23rd coaches poll despite computers having them around 80th. THG, despite it being his alma mater, refuses to give them their due. Bowling Green, Eastern Michigan, Western Michigan, Toledo and Ohio U still on the slate before the MAC tournament.
items.quote: It's unprecedented. THG, despite this being his alma mater, he hates Miami basketball.
items.updates_summary: Miami (OH) completed a perfect 31-0 regular season, winning the MAC regular-season title and becoming the fifth D-I program this century to go undefeated in the regular season. They were then stunned by 8-seed UMass 87-83 in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals, but received an at-large bid as an 11 seed, beat SMU 89-79 in the First Four for the program's first NCAA Tournament win since 1999, and lost to 6-seed Tennessee in the Round of 64.

updates.headline: 30-0! Miami Clinches 22nd MAC Regular-Season Title Over Toledo
updates.summary: Miami beat Toledo 74-72 in front of a sellout Millett Hall crowd to clinch the outright MAC regular-season title and move to 30-0. Coach Travis Steele said finishing undefeated and winning the MAC Tournament were the remaining goals.

updates.headline: RedHawks complete 31-0 regular season with OT win at Ohio
updates.summary: Miami finished the regular season 31-0 with a 110-108 overtime win at rival Ohio, becoming the fifth D-I program this century to go unbeaten in the regular season. Jackson Paveletzke's potential buzzer-beater for Ohio missed.

updates.headline: Undefeated no more: Miami (Ohio) falls to UMass 87-83 in MAC quarterfinals
updates.summary: The 1-seed RedHawks, 31-0 entering the day, lost their first game of the season to 8-seed UMass in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals. UMass dominated the glass 41-24, and Leonardo Bettiol dropped 25 points.

updates.headline: Miami (Ohio) still projected into NCAA Tournament field despite UMass loss
updates.summary: Despite the stunning quarterfinal loss, bracketologists kept Miami in the field as an at-large, and the UMass upset meant the MAC was likely to be a two-bid league for the first time since 1999.

updates.headline: Why Miami (Ohio)'s Loss Could Mean More Money for the MAC
updates.summary: Sportico analyzed how the RedHawks' quarterfinal loss, by nearly guaranteeing the MAC two March Madness bids, could yield additional NCAA tournament 'units' worth $300,000-$400,000 annually for six years to MAC programs.

updates.headline: Miami earns 11-seed, beats SMU in First Four before falling to Tennessee
updates.summary: Miami received an at-large 11 seed—its first NCAA Tournament appearance since 2007—and defeated SMU 89-79 in the First Four for the program's first tournament win since 1999 before losing to 6-seed Tennessee in the Round of 64.

items.title: Rashee Rice domestic violence lawsuit
items.pov_summary: Rashee Rice being sued in civil court for alleged domestic violence going back years. Hosts note the Chiefs continue to 'employ some great individuals' — real character guys. Chiefs Corner remains open.
items.quote: The Chiefs continue to really employ some great individuals, character guys.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, the NFL concluded its investigation and announced it will not discipline Rashee Rice, finding insufficient evidence that he violated the personal conduct policy. The civil lawsuit filed by Dacoda Jones remains open, and Rice's attorney cited a prior affidavit of non-prosecution from Jones in defense.

updates.headline: NFL won't discipline Rashee Rice over domestic violence allegations
updates.summary: The NFL determined Rice 'has not engaged in conduct that violates the personal conduct policy,' closing its investigation into Dacoda Jones's allegations. The civil lawsuit remains open.

updates.headline: NFL closes personal conduct probe involving Rashee Rice
updates.summary: Per Adam Schefter, the league cited insufficient evidence. Rice's attorney Sean Lindsey thanked the NFL and said Rice looks forward to the 2026-27 season.

updates.headline: NFL declines to suspend Rice; lawsuit still active
updates.summary: SI notes that while the NFL closed its review, Jones's civil suit seeking $1 million in damages continues. Rice's lawyer pointed to an October 2025 affidavit of non-prosecution from Jones.

items.title: Oscars prep and Best Picture nominee bloat
items.pov_summary: DJ's Oscars pod is imminent. Early verdict: Frankenstein is a nothing burger, F1 getting a Best Picture nom is classic 'was it good? no, did you enjoy it? yes' territory. He understands the 10-nominee logic (helps fringe films like Train Dreams, Sentimental Value get seen) but thinks it lets in slop like F1, Wicked, or Top Gun Maverick. Mercifully Avatar and Wicked didn't make it this year.
items.quote: Best picture feels a little questionable... occasionally you get a Top Gun Maverick or F1 or Avatar Wicked or whatever that slips in that's like, all right, well, we didn't need to do this.
items.updates_summary: The 98th Academy Awards took place March 15, 2026, with One Battle After Another winning Best Picture (six total wins including Director and Adapted Screenplay for Paul Thomas Anderson). Sinners, the most-nominated film in Oscar history with 16 nods, took four including Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan). Notably for the hosts' "bloat" thesis: F1 won Best Sound and Frankenstein took three craft awards, validating the logic that some of the more populist/fringe nominees still factored into the night.

updates.headline: 'One Battle After Another' wins Best Picture at 98th Oscars
updates.summary: Paul Thomas Anderson's thriller beat Sinners in a tight race, winning six total Oscars including Best Director and Best Adapted Screenplay — PTA's first-ever wins. Jessie Buckley won Best Actress for Hamnet and Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor for Sinners.

updates.headline: Sinners sets nominations record; Warner Bros. ties studio wins record
updates.summary: Sinners' 16 nominations surpassed the previous record of 14 held by All About Eve, Titanic, and La La Land. Warner Bros. won 11 Oscars across One Battle After Another, Sinners, and Weapons, tying its own studio record.

updates.headline: F1 and Frankenstein factor in craft categories despite Best Picture 'bloat' concerns
updates.summary: F1 won Best Sound and Frankenstein took three craft statuettes (Makeup & Hairstyling, Costume Design, Production Design) — a partial vindication of the expanded nominee field that DJ had flagged as letting in 'slop.'

updates.headline: Academy debuts Best Casting award; new mandatory-viewing voting rule
updates.summary: Cassandra Kulukundis won the inaugural Best Casting Oscar for One Battle After Another — the first new category since Best Animated Feature in 2001. The ceremony was also the first to enforce a rule requiring members to view every nominated film before voting.

updates.headline: Sentimental Value wins Best International Feature
updates.summary: Norway's Sentimental Value — one of the 'fringe' films DJ credited the 10-nominee field for surfacing — won Best International Feature, though it went 0-for on its other nominations including Best Picture.

items.title: TC loses 190 curated Safari tabs wiping old phone
items.pov_summary: TC wiped his old iPhone to upgrade to a 17 Pro Max and didn't realize Safari tabs don't back up to iCloud — 190 meticulously curated tabs of articles, recommendations and wisdom are gone forever. He's treating it as an incalculable loss that could alter the course of his life, joking he's entering the dark ages. Randy floated grief counseling.
items.status: unresolvable loss, TC seeking IT forensics help
items.quote: I legitimately like I I had this massive sense of anxiety like this could change the course of the rest of my life because I don't have these tabs anymore.
items.updates_summary: This is a personal show-bit about host TC losing his Safari tabs during a phone upgrade — not a news topic with external developments to track. No updates to report.

items.title: Epstein files and Prince Andrew fallout
items.pov_summary: The hosts are still watching the files saga and are frustrated by the lack of real accountability. They want arrests and jail time regardless of political affiliation and have been banging this drum for years — the country won't get out of its malaise until people actually go to jail.
items.quote: If people need to be arrested, if people need to go to jail, like let's let's do it. We need to get some people in jail.
items.updates_summary: Since the March 5 episode, there have been several meaningful developments the hosts would care about given their demand for real accountability: AG Pam Bondi was fired by Trump on April 2 amid bipartisan criticism of her handling of the files, Melania Trump delivered a rare White House denial that she was an Epstein victim, and a late-March DOJ release included previously withheld documents about unsubstantiated allegations against Trump. On the Prince Andrew front, the UK government agreed to release his trade envoy files, and UN human rights experts again demanded accountability on April 16. However, U.S. prosecutors still have not brought new charges from the files more than two months after the January release.

updates.headline: Trump fires Attorney General Pam Bondi amid Epstein files backlash
updates.summary: Trump removed Bondi as Attorney General following bipartisan criticism over her handling of the Epstein files release and installed Deputy AG Todd Blanche as Acting AG. Bondi had been subpoenaed by the House Oversight Committee on March 4 and subsequently declined to testify after being fired.

updates.headline: NPR: No new U.S. arrests two months after Epstein files release
updates.summary: Despite more than 3 million pages released and bipartisan demands for accountability, federal prosecutors have brought no new charges. DOJ said there is no credible evidence Epstein's network extended beyond him and Maxwell — directly speaking to the hosts' frustration about a lack of jail time.

updates.headline: Melania Trump delivers White House statement denying she was an Epstein victim
updates.summary: In a rare public address, the first lady denied 'lies linking me to the disgraceful Jeffrey Epstein' and said she had no knowledge of crimes by Epstein or Maxwell.

updates.headline: DOJ publishes additional 3 million pages under Epstein Files Transparency Act
updates.summary: The Justice Department announced a further tranche bringing total production to roughly 3.5 million pages, along with 2,000 videos and 180,000 images. Later in March, DOJ made public previously unreleased documents containing unsubstantiated claims by a woman that she was abused as a teen by Epstein and Trump.

updates.headline: UK government to release Mountbatten-Windsor trade envoy files
updates.summary: Parliament approved a Liberal Democrat motion forcing disclosure of documents related to Andrew's appointment as trade envoy, though some publication may be delayed until police finish investigating. Ex-Labour figure Peter Mandelson was also bailed in connection with related allegations.

updates.headline: UN experts renew call for accountability in Epstein case
updates.summary: As global attention wanes, Human Rights Council-appointed experts again demanded concrete action, reparations and transparency, warning states' obligation 'is long overdue.'

items.title: Chewing gum sales collapsing post-pandemic
items.pov_summary: DJ flagged and TC confirmed that proper bubble gum is disappearing from shelves — Bubbleicious is nowhere to be found and Fruit Stripe has been discontinued. TC is a gum guy and is genuinely bothered by the decline of the category.
items.quote: People just aren't buying chewing gum like they used to... I can't find Bubbleicious anywhere.
items.updates_summary: No substantive news developments about the chewing gum category's post-pandemic decline emerged between the episode's air date (March 5, 2026) and today. Coverage from this window is limited to general industry market reports reiterating the long-running story — that sales collapsed during COVID and have only partially recovered — without new product news, relaunches, or brand obituaries specifically tied to the hosts' complaints about Bubblicious and Fruit Stripe.

items.title: Brad Reese crusades against Hershey ingredient changes
items.pov_summary: The grandson of Reese's inventor is going off on LinkedIn accusing Hershey of secretly swapping in cheaper imitation chocolate and peanut butter. Randy is fully riding with Brad — it's milk chocolate plus peanut butter, that's it — and offering moral support until something changes. Mr. Beast amplified the letter.
items.quote: I will ride with Brad Reese uh on on that point... I might go out and buy Reese's, but I think he would tell me don't do that. We need to push for some change.
items.updates_summary: Brad Reese's LinkedIn crusade scored a major win: in early April 2026, Hershey announced it will revert affected Reese's products to "classic" milk and dark chocolate recipes starting in 2027. However, Brad called the reversal a "PR stunt" for being too slow, and more than 50 members of the broader Reese family publicly distanced themselves from his campaign in a statement provided to Hershey.

updates.headline: Hershey to shift some Reese's products back to 'classic' recipes
updates.summary: Hershey CEO Kirk Tanner announced the company will return affected Reese's products to classic milk and dark chocolate recipes in 2027, phase out compound coatings in under 3% of Reese's items, revise Kit Kat for a creamier taste, eliminate artificial colors by end of 2027, and boost R&D spending 25%. Brad Reese dismissed the timeline as a 'PR stunt,' saying if it were real they'd do it immediately.

updates.headline: Reese family publicly disavows Brad Reese's campaign
updates.summary: In a statement provided to Hershey by Rebecca Hilgers and said to represent more than 50 Reese family members, the family said they have no involvement in and do not support Brad Reese's claims, adding that his statements are his own and do not reflect the family's view.

updates.headline: Reese's grandson may taste sweet victory as Hershey reverses course
updates.summary: NBC reports Brad Reese is skeptical of Hershey's announcement, calling it an attempt to 'change the PR narrative' and vowing to test next year's Valentine's Day Mini Hearts to verify whether real milk chocolate has returned.

items.title: Judge rules boneless wings don't need bones
items.pov_summary: A serial plaintiff lost a deceptive-labeling suit against Buffalo Wild Wings with a judge cracking wise that the claim had 'no meat on its bones.' The hosts side philosophically with the plaintiff — boneless wings are nuggets, words matter — but agree the lawsuit was frivolous. Neither host eats boneless wings anyway.
items.status: resolved in court, hosts linguistically unconvinced
items.quote: Boneless chicken wings are not wings. They're nuggets or chicken fingers... words matter.
items.updates_summary: No substantive developments since the episode aired. The plaintiff had a March 20, 2026 deadline to file an amended complaint, but available reporting and the public docket through early April show no notable follow-up filing or new ruling, and the judge had already signaled skepticism that any amended complaint could salvage the claim.

items.title: Hawks Magic City night draws Spurs player's ire
items.pov_summary: The Atlanta Hawks are doing a Magic City themed night and Spurs player Luke Cornet called it a bad look for the league. The hosts think Luke doesn't get it — Magic City is a cultural institution in Atlanta and tied to the whole lemon-pepper-wings Jamal Crawford lore. Hawks finally fun again post-Trae Young.
items.status: scheduled, hosts are pro-Magic City night
items.quote: Magic City is a a cultural institution in Atlanta... We got bigger issues in the world. I have no problems with a Magic City night.
items.updates_summary: After the episode aired, the NBA stepped in and canceled the Hawks' Magic City Night, with Commissioner Adam Silver citing concerns from league stakeholders. The cancellation drew further reactions from players (Draymond Green defended Magic City; Jeff Teague criticized Kornet) and from Magic City dancers who called the cancellation a slap in the face.

updates.headline: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' Magic City Night promotion
updates.summary: Adam Silver announced the league called off the March 16 promotion after hearing 'significant concerns' from fans, partners and employees. The Hawks said they were disappointed but respected the decision.

updates.headline: Jeff Teague rips Luke Kornet over Magic City criticism
updates.summary: Former Hawk Jeff Teague said Kornet had no business speaking out since he wasn't even playing that night, arguing Magic City's cultural importance to Atlanta is bigger than Kornet could comprehend.

updates.headline: Atlanta locals react to canceled Magic City Night
updates.summary: Hawks fans and Atlantans pushed back on the cancellation, with season-ticket holders calling the outrage overblown and noting Magic City's role as a launching pad for Atlanta hip-hop artists like Migos and Future.

updates.headline: Draymond Green defends Magic City Night as art
updates.summary: Draymond Green weighed in publicly defending the Hawks' promotion, framing strip clubs as art and siding against Kornet's position.

updates.headline: Magic City dancer calls cancellation 'a slap in the face'
updates.summary: A dancer known as NatsHoney offered a rebuttal to Kornet and the NBA's moralizing, arguing the cancellation disrespected the women who work at the club.

items.title: Publix invades Kroger territory in Kentucky
items.pov_summary: Publix is opening ~seven stores in Boone County, Kentucky right at Kroger's doorstep, forcing Kroger to upgrade. The hosts welcome the supermarket wars as healthy competition, though TC thinks Publix is overpriced and Kroger stores outside Cincinnati are trash.
items.quote: I for one welcome our our supermarket wars.
items.updates_summary: Since the March 5 episode, Publix has escalated its Kentucky push by signing leases for three more stores beyond the Northern Kentucky ring, while Kroger has responded with price cuts and store upgrades — and is counter-invading Publix's home turf in Jacksonville, Florida.

updates.headline: Publix adds 3 more stores to Kentucky pipeline
updates.summary: Publix signed leases for three additional Kentucky stores in Bowling Green, Richmond and Versailles, on top of the six already open, pushing deeper into Kroger's stronghold.

updates.headline: Publix takes aim at Kroger while hitting most targets in Q4
updates.summary: Kroger responded to Publix's Kentucky expansion by lowering prices at its Northern Kentucky stores and announcing plans to open new locations in Jacksonville, Florida — a Publix-dominated market.

updates.headline: Kroger Faces New Rivalry as Publix Expands into Kentucky
updates.summary: Kroger has slashed prices and revamped its Edgewood, KY store as Publix targets roughly a dozen Kentucky locations by year-end, with both chains also pressured by Walmart and Aldi.

updates.headline: Publix intensifies its battle with Kroger in Kentucky
updates.summary: New Publix stores in Richmond, Versailles, and Bowling Green will each span nearly 55,000 square feet with attached Publix Liquors, bringing the chain toward a dozen Kentucky locations by year-end.

items.title: American Airlines double-catering London flights
items.pov_summary: American is so operationally broken that they're double-catering all London Heathrow departures from the US — no seafood, no ice cream, stale food on the return. TC calls the airline lazy, down-market, sick and decrepit, especially embarrassing during their 100th anniversary year. United's food program is meanwhile leveling up.
items.status: ongoing operational mess, TC bearish on AA
items.quote: Lazy down market. It's crazy. Really bad. So very very sick and decrepit airline at this point.
items.updates_summary: The Heathrow catering mess has dragged on well past the episode air date. British Airways stepped in with a partial fix via premium caterer Do&Co for Business Class (and first-meal Premium Economy), but Economy and secondary service remain on US double-catering. American's flight attendants union (APFA) filed a formal grievance demanding extra pay over the increased workload, with no confirmed timeline for full resolution.

updates.headline: American Airlines flight attendants demand extra pay over Heathrow catering crisis
updates.summary: The APFA filed a formal grievance on behalf of crew, citing unpredictable conditions and no corresponding adjustment to staffing or pay after AA suspended its longtime LHR caterer and went to double catering on US-bound returns. British Airways stepped in via Do&Co to partially restore Business Class and first-meal Premium Economy service.

updates.headline: Crew revolt escalates as London catering crisis deepens
updates.summary: Weeks into the disruption, Economy Class and secondary meal services still rely on double catering from the US. The APFA says the altered model has materially increased crew responsibilities, and no timeline has been confirmed for a complete resolution.

updates.headline: Blogger pushback: AA flight attendants' pay demand called 'weak'
updates.summary: Matthew Klint argues that double catering is a temporary disruption, not a structural change to the job, and that demanding premium pay for a short-term catering transition is a stretch — adding color to the ongoing labor dispute.

items.title: Miami RedHawks hoops 29-0 and ranked 19th
items.pov_summary: Randy's Miami RedHawks are 29-0, ranked 19th, chasing a MAC regular season title and an NCAA tournament bid — unthinkable after 26 years of irrelevance. Coach Travis Steele lost his cool at Western Michigan and knocked over a DJ's speaker, but has since apologized and offered to replace the equipment. Randy cautiously hopes Steele stays.
items.status: ongoing dream season, tournament bid looming
items.quote: I had lost hope. I never thought we'd be ranked again. I never thought we'd be 29-0... I am absolutely on cloud nine.
items.updates_summary: Miami's historic run continued but ended in the NCAA Tournament Round of 64. After finishing the regular season 31-0, the RedHawks lost to UMass in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals, earned an at-large 11-seed, beat SMU 89-79 in the First Four in Dayton, then fell 78-56 to Tennessee. Head coach Travis Steele was named the 2026 John McLendon National Coach of the Year and — despite links to the Butler job — confirmed he's staying at Miami, saying he's close to finalizing a new contract extension through 2033-34.

updates.headline: UMass upsets undefeated Miami 87-83 in MAC Tournament quarterfinals
updates.summary: Frank Martin's UMass handed Miami its first loss of the season, ending the RedHawks' undefeated bid and putting their at-large hopes in question. Steele used the postgame to criticize potential NCAA Tournament expansion, arguing it wouldn't help mid-majors.

updates.headline: Miami earns at-large bid as 11-seed, drawn into First Four vs. SMU
updates.summary: Despite the MAC tournament loss, the 31-1 RedHawks were selected as an 11-seed and sent to the First Four at UD Arena in Dayton — effectively a home game — against fellow 11-seed SMU.

updates.headline: Steele linked to Butler head coaching job
updates.summary: Ahead of the First Four, Jeff Goodman reported Steele — a Butler alum and former student manager — was among early candidates for the Bulldogs job after Thad Matta's retirement.

updates.headline: Miami beats SMU 89-79 in First Four for first NCAA Tournament win since 1999
updates.summary: The RedHawks went 16-of-41 from three in front of a partisan Dayton crowd to earn their first NCAA Tournament victory in 27 years.

updates.headline: Tennessee ends Miami's season 78-56 in first round
updates.summary: Ja'Kobi Gillespie torched Miami from deep as the 6-seed Volunteers eliminated the RedHawks in Philadelphia, finishing Miami at 32-2.

updates.headline: Steele named 2026 John McLendon National Coach of the Year
updates.summary: College Insider named Steele the top head coach across all levels of college basketball. He was also a Naismith Coach of the Year semifinalist and unanimous MAC Coach of the Year.

updates.headline: Steele confirms he's staying at Miami, close to new contract through 2033-34
updates.summary: Steele said 'I'm 100 percent here' and is close to finalizing an extension that would make him one of the highest-paid coaches in the MAC. Miami lost Brant Byers, Eian Elmer, and Trey Perry to the transfer portal but added Milwaukee guard Stevie Elam.

items.title: Bruce Pearl trashes mid-majors to prop up Auburn
items.pov_summary: Bruce Pearl is out bashing mid-majors like Miami to help his son's mediocre 15-14 Auburn team's bubble case. Randy thinks Pearl is a low-life clown making a lazy, nakedly biased argument — a 31-0 Miami belongs in the field of 68 ahead of middling power-conference teams. Joe Lunardi data backs up that 27+ win mid-majors fare better in March than middling power teams.
items.quote: Bruce Pearl's a buffoon and a clown... he makes Rick Pitino look like a like an angel... It's such a lazy, stupid argument, I think.
items.updates_summary: The Pearl/Auburn saga played out exactly as the hosts predicted — and got worse. Auburn collapsed to a 17-16 finish, was snubbed from the NCAA Tournament and sent to the NIT, while Miami (Ohio) made the field. Bruce Pearl doubled down throughout: admitting nepotism in hiring his son, screaming at refs from the stands during Auburn's SEC Tournament exit, and still lobbying for Auburn on the CBS Selection Show — drawing public rebukes from Miami (OH)'s AD and a viral UMBC troll.

updates.headline: Miami (OH) AD David Sayler rips Bruce Pearl, suggests TV 'disclaimer' over Auburn bias
updates.summary: Miami (Ohio) athletic director David Sayler publicly called out Pearl, saying he shouldn't be on TV opining on the tournament when he's clearly biased and using 'we' about Auburn. Sayler said networks should add a disclaimer when Pearl discusses bubble teams.

updates.headline: Pearl openly admits nepotism in hiring son Steven at Auburn
updates.summary: On Dan Dakich's 'Don't @ Me' show, Pearl flatly confirmed he helped his son get the Auburn head coaching job through nepotism, reigniting the governance and fairness debate as Auburn's bubble case hung in the balance.

updates.headline: Bruce Pearl explodes from the stands as Auburn is bounced from SEC Tournament
updates.summary: During Auburn's second-round SEC Tournament loss to Tennessee on March 12, Bruce Pearl was filmed hurling expletives at officials from the stands, shocking Tennessee fans around him as his son Steven's team was eliminated.

updates.headline: Auburn snubbed from NCAA Tournament, heads to NIT at 17-16
updates.summary: After losing nine of their last 12 games and finishing 17-16, Auburn was left out of the field. Pearl, working the Selection Show, visibly bristled and continued lobbying on air, citing wins over Florida, St. John's and Arkansas.

updates.headline: UMBC trolls Bruce Pearl and Auburn after earning autobid
updates.summary: UMBC's athletics account, famous for its 2018 16-over-1 trolling, used its America East title to mock Pearl: joking the best part of an autobid is not needing 'a relative going on tv' to argue they deserve in over 30-win Miami (Ohio).

updates.headline: Pearl pivots to arguing Auburn was better than SMU after snub
updates.summary: Post-selection, Pearl shifted to attacking SMU's inclusion, arguing Oklahoma or Auburn should have gotten the last at-large over the Mustangs, whose 191 non-conference SOS he cited.

items.title: Tony Dungy pushed out of Sunday Night Football
items.pov_summary: Tony Dungy appears to be out at NBC's Sunday Night Football, potentially clearing space for Mike Tomlin. The hosts think Dungy is the ace of spades of bad football analysts — never says anything insightful — and the Clapper has quietly become genuinely good, especially on live games.
items.quote: I think it's got to be Dungy. He's so bad, man. Like he's never said anything even remotely insightful.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode, Dungy's departure was officially confirmed — he announced on X on March 12 that NBC informed him he won't return to Football Night in America. Reporting since then suggests Jason Garrett could also be out, and Mike Tomlin is viewed as the clear favorite to fill the vacancy, with his TV free agency expected to resolve quickly ahead of the networks' April/May upfronts.

updates.headline: Tony Dungy confirms NBC won't bring him back to Football Night in America
updates.summary: Dungy announced on X that NBC informed him he won't return this fall after 17 seasons, thanking the network and specifically Rodney Harrison. He did not rule out a return to broadcasting elsewhere.

updates.headline: 5 Things We're Hearing About Mike Tomlin's TV Free Agency
updates.summary: NBC is viewed as the clear favorite for Tomlin, with fellow analyst Jason Garrett also reportedly possibly out — giving NBC potentially two ex-coach spots to fill. Tomlin's deal could reach eight figures as a studio analyst, and the sweepstakes is expected to resolve before the April/May upfronts.

updates.headline: Mike Tomlin's future in TV is heading in one obvious direction
updates.summary: With Dungy out, NBC's FNIA needs a companion for Maria Taylor and Rodney Harrison, and Tomlin — close friends with Fox's Jay Glazer — is seen as the obvious fit, though Fox could theoretically expand its desk to accommodate him.

updates.headline: Mike Tomlin emerges as preferred replacement for Tony Dungy at NBC
updates.summary: Per Andrew Marchand, Tomlin is the No. 1 NFL TV studio target but may be reluctant to enter TV, and Ronde Barber has said he believes Tomlin is done with football for good.

items.title: US Half Marathon leader sent off course by lead vehicle
items.pov_summary: Jess Mclain was leading the US Half Marathon in Atlanta with two miles to go when the lead vehicle took a wrong turn and she finished ninth. Appeals found the course wasn't adequately marked but refused to alter results. TC thinks following the course is part of the sport, but shame on the Atlanta Track Club.
items.status: results final, hosts split on fairness
items.quote: Shame on the Atlanta Track Club, which is a very proud and storied institution.
items.updates_summary: After the episode aired, World Athletics approved a one-off exception allowing USATF to field a seven-woman half marathon team (instead of the standard four) at the September 2026 World Road Running Championships in Copenhagen. The three runners led off course — Jess McClain, Emma Grace Hurley and Ednah Kurgat — will be invited alongside the three official podium finishers (Molly Born, Carrie Ellwood, Annie Rodenfels), with a seventh spot to be awarded via world rankings in early May.

updates.headline: World Athletics Approves Seven-Woman U.S. Team for 2026 Road Running Championships
updates.summary: World Athletics granted USATF a one-off exception to enter seven women in the half marathon at Copenhagen 2026, so that McClain, Hurley and Kurgat can compete alongside the official top-three finishers. USATF CEO Max Siegel thanked World Athletics and said the focus from the start was on doing right by the athletes.

updates.headline: Runners misled at a qualifier race will still get invites to the world championship
updates.summary: NPR reported the resolution, with Molly Born calling it the best possible outcome for a 'special situation.' The seventh team member will be named in early May based on world rankings, though only four U.S. runners will score for the team.

updates.headline: USATF Announces Expanded Women's Team for World Road Running Championships Following Atlanta Incident
updates.summary: USATF's official statement confirmed team spots would go to the three runners led off course plus the top three official finishers, with the full team to be named after the USATF 1 Mile (April 22) and 5K (May 2) Championships.

items.title: USC's Chad Baker age-fraud rumor debunked
items.pov_summary: An anonymous tip claimed USC hooper Chad Baker was actually 32 and a father of four — alas, it's not true, he's really 26. TC is bummed because it would have been one of the great sports stories. What are we doing.
items.status: debunked, hosts disappointed it wasn't real
items.quote: I'm not shocked that it turns out to be uh... this would have been one of the more unbelievable stories.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, Baker-Mazara himself broke his silence, issuing a statement denying the false age and character rumors that spread on social media. He's now 26, out of NCAA eligibility, and moving on to pro ball in Puerto Rico.

updates.headline: Baker-Mazara releases statement regarding dismissal
updates.summary: Baker-Mazara publicly addressed his March 1 dismissal from USC, denying the circulating false narratives about his character and age. He confirmed plans to play with the Gigantes de Carolina in Puerto Rico in April after exhausting his NCAA eligibility.

items.title: Eric Craw responds to religious mixup
items.pov_summary: The guys are pleased Eric Craw showed up in the YouTube comments to own the roast after misunderstanding their 'Mr. Jeezy' shoutout as a Jesus reference. They wanted a mea culpa but will take the acknowledgment. Still boggles their minds how a longtime listener could miss the Young Jeezy bit for this long.
items.status: ongoing bit, Eric has engaged, hosts amused
items.quote: How can you listen to this show seemingly... this whole time you're thinking we're thanking JC and not Mr. Jeezy.
items.updates_summary: No substantive news developments found since the episode aired. This was an inside-baseball show bit about a listener (Eric Craw) acknowledging a roast in the podcast's YouTube comments after misinterpreting a "Mr. Jeezy" reference — not a topic that typically generates outside news coverage.

items.title: Fast food CEO wars and the Big Arch
items.pov_summary: Big has Instagram blocked so he's flying blind, but TC dunks on the McDonald's CEO for his staged Big Arch video where he takes a tiny nibble — calls him a lizard person from Indian Hill. Burger King's guy gets more respect as a true fast food lifer. They want DT to review the Big Arch.
items.status: ongoing, hosts want to dig in further next week
items.quote: He just looks like a lizard person... We don't like Indian Hill.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski broke his silence on the viral "Big Arch" backlash in a Wall Street Journal interview, admitting his kid told him he'd gone viral "but not in a good way." Competitor piling-on continued, with Wendy's announcing a $100,000 "Chief Tasting Officer" gig as a jab at Kempczinski's performative nibble. McDonald's said early Big Arch sales are beating expectations despite (or because of) the meme-fest.

updates.headline: McDonald's CEO finally responds to Big Arch backlash
updates.summary: Kempczinski told the WSJ that one of his kids called and said, 'Dad you've gone viral, but not in a good way,' which is when he realized the tiny-bite video had blown up. He shrugged it off, saying it's 'great that people are talking about the Big Arch.'

updates.headline: Wendy's trolls McDonald's with $100K 'Chief Tasting Officer' gig
updates.summary: Amid the competitor pile-on sparked by Kempczinski's video, Wendy's posted a snarky video of its U.S. president Pete Suerken chomping a Baconator and announced a new Chief Tasting Officer position with a $100,000 salary — no experience required — an extended jab at the McDonald's CEO.

items.title: Geologist offers mantle rock samples
items.pov_summary: A listener geologist DMed them about the Soviet Cola Superdeep Borehole and offered to send mantle peridotite xenoliths from New Mexico. Randy is delighted and wants one for his shelf. Adds tabs and fuel to their ongoing 'drill to the core' fascination.
items.status: samples inbound, Wikipedia rabbit hole pending
items.quote: That's delightful. I would love one. I want to put it on my shelf right behind me.
items.updates_summary: No substantive news developments on this topic since the episode aired. The offer of New Mexico mantle peridotite xenoliths was a private listener interaction, and no public follow-up or related deep-Earth drilling news relevant to the hosts' bit has emerged in the March 19 – April 18, 2026 window.

items.title: Oscars recap — One Battle, Michael B Jordan, Sean Penn no-show
items.pov_summary: Their consensus pick One Battle After Another won Best Picture and PTA won Best Director, though Leo lost to Michael B. Jordan for Sinners. Randy digs Sean Penn just ghosting the ceremony entirely — calls it sick. Overall a strong Best Actor field and no Will Smith slap drama.
items.quote: I kind of dig when people get, you know, just like I don't have time for these award shows. They don't mean anything to me.
items.updates_summary: Minimal new developments since the episode aired on March 19. The main ongoing storyline has been Sean Penn's Ukraine trip in lieu of the ceremony, including a widely-shared "IronOscar" made from a damaged Ukrainian railcar, though most of that coverage hit just before the episode. No major post-episode surprises on One Battle After Another or Michael B. Jordan.

updates.headline: Sean Penn receives makeshift 'IronOscar' in Ukraine after skipping ceremony
updates.summary: Ukrainian Railways CEO Oleksandr Pertsovskyi presented Penn with a flat metallic trophy forged from the metal of a railcar damaged in a Russian missile attack, in recognition of Penn's presence in Ukraine instead of at the Dolby Theatre.

updates.headline: Variety details inscription on Penn's 'IronOscar' from Ukrainian Railways
updates.summary: Variety reported additional detail on the engraved inscription — noting the steel 'was not melted into a weapon' but 'forged into gratitude' — framing the gesture as a direct counterpoint to Penn's past comments about melting down his real Oscars for Ukrainian ammunition.

items.title: Miami Red Hawks make NCAA tournament
items.pov_summary: Miami went 31-0 in the regular season but got bullied on the glass in the MAC tourney, dropping them into the First Four to face SMU. Randy's mildly salty about the KenPom predictive metrics dragging them down, but he's hyped for a quasi-home game at UD Arena and thinks SMU is beatable if Miami shoots threes well.
items.status: play-in Wednesday vs SMU, Randy and TC eyeing Philly trip if they advance
items.quote: I'm a little disappointed Miami had to go the first four route... but let's go win Wednesday against SMU. Get some momentum.
items.updates_summary: Miami beat SMU 89-79 in the First Four at UD Arena behind Eian Elmer's 23 points and 16-of-41 three-point shooting — the program's first NCAA Tournament win since 1999. The magical season then ended two days later in Philadelphia, where No. 6 Tennessee routed the RedHawks 78-56 behind Ja'Kobi Gillespie's 29 points, though Peter Suder dropped 27 in the loss. Miami finished 32-2.

updates.headline: Miami (Ohio) beats SMU 89-79 in First Four for first NCAA Tournament win in 27 years
updates.summary: Eian Elmer scored 23 points (6-of-9 from three) and the RedHawks shot 16-of-41 from beyond the arc in a decidedly pro-Miami crowd at UD Arena. Brant Byers added 19 and Luke Skaljac 17, with Miami matching SMU on the glass 35-35 — directly answering the rebounding concerns from the MAC tourney loss.

updates.headline: Tennessee routs Miami (Ohio) 78-56 to end RedHawks' magical season
updates.summary: Ja'Kobi Gillespie poured in 29 points (6-of-11 from three) as the Vols led by as many as 26 in Philadelphia. Peter Suder's 27 points were the lone bright spot; Miami finished 32-2, with coach Travis Steele vowing the program would be back.

items.title: NCAA Tournament field feels chalky
items.pov_summary: Randy thinks true Cinderellas are getting squeezed out of modern college hoops but likes Akron, High Point, and the West Coast Conference sleepers like St. Mary's and Santa Clara. Duke and Michigan are his top picks; Arizona has questions. He's rooting for the small West Coast schools as the platonic ideal of college basketball.
items.quote: I think it's going to be tough for that like true Cinderella... That's like the ideal of college basketball for me.
items.updates_summary: The "chalky" fears largely played out: Michigan won the title (vindicating one of Randy's top picks) while Duke lost and Arizona reached the Final Four but got blown out. Randy's Cinderella sleepers mostly flopped — Akron, Santa Clara and St. Mary's all bowed out (Saint Mary's lost in the first round to Texas A&M), though High Point did pull off a 12-over-5 upset of Wisconsin in the opening round.

updates.headline: Michigan beats UConn 69-63 to win 2026 NCAA title
updates.summary: Michigan captured its first national championship since 1989 under Dusty May, beating UConn 69-63 behind 19 points from Elliot Cadeau, who was named Most Outstanding Player. Validates Randy's top pick from the episode.

updates.headline: Michigan routs Arizona 91-73 in Final Four
updates.summary: In a 1-vs-1 seed matchup of the top two KenPom teams, Michigan blew out Arizona by 18 to reach the title game, continuing the chalky trend Randy worried about. Arizona's questions came to the fore in the biggest moment.

updates.headline: High Point upsets Wisconsin as 12-seed; Saint Mary's bounced in first round
updates.summary: Randy's pick High Point delivered with a 12-over-5 upset of Wisconsin on a last-second bucket by Johnston. However, WCC sleeper Saint Mary's lost in the first round to Texas A&M, undone by 18 turnovers.

updates.headline: Tournament field confirmed as chalky: only six upsets total
updates.summary: The 2026 tournament produced just six upsets (games where the winner was seeded at least five lines worse), reinforcing the pre-tournament narrative that Cinderellas are being squeezed out.

items.title: Xavier coaching carousel and UC job
items.pov_summary: TC openly hopes Richard Pitino Jr. (aka Cry Keeper 2.0) stays at Xavier so they have to fire him and eat the buyout — he hates Xavier. Utah State's coach looks like the UC frontrunner. They also worry Butler could poach Travis Steele from Miami, which would be a gut punch.
items.quote: I hope Xavier has to fire him at the end of next year and pay the buyout and just set the program back because Xavier sucks.
items.updates_summary: The Cincinnati job went exactly where the hosts predicted: <cite index="18-5,18-6">Cincinnati hired Utah State's Jerrod Calhoun as its next head coach on a six-year deal starting at $3.7 million per year</cite>, with Calhoun — a Cincinnati alum who started under Bob Huggins — returning home. Meanwhile, TC's worst-case scenario is playing out at Xavier: Richard Pitino is staying put and is already working the transfer portal for the 2026-27 season. Butler did not poach Travis Steele from Miami — instead, <cite index="1-20,1-24,1-25">Thad Matta retired and Butler hired former Butler player Ronald Nored (who played under Brad Stevens on the back-to-back title game teams), with Matta remaining as special assistant to the president and AD</cite>.

updates.headline: Cincinnati hires Utah State's Jerrod Calhoun as head coach
updates.summary: Calhoun, a Cincinnati alum and Bob Huggins disciple, agreed to a six-year, $3.7M/year deal after leading Utah State to back-to-back NCAA Tournaments and the second round in 2026. Utah State will collect nearly $3.9M in buyout money.

updates.headline: Calhoun introduced: 'People need to fear us in the state of Ohio'
updates.summary: At his Fifth Third Arena introduction, Calhoun leaned into the Ohio rivalry angle and emphasized culture over pure NIL spending, noting UC has a reported ~$10M NIL budget heading into the portal.

updates.headline: Butler hires Ronald Nored after Thad Matta retirement
updates.summary: Instead of pursuing Travis Steele, Butler promoted/hired former Butler guard Ronald Nored (a UConn assistant and architect of the Huskies' title-winning offense) to replace the retiring Matta, who remains with the school as a special assistant.

updates.headline: Pitino staying at Xavier, hits portal hard
updates.summary: Richard Pitino is not on the move — he's already landed transfer portal pieces including Tru Washington (a guard who played for him at New Mexico) as Xavier retools its 2026-27 roster. No Xavier buyout appears to be coming, to TC's dismay.

items.title: World Baseball Classic controversy
items.pov_summary: The US beat the DR on an egregious low strike call and now faces Italy or Puerto Rico in the final. TC's skeptical of how fast-and-loose the WBC is with nationality eligibility — Aaron Nola starting for Italy? The US seems to be getting some favorable whistles.
items.quote: I think they're playing a little fast and loose with some of these nationality... If you've ever had a cup of espresso.
items.updates_summary: Venezuela upset Team USA 3-2 in the WBC final on March 17, capturing their first-ever WBC title and Venezuela's first major international baseball championship since 1945. The controversial Perdomo strike-three call that ended the US-DR semifinal has fueled growing calls to implement the Automated Ball-Strike (ABS) challenge system in the next WBC, with DR GM Nelson Cruz publicly endorsing the change.

updates.headline: Venezuela upsets Team USA 3-2 to win first WBC title
updates.summary: Venezuela won its first World Baseball Classic championship after Eugenio Suárez's ninth-inning RBI double broke a 2-2 tie that had been knotted by Bryce Harper's eighth-inning home run. Daniel Palencia closed it out, handing the US its second straight runner-up finish.

updates.headline: Maikel Garcia named WBC MVP as Venezuela celebrates historic win
updates.summary: Royals infielder Maikel Garcia was named tournament MVP. Manager Mark DeRosa lamented that Team USA "couldn't get the offense going the entire tournament," with Eduardo Rodriguez's changeup stifling the loaded American lineup in the final.

updates.headline: DR players, GM Nelson Cruz call for ABS challenge system after controversial semifinal exit
updates.summary: Geraldo Perdomo said he knew the umpire missed the call, and DR GM Nelson Cruz said he hopes ABS will be instituted in the next WBC so players can challenge such pitches — the 2026 tournament did not use ABS challenges.

updates.headline: DeRosa held back US relievers at MLB teams' request, raising buy-in questions
updates.summary: Reports after the final noted that three MLB teams asked Venezuela's manager not to use their relievers on back-to-back nights, while DeRosa similarly held back high-leverage US arms — reigniting debate about MLB-team buy-in and pitcher usage rules.

updates.headline: Manfred floats midseason WBC as tournament continues to grow
updates.summary: Commissioner Rob Manfred said the continued success of the WBC could lead to a midseason format in the future — a response to ongoing complaints about pitch-count limits and spring-training timing.

items.title: Bam Adebayo drops 83 on the Wizards
items.pov_summary: Bam going for the second-highest scoring game in NBA history feels random and tacky to the guys — they thought Heat Culture was supposed to be bigger than stat-chasing. He was getting the foie gras treatment at the line. Kobe stans were understandably upset.
items.quote: I never thought I'd live in a world where Bam Adebayo went for 83 points in an NBA game... You can't spell I. There's no I in heat, but evidently there's a me.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, Adebayo and the Heat played the Wizards again on April 4, 2026, where Washington triple-teamed him on his first touch and held him to 14 points in a 152-136 Heat win. Adebayo has pushed back at the "unethical" criticism — blaming Wizards coach Brian Keefe's scheme — and said the 83-point game has permanently changed how the league views him offensively. The Heat also clinched a playoff berth on April 18 via the play-in as the 10-seed.

updates.headline: Adebayo says 83-point game changed how NBA views him offensively
updates.summary: In interviews with NBC News and others, Adebayo said teams now have to guard him differently because he proved he can hit 7 threes and draw free throws. He also said he wouldn't trade the 83 for a Defensive Player of the Year award.

updates.headline: Wizards triple-team Adebayo on first touch in rematch, hold him to 14 in 152-136 Heat win
updates.summary: In the first meeting since the 83-point game, Washington sent a triple-team at Adebayo immediately. He finished with 14 points, 9 rebounds and 7 assists as the Heat cruised.

updates.headline: Jalen Rose credits A'ja Wilson's courtside presence for fueling the 83-point night
updates.summary: On The Dan Patrick Show, Jalen Rose argued Adebayo wouldn't have scored 83 without girlfriend A'ja Wilson watching live for the first time. Adebayo himself said he felt 'inspired' by her presence that night.

updates.headline: Heat clinch playoff spot as 10-seed via play-in win over Hawks
updates.summary: Adebayo posted 17 points, 11 rebounds, 5 assists, 2 steals and 5 blocks in a 123-114 OT win over Atlanta, making Miami the first 10-seed ever to clinch a playoff berth.

items.title: Cal State Bakersfield pimping scandal
items.pov_summary: An assistant basketball coach at Cal State Bakersfield was running a human trafficking operation across four western states, taking down the head coach and AD in the process. The guys find it bizarre and very on-brand for Bakersfield. Not many pimps on college basketball staffs.
items.quote: You don't get many pimps on college basketball staffs.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, a California judge ruled there's sufficient evidence to send former Cal State Bakersfield assistant Kevin Mays to trial on pimping, firearms, drug and child pornography charges. A May 15 readiness hearing has been set with a pending May 26 trial date.

updates.headline: Ex-Cal State Bakersfield assistant Kevin Mays ordered to stand trial in pimping case
updates.summary: A California judge ruled there is sufficient evidence to prosecute Mays on felony and misdemeanor charges including pimping, automatic firearms, drugs and child pornography. A police forensic examiner testified that 28 videos found on Mays' phone depicted sexual conduct with a minor, and police detailed a Sacramento sting operation that led to the alleged victim.

updates.headline: May 15 readiness hearing set for ex-CSUB assistant coach accused of trafficking
updates.summary: Kern County court records show Mays is scheduled for a May 15 readiness hearing with a pending trial date of May 26. Mays has pleaded not guilty to all 11 charges and remains held without bail.

items.title: F1 new engine rules and Max's complaints
items.pov_summary: Melbourne had better passing but the battery-harvesting stuff felt tacky and amateur-ish to TC. Max Verstappen is unhappy because anything that erodes his edge makes him unhappy; meanwhile Toto and Mercedes seem to have cracked the code early. Bahrain and Saudi Arabia races got cancelled due to Gulf turmoil.
items.quote: The battery stuff feels a little little tacky, little amateur-ish of like having to harvest more energy from the battery into turns.
items.updates_summary: Since the episode aired, F1 has begun formal discussions about tweaking the controversial 2026 rules during the five-week break caused by the cancelled Bahrain and Saudi Arabian GPs. A Japanese GP at Suzuka (won by Kimi Antonelli) saw a scary Bearman crash that intensified driver concerns, with GPDA director Carlos Sainz publicly criticizing the FIA. Three team-chief meetings have been scheduled, culminating April 20, with likely tweaks focused on qualifying "super-clipping" and safety rather than wholesale changes; CEO Domenicali has publicly pushed back on the "artificial" racing criticism.

updates.headline: F1 begins discussions on rule changes for rest of 2026 season
updates.summary: F1 has started a series of three meetings about possible tweaks to the 2026 regulations, with a key April 20 meeting between team chiefs and Domenicali to decide on any changes before Miami. Changes are expected to be limited to qualifying 'super-clipping' fixes and safety adjustments rather than wholesale reform.

updates.headline: Domenicali hits back at critics of 2026 rules
updates.summary: F1 CEO Stefano Domenicali rejected the 'artificial overtaking' criticism, comparing energy management to 1980s turbo-era fuel management. He spoke after complaints about 'Mario Kart' style racing prompted the April meetings between F1, FIA and teams.

updates.headline: Bearman crash at Suzuka prompts Sainz safety rebuke
updates.summary: Oliver Bearman spun and crashed at the Japanese GP while avoiding Colapinto, suffering a minor knee injury. GPDA director Carlos Sainz publicly criticized F1 and the FIA for ignoring driver warnings about dangerous closing-speed differentials caused by the new energy deployment rules.

updates.headline: Reports: Verstappen considering retirement at end of 2026
updates.summary: Reports emerged that Verstappen is considering walking away from F1 at the end of the season due to his dislike of the new regulations, though he has hinted he is not actively looking for a reason to leave. His current Red Bull contract runs through 2028.

updates.headline: F1 confirms 22-race 2026 calendar with no replacement for cancelled Gulf races
updates.summary: After cancelling Bahrain (April 12) and Saudi Arabia (April 19) due to the Iran war, F1 confirmed there will be no replacement races, creating a five-week gap until Miami on May 3. Imola, Portimao and a Suzuka double-header were all considered but ruled out due to logistics and ticketing timelines.

items.title: Old Firm derby pitch invasion in Glasgow
items.pov_summary: Celtic fans stormed the pitch after beating Rangers. Big (a Celtic guy) and TC (Rangers) reach across the aisle to condemn it. Can't have pitch invasions.
items.quote: Can't have it. Can't have it.
items.updates_summary: No substantive new developments found between the episode airing (March 19, 2026) and today (April 18, 2026). Coverage found was almost entirely from the immediate aftermath of the March 8 incident — police confirming arrests, an SFA investigation being launched, and Police Scotland signaling that large away allocations for Old Firm matches would not be supported going forward. No confirmed sanctions, charging outcomes, or follow-up filings within the target window surfaced in searches.

items.title: NFL free agency — Crosby saga and Bengals moves
items.pov_summary: Maxx Crosby failed the Ravens physical and they backed out, which is karmically appropriate for Baltimore per Trey Hendrickson. The Bengals finally got groceries, signing safety Brian Cook (a real tackler, meeting Big's Al Golden DB standard) and edge rusher Boyet Maf. Still need a linebacker.
items.status: free agency active, Bengals not done
items.quote: Your defensive backs tackle is a window to the soul of your of your team.
items.updates_summary: The Crosby/Ravens fallout has continued with Ravens HC Jesse Minter publicly addressing the failed trade, and analysts noting the suspicious timing of Baltimore pivoting to Trey Hendrickson within hours. On the Bengals side, Cincinnati kept adding to the defensive backfield after the episode aired, signing safety Kyle Dugger and CB Ja'Sir Taylor on April 2, and re-signing QB Joe Flacco on March 25 — though a linebacker addition has still not materialized.

updates.headline: Bengals sign S Kyle Dugger and CB Ja'Sir Taylor to one-year deals
updates.summary: Cincinnati added more secondary help after the episode aired, bringing in former Patriots safety Kyle Dugger and CB Ja'Sir Taylor on April 2 one-year contracts, further fleshing out the DB room beyond the Bryan Cook signing.

updates.headline: Bengals re-sign Joe Flacco for 2026 as Burrow's backup
updates.summary: Cincinnati re-signed Joe Flacco to a one-year contract for his 19th NFL season, locking in the veteran backup behind Joe Burrow.

updates.headline: Ravens HC Jesse Minter addresses failed Crosby trade
updates.summary: Minter pushed back on the 'cold feet' narrative on NFL Live, saying the Ravens were genuinely excited about adding Crosby and were disappointed the physical issue derailed the deal before they pivoted to Hendrickson.

updates.headline: New reporting details Ravens' concerns over Crosby's knee longevity
updates.summary: Follow-up reporting indicates Baltimore's concern centered on a degenerative issue in Crosby's knee and his durability over multiple seasons, rather than just the meniscus repair timeline — reinforcing the 'cold feet' reading given the nearly identical $112M Hendrickson deal signed hours later.

items.title: Joe Burrow and Jessica Alba rumors
items.pov_summary: Turns out they aren't dating — Alba's seeing some actor — but Big loves Burrow gossip regardless. Just a couple of bros chopping it up. Burrow is a one-of-one fascinating individual.
items.quote: I love Burrow gossip and stuff. He's just fascinating individual.
items.updates_summary: No meaningful new developments on the Burrow/Alba rumor since the episode. The story had already been debunked before the show aired, and nothing has resurfaced in the month since. Alba remains with Danny Ramirez, and Burrow's post-episode news cycle has shifted to NFL trade speculation rather than personal life.

items.title: Tua to the Falcons rumors
items.pov_summary: Big hates it — if you think Tua is the answer you're asking the wrong question. Tough stretch for Michael Penix with Tua, Jan Dodson, and James Pierce getting charged all piling up for the Falcons.
items.quote: If you think two is the answer, I think you're asking the wrong question. Like I just I think we've seen enough.
items.updates_summary: Since the March 19 episode, the Tua-to-Atlanta signing has moved from rumor to reality with new commentary from the Falcons' brass. Head coach Kevin Stefanski and GM Ian Cunningham have publicly confirmed there will be an open QB competition between Tua Tagovailoa and Michael Penix Jr., while offering no firm timeline for Penix's return from his torn ACL. Tua has publicly embraced the competition, while analysts note the Falcons are positioned to return to the QB market in the stronger 2027 draft class if neither player seizes the job.

updates.headline: Stefanski on signing Tua: 'There's something to be said when you're fired... you want to prove people wrong'
updates.summary: Stefanski framed Tua as a motivated fit coming off being cut, said Atlanta is offering him a chance to compete for a starting job, and declined to give a timeline for Penix's ACL recovery beyond saying he's rehabbing daily.

updates.headline: Falcons confirm open QB competition; no starter named during offseason program
updates.summary: Stefanski and Cunningham publicly confirmed a legitimate competition between Penix and Tua, with Stefanski emphasizing daily evaluation and playbook install — i.e., Penix is not being anointed as the 2026 starter.

updates.headline: Tua embraces Falcons QB battle with Penix
updates.summary: Tagovailoa said he's focused on making the most of his one-year deal and welcomes the competition, while NFL.com compared his potential arc to Sam Darnold's reset under Stefanski.

updates.headline: Analysis: 2027 draft is Atlanta's safety net if Tua/Penix don't pan out
updates.summary: SI's Falcons site notes that if neither Tua nor Penix proves to be a franchise answer in 2026, the Falcons will enter a much deeper 2027 QB draft class (Moore, Manning, Carr, Sayin, etc.) with cap space and likely a high pick.

items.title: Adam Silver shuts down Hawks' Magic City night
items.pov_summary: The Hawks planned a Magic City Monday celebrating Atlanta culture and Silver killed it. Big thinks Silver should worry about the actual problems — tanking, the Kawhi off-books money, gambling probes — rather than policing fun. The luster has come off the commissioner.
items.status: event cancelled, broader Silver criticism mounting
items.quote: Adam Silver, you got to get your house in order, my man. The tanking is out of control in the NBA.
items.updates_summary: Little new news on the Magic City cancellation itself since the episode — the story has mostly faded. The most notable post-episode item is a Sportico analysis piece examining the limits of league brand control, which also revealed that Hawks owner Jami Gertz wore Magic City apparel at the March 16 game in apparent protest of Silver's intervention.

updates.headline: The NBA's Magic City Dance Shows the Limits of Brand Control
updates.summary: Sportico analysis argues the NBA's intervention reinforced traditional league governance but exposed how cultural reality now lives outside league control. The piece also noted that Hawks principal owner Jami Gertz wore Magic City apparel at the game after the league canceled the promotion — a quiet protest against Silver's decision.

items.title: 600 tubs of French onion dip mystery
items.pov_summary: Philly restaurants mysteriously received 600-pound shipments of French onion dip each, creating refrigeration chaos. TC is charmed by the story and uses it to segue into a Dean's French onion dip tangent tied to the Phil Mickelson Dean Foods insider trading case.
items.quote: Every time anybody brings up the Mickelson insider trading, I get excited and I get hungry for Dean's French onion dip.
items.updates_summary: No meaningful developments have surfaced since the episode aired on March 19, 2026. Coverage of the Philly "Dip Gate 2026" mystery peaked March 7-14, and neither HP Hood/Heluva Good! nor investigators have publicly identified the third-party party responsible for the 600-tub shipments in the weeks since.

items.title: Ticketmaster/Live Nation DOJ settlement stinks
items.pov_summary: Matt Stoller went off on what looks like pay-for-play between Live Nation lobbying and DOJ officials to settle the antitrust case cheaply. States including Texas aren't settling and will likely embarrass the feds. TC thought we were supposed to be for free markets, not oligopoly power.
items.quote: It's kind of like where the populist message has butted up against the money.
items.updates_summary: The hosts' take was largely vindicated. On April 15, 2026, a Manhattan federal jury found Live Nation and Ticketmaster liable for illegally monopolizing the live events market after 33 states and DC rejected the DOJ settlement and pressed on to trial. The verdict sets up a remedies phase where a judge could order structural relief — potentially including a breakup — and lawmakers including Klobuchar, Warren, and Blumenthal have urged the judge to scrutinize the DOJ's "slap on the wrist" settlement.

updates.headline: Jury finds Live Nation and Ticketmaster operated an illegal monopoly
updates.summary: A Manhattan federal jury ruled that Live Nation/Ticketmaster illegally monopolized the live events industry and overcharged consumers. The verdict came after 33 states and DC refused to join the DOJ settlement and took the case to trial themselves, with the jury finding overcharges of $1.72 per ticket in 22 states.

updates.headline: States win antitrust case after DOJ bailed
updates.summary: Axios notes the Trump administration settled over the objections of some of DOJ's own antitrust officials, and that the case was considered one of DOJ's strongest in years. Gail Slater, the former antitrust division head pushed out before trial, publicly congratulated the state AG coalition.

updates.headline: Judge could now reject the DOJ settlement in light of states' verdict
updates.summary: Notre Dame law professor Roger Alford told CBS a judge could eventually reject the DOJ settlement given the states' win — a direct hit to the deal Stoller criticized. Remedies could include forced divestitures, refunds, or a full breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster.

updates.headline: Senators Klobuchar, Warren, Blumenthal urge judge to scrutinize DOJ settlement
updates.summary: Democratic senators sent a letter to the judge calling the DOJ settlement insufficient and asking him to closely scrutinize it — echoing Stoller's critique that the deal was a giveaway. The states are expected to push for structural remedies well beyond what DOJ negotiated.

updates.headline: CNN details 'secret' DOJ settlement reached weeks after antitrust chief ousted
